clean_body: Today is about honoring the countless labor rights activists before us. It’s also about continuing the struggle for worker political power. Happy Labor Day, team. Today, we recognize the ongoing struggle for labor rights in our country, and the countless union organizers and activists who fought tirelessly for them—often in the face of violent resistance. Growing up poor and watching my mom and grandparents work around the clock just to scrape by, the Labor Movement is personal to me. I will always stand arm in arm with every worker, union, and labor organizer to advance higher wages, better working conditions, and an economy that benefits the many over the few. Today, I stand in solidarity with the Labor Movement as we continue building political power for working people to ensure everyone has the right to live a life with purpose and dignity. Despite sweeping attacks on unions and organized labor by an anti-worker Republican Party, union support has reached a historic high. Over 71% of Americans, Republicans and Independents included, are in support of unions, while nine in 10 Democrats and people under 30 view unions favorably. Organizing for rights in the workplace is happening right now, and it’s not just happening in factories or construction sites. Workers in law offices, fast food restaurants, retail stores, and local coffee shops are all using their power to demand dignity and respect at every level in the workplace. Unions and the Labor Movement are our frontline defense against low wages, unsafe working conditions, rising inequality, discrimination, and oppression in all its forms. Nothing is a more powerful force for progress than workers linking arms in solidarity. I owe my campaign to the countless labor organizers and activists before me, and vow to continue the fight for labor and worker dignity now and always. Wishing you a happy and safe Labor Day. In solidarity, Mondaire = = | PAID FOR BY MONDAIRE FOR CONGRESS P.O. BOX #873, SLEEPY HOLLOW, NY 10591

name: Congressman Raja Krishnamoorthi
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Raja for Congress
body:
Dear Peter,

Labor Day is not just a long weekend or the unofficial end of summer. On this day, we celebrate the enduring achievements of American workers.

Labor Day originated in the labor rights movement of the late 19th century. At that time, Americans were working long hours just to make ends meet. Workers faced the risk of bodily injury as they toiled away in unsafe working conditions. Child labor was at its peak. The labor movement helped end child labor and successfully advocated for safe and sustainable working conditions for all Americans. We cherish these protections today.

In June 1894, President Grover Cleveland signed a bill into law making Labor Day a legal holiday to recognize the tremendous efforts of American workers in helping to build what is now the world’s greatest economy.

We owe a deep debt of gratitude to our workers and others who power our country forward each and every day. So as you celebrate this year, let's remember those who sacrificed so much to earn us the way of life we enjoy, and thank the workers who keep our economy running today.

Thank you again for everything you do to keep America working for everyone.

Happy Labor Day!

Warm Regards,

Raja

Paid for by Friends of Raja for Congress

Raja for Congress
PO Box 681202
Schaumburg, IL 60168
United States

name: Wes Moore
subject: For the union makes us strong
body:
I have seen the power of unions to provide a path to and protect the middle class. And it's why I'm not shocked to see unions on the rise in this country. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69174596/425330300/-2109446099?refcode=EM_FL_WES_20230902_fr_laborday_cand_mf_180da_xx_hdr&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOGVkYmVhZi0yODRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kvQSIY94nDKrgE5mPKdITqqr3t4qNRSMCPHKU-Tn-SM=)
---
Teammates, I was born in a union-strong household. When I was just 3 years old and my father died unexpectedly, it was his union that paid for his funeral. When my sister when she got sick, her union supported her. | [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69174597/425330301/-2109446099?refcode=EM_FL_WES_20230902_fr_laborday_cand_mf_180da_xx_img&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDQ1NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwOGVkYmVhZi0yODRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kvQSIY94nDKrgE5mPKdITqqr3t4qNRSMCPHKU-Tn-SM=)
---
So when I became CEO of one of the nation's largest anti-poverty organizations, it was unequivocal that we'd work with organized labor. We provided millions to a local union to improve training for home health workers, invested over $10 million annually in job training, and provided emergency relief funding for child care and health care workers during the pandemic.
**I have seen the power of unions to provide a path to and protect the middle class. And it's why I'm not shocked to see unions on the rise in this country.**
Stagnant wages, cruel working conditions, and rising inequality are boiling over into what many have dubbed #HotLaborSummer. **Workers are demanding better, and they are doing it with the overwhelming power that only comes from a union.**
Unions make our economy strong. Unions make Maryland strong. _The unions make us strong._
And that is how we make a Maryland where no one is left behind.
Solidarity forever,
Wes

| Wes Moore is a combat veteran, Rhodes Scholar, former small business owner and leader of one of the nation’s largest poverty-fighting organizations — and now, your governor! Thanks to supporters like you, we made history by electing Wes the 63rd Governor of Maryland. Support Wes as he works to move our state forward and leave no one behind.

Hi Peter,
This Labor Day we want to celebrate and acknowledge all of the hard working men and women who made key contributions to making America strong. We celebrate the social and economic achievements of American workers all across this country.
https://www.mobilize.us/amandaedwardsforcongress/event/575642/ [https://www.mobilize.us/amandaedwardsforcongress/event/575642/]
This also provides an opportunity to pause and reflect on how far we have come, while we double down on our efforts to make sure that we work to build an economy where every worker has the opportunity to thrive, not just survive.
Onward,
Amanda K. Edwards
Amanda K. Edwards has the experience and innovative vision to move Houston forward. To learn more, visit our website. clean_body: [] Hi Peter, This Labor Day we want to celebrate and acknowledge all of the hard working men and women who made key contributions to making America strong. We celebrate the social and economic achievements of American workers all across this country. [] This also provides an opportunity to pause and reflect on how far we have come, while we double down on our efforts to make sure that we work to build an economy where every worker has the opportunity to thrive, not just survive. name: Defend the Constitution
subject: Your input is needed
body:
The Constitution established a limited government in which the people ruled through their elected officials.

| | |
---
| | This sponsored message from Defend the Constitution is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=b03bdd21c9754628b90d6f531ad80b8c&_e=lJ1VMDMbm5oBbwgAX9XAnPmYGV9c-KNwi15HP2pJMpviIVLHpImkueJFHsRIPsQzba-U9VRNYGHqBJu9mhFhVvCHzKVW2qm4eXxcyYsJiKotIws6mGHTZvgkn2I89BSDVsV2AFDyNkOoD-jud7d_CeKhYv2eKOmBZvLSo37nyRS9vAgB-C0dqajcsfuPedVtwYO5c9Yd-6M0dWnbHaaA_PIkcnzKODIzK0lompKh7OoaVuCVWZ8vYfQtXsw-XboWDocyjczV9aSC1e53axgru8qAFteU0Xmk89DTwP3H2BxxVFWJGOHpo3yRng0LmccMwbkpWae0qbqb5zgjkIVVLDqxvQYvIqoiKboNstC_qpsDujevdbaw8j74vmHgKi5ykHU76O8Nw4EtkeM0jNjNWz637RFVRZZm8NODqa0vBVA%3D)
---
| | | Dear Fellow American, Hillsdale College has selected you to participate in its [**National Referendum on the Administrative State**](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=b03bdd21c9754628b90d6f531ad80b8c&_e=lJ1VMDMbm5oBbwgAX9XAnPmYGV9c-KNwi15HP2pJMpviIVLHpImkueJFHsRIPsQzba-U9VRNYGHqBJu9mhFhVvCHzKVW2qm4eXxcyYsJiKotIws6mGHTZvgkn2I89BSDVsV2AFDyNkOoD-jud7d_CeKhYv2eKOmBZvLSo37nyRS9vAgB-C0dqajcsfuPedVtwYO5c9Yd-6M0dWnbHaaA_PIkcnzKODIzK0lompKh7OoaVuCVWZ8vYfQtXsw-XboWDocyjczV9aSC1e53axgru6wkB7B-dQbd-C7DbMFcQ6xlFjEHScPeJL3yodON6HpVX8bC9LATyZmEWdQF6KzZjS2b8JegaXU4wCtU3NilIolvfB_qgpmtShQuZKMw6t9R16bFGOibJtTn00h8b0oP3rRT_VKsnEL1Z4fVFYPXTm0%3D).
## Do you agree that there is a double standard for members of the administrative state--that the ruling elite is not required to follow the same rules as other Americans?
[YES](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=b03bdd21c9754628b90d6f531ad80b8c&_e=lJ1VMDMbm5oBbwgAX9XAnPmYGV9c-KNwi15HP2pJMpviIVLHpImkueJFHsRIPsQzba-U9VRNYGHqBJu9mhFhVvCHzKVW2qm4eXxcyYsJiKotIws6mGHTZvgkn2I89BSDVsV2AFDyNkOoD-jud7d_CeKhYv2eKOmBZvLSo37nyRS9vAgB-C0dqajcsfuPedVtwYO5c9Yd-6M0dWnbHaaA_PIkcnzKODIzK0lompKh7OoaVuCVWZ8vYfQtXsw-XboWDocyjczV9aSC1e53axgru4ZLkqMTS2IUW1ClSlfi_cMCi87q1Bp6phFam6rwiDp3eMSFnLUPTtLqHxifBz03TV7J7WuVUk_VSsuoX5Bu0mUE42BPeEuMSbIx8rd8nIpr2kFiVZuT0o1JZdn8vqZsdWMJ8QstXaVeIwAY2johY4c%3D) [NO](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=b03bdd21c9754628b90d6f531ad80b8c&_e=lJ1VMDMbm5oBbwgAX9XAnPmYGV9c-KNwi15HP2pJMpviIVLHpImkueJFHsRIPsQzba-U9VRNYGHqBJu9mhFhVvCHzKVW2qm4eXxcyYsJiKotIws6mGHTZvgkn2I89BSDVsV2AFDyNkOoD-jud7d_CeKhYv2eKOmBZvLSo37nyRS9vAgB-C0dqajcsfuPedVtwYO5c9Yd-6M0dWnbHaaA_PIkcnzKODIzK0lompKh7OoaVuCVWZ8vYfQtXsw-XboWDocyjczV9aSC1e53axgru_taTfUJBB24TxqNH-WOHTbYtHWd-l3BYIk8PRxqCA2OonrCYzYXBVCdBOR-RDi1s5n8uq8h5VTLtpyLyD6J8_q77Cpa6Plm72wP6pmYKl0sMq5LpFbSjRXh-b0Q4b4Aa7DhMo5xYMuuOOGjY1HjTYM%3D) Responding will only take a few minutes. Your input as an informed patriot is needed right away...because **our country is facing a constitutional crisis**.

It's a crisis that involves a fundamental political question: **who rules whom?**

The Constitution established a limited government in which the **people** ruled through their elected officials. Under this form of government, America became the freest and most prosperous nation in history.

Today, however, our constitutional system is one that increasingly exists in name only. **The Constitution of "We the People" is being replaced by an elite, out-of-touch, and unaccountable administrative state that rules without the consent of American citizens.** This means we live in an era of growing bureaucratic despotism, manifested in an administrative state that bypasses the Constitution, rejects the separation of powers, and replaces the rule of law with regulations.

**But we can 't just TALK about the problem. We also have to TAKE ACTION--to DO SOMETHING about it, while there's still time to save our country. **

That is why I'm writing to you today.

Hillsdale College is committed to returning to the Constitution of "We the People"--before it's too late.

To do that, I need your input. What are your opinions on this topic--knowing that we can propose SOLUTIONS that will restore liberty?

**Please let me know your thoughts today by answering the National Referendum on the Administrative State using this secure link:**

**[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/administrative-state-survey/](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=b03bdd21c9754628b90d6f531ad80b8c&_e=lJ1VMDMbm5oBbwgAX9XAnPmYGV9c-KNwi15HP2pJMpviIVLHpImkueJFHsRIPsQzba-U9VRNYGHqBJu9mhFhVvCHzKVW2qm4eXxcyYsJiKotIws6mGHTZvgkn2I89BSDVsV2AFDyNkOoD-jud7d_CeKhYv2eKOmBZvLSo37nyRS9vAgB-C0dqajcsfuPedVtwYO5c9Yd-6M0dWnbHaaA_PIkcnzKODIzK0lompKh7OoaVuCVWZ8vYfQtXsw-XboWDocyjczV9aSC1e53axgru5-z3t88KbHd7UpEsJ6dfo4weTDF9H3qNBO2M0XGaP_xGYLk4U5l-cz6fWUJCbbxC9_rK_DxXLHtteoStnOdLhdYZWVgTOzwMce_iW1i9cxCI0oGwfAtjT83Y1X4pITIRAYeXssFLHXUqa21AQNTIJo%3D)**

Thank you for being on the side of the Constitution and liberty!

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
---
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

Defend the Constitution
---
_625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314_

name: Andy Beshear
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
body:
Peter,

This Labor Day, I want to pay tribute to the labor movement and honor the hardworking people of our Commonwealth.

Not so long ago, many working Americans did not enjoy the rights and freedoms we have today. The 40-hour work week, minimum wage, child labor laws, and many other policies are in place because of the advocacy and dedication of unions and the labor movement.

I'm grateful for how far we've come, thanks to the efforts of the workers who came before us. I also recognize how much work there is still to be done.

Today – and every day – I stand in solidarity with the labor movement, unions, and every Kentuckian who wakes up each morning, goes to work, and puts food on the table. I will always stand with Kentucky workers and fight for their rights.

Happy Labor Day!

Andy

PAID FOR BY ANDY BESHEAR FOR GOVERNOR
Andy Beshear for Governor
PO Box 4278
Louisville, KY 40204
United States

name: Emily Busch
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EMILY BUSCH FOR CONGRESS
body:
Happy Labor Day, Peter! On this day may we have one last cookout, wear our whites, take one more trip up north, and enjoy much needed family time. But let's not forget that today we celebrate the true heartbeat of our state and nation: its workers. As we take a moment to reflect on the monumental contributions of Michigan's workforce, I am reminded of the values instilled in me by my own family and upbringing. | | | [](http://links.emilyformichigan.com/u/click?_t=e92c5e294c1440b880a7ffdf88c24a48&_m=01ede6163713453eb460bda88d0362ca&_e=KYJ191RFptdxnSfgCRPA3MQdr76oxDb7LE08EaD9luylf_0JxRrabh200LOgpOc98rsoMpXfnyS7l1-Ci-K-pwY_yA5xgVT1C8fyaAEBbUtawWfaEIjf1rIJeWMFC-sw11rA0QB5cPI9PIChtMNzmn6ytJKVzbt01MCxiKhyLiiwkUq2xeXwz84Wgm8ojGsCkAY2cznj3aP2R8wVVMuiNA%3D%3D) Happy Labor Day, Peter! On this day may we have one last cookout, wear our whites, take one more trip up north, and enjoy much needed family time. But let's not forget that today we celebrate the true heartbeat of our state and nation: its workers. As we take a moment to reflect on the monumental contributions of Michigan's workforce, I am reminded of the values instilled in me by my own family and upbringing. Growing up in a tight-knit, middle-class Midwestern family, I learned the power of hard work, perseverance, and community. My mother, a dedicated nurse, taught me the importance of caring for others. Equally influential was my father, a manufacturing representative, who showed me the intricacies of the industrial landscape. Through his experiences, I witnessed firsthand the dedication of our nation's workers to producing quality goods and driving innovation. As we celebrate Labor Day, let's honor the legacy of the many industries that put Michigan on the map--like the auto industry--and remember the sacrifices so many made to get us to where we are now. These unions have been the driving force behind workers' rights, fair wages, and safer workplaces. Michigan's workers are the backbone of our state's success, and it's time their contributions are fully recognized and supported. Happy Labor Day to all! – Emily PAID FOR BY EMILY BUSCH FOR CONGRESS.

---

Emily Busch,
clean_body: Happy Labor Day, Peter! On this day may we have one last cookout, wear our whites, take one more trip up north, and enjoy much needed family time. But let's not forget that today we celebrate the true heartbeat of our state and nation: its workers. As we take a moment to reflect on the monumental contributions of Michigan's workforce, I am reminded of the values instilled in me by my own family and upbringing. | | | Happy Labor Day, Peter! On this day may we have one last cookout, wear our whites, take one more trip up north, and enjoy much needed family time. But let's not forget that today we celebrate the true heartbeat of our state and nation: its workers. As we take a moment to reflect on the monumental contributions of Michigan's workforce, I am reminded of the values instilled in me by my own family and upbringing. Growing up in a tight-knit, middle-class Midwestern family, I learned the power of hard work, perseverance, and community. My mother, a dedicated nurse, taught me the importance of caring for others. Equally influential was my father, a manufacturing representative, who showed me the intricacies of the industrial landscape. Through his experiences, I witnessed firsthand the dedication of our nation's workers to producing quality goods and driving innovation. As we celebrate Labor Day, let's honor the legacy of the many industries that put Michigan on the map--like the auto industry--and remember the sacrifices so many made to get us to where we are now. These unions have been the driving force behind workers' rights, fair wages, and safer workplaces. Michigan's workers are the backbone of our state's success, and it's time their contributions are fully recognized and supported. Happy Labor Day to all! – Emily PAID FOR BY EMILY BUSCH FOR CONGRESS.

name: Ohio Democratic Party
subject: Honoring the American Labor Movement
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69176448/425337930/-438463598?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84OTI0NyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhYTJjODM1Ny0yYjRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=LY_cwfO8NnfdC6jCmKm_v98VoKCB8KMXYFipC5kbW0k=) | Happy Labor Day, team!

As you enjoy this well-deserved long weekend, we hope you'll remember that Labor Day is about more than enjoying the unofficial end to summer or an extra day off for some workers.

It's also about honoring the labor movement that brought us safer working conditions, fair pay, a 40-hour workweek, child labor laws, vital social safety nets like Social Security and Medicare, weekends and holidays off, and so much more. Before the labor movement, workers across the country were working for little pay in unsafe and unsanitary workplaces. But thanks to the efforts of American laborers, many of us are able to enjoy the benefits of their hard work. **But team, the fight to protect and expand workers' rights is far from over.**

Today – whether you're working or enjoying the day off – we hope you'll join us in taking a moment to remember why we celebrate Labor Day and why we must continue to fight to protect workers' rights in Ohio and across our country.

Thank you,

Ohio Democratic Party | **A reproductive rights initiative is on the Ohio ballot this November. name: Angela Alsobrooks
subject: What Labor Day means to me
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=415%2E52803%2EG3OD7T)

| | Peter, workers and their families are the backbone of the can-do American identity, and today we recognize and celebrate the achievements of the labor movement. One of my earliest memories is of my father taking me and my sister Kim to work with him on his delivery route for the Washington Post. We would wake up early in the morning and ride with him on his truck as he delivered the paper. He would tell us, _“I jump up and down on this dirty truck so you never have to.”_ He taught us that a job is much more than a paycheck, and that there is dignity in all work. His story is what drives me to fight for working people all across our state. Labor Day serves as a testament to what workers and unions have accomplished to improve working conditions, collectively bargain, and strive for higher wages and workplace safety. It’s also an acknowledgement that we’ve still got a long way to go to ensure workers can provide for their families and retire when they’re ready. In the Senate, I will be a strong supporter of passing the PRO Act to ensure that we can strengthen workers’ rights through collective bargaining. All work has dignity, and it’s important that we empower workers to join and form unions and that we have strong laws that protect those basic fundamental rights. Thank you to all the laborers who have fought on the picket lines and in the workplace, in courtrooms and in the streets, pushing our country forward. Thank you, Angela Alsobrooks | | | [Donate](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/5436?t=9&amount=25&akid=415%2E52803%2EG3OD7T)

clean_body: | | | Peter, workers and their families are the backbone of the can-do American identity, and today we recognize and celebrate the achievements of the labor movement. One of my earliest memories is of my father taking me and my sister Kim to work with him on his delivery route for the Washington Post. We would wake up early in the morning and ride with him on his truck as he delivered the paper. He would tell us, “I jump up and down on this dirty truck so you never have to.” He taught us that a job is much more than a paycheck, and that there is dignity in all work. His story is what drives me to fight for working people all across our state. Labor Day serves as a testament to what workers and unions have accomplished to improve working conditions, collectively bargain, and strive for higher wages and workplace safety. It’s also an acknowledgement that we’ve still got a long way to go to ensure workers can provide for their families and retire when they’re ready. In the Senate, I will be a strong supporter of passing the PRO Act to ensure that we can strengthen workers’ rights through collective bargaining. All work has dignity, and it’s important that we empower workers to join and form unions and that we have strong laws that protect those basic fundamental rights. Thank you to all the laborers who have fought on the picket lines and in the workplace, in courtrooms and in the streets, pushing our country forward. Thank you, Angela Alsobrooks | | | | | | | We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in touch with supporters like you. subject: On this Labor Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY]( "PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY")
body:
Remember what the economy was like before Bidenomics? | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/ne.gop/n41kepo7ku-12649164?e=25844e23a8)
---
| | [ ](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=e81cf46ddf&e=25844e23a8)
---
| | Peter,
---
| Earlier this summer Joe Biden declared that Bidenomics is working.

We do not think that that word means what Joe Biden thinks it means.
---
| [ ](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=69b52cc91e&e=25844e23a8)
---
| Joe Biden’s economic agenda is failing us.

The average middle-class household has lost over **$33,000** in real wealth over the past year. We feel this pain every time we gas up our cars, purchase necessary items like school supplies for our children, and see the price of groceries continuing to rise.

Meanwhile layoffs are on the rise and the Biden Admin spins employment numbers as a PR stunt. Savvy Americans know that _‘increased jobs’_ translates to many Americans juggling 2 or 3 jobs just to afford basic necessities of rent, food, utilities, etc.

_**We know the source of these woes.**_

An overwhelming **80%** of Americans believe the economy is in bad shape, a sentiment that has grown 17 points since Biden took office!

This Labor Day, we must be honest with ourselves on the state of our economy and the hardships it has imposed on We The People.

**It is crucial that we elect strong Platform Republicans to put our finances back on track and reignite the American Dream for all. Platform Republicans who truly believe in lower government spending and fiscal responsibility.**
---
| Respectfully,

Eric Underwood - Chairman
Republican Party of Nebraska

[**Join us in our mission to restore economic prosperity and secure a brighter future for our nation. Your contribution today of $9.40 will symbolize our unified determination to secure America’s economic future by defeating the DC uniparty.**](https://mcshanellc.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=28a0c2f460&e=25844e23a8)
---

name: Becca Balint
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont
body:
Organized labor and unions built America’s middle class.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69178826/425346956/966991095?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhOTU0ZmQ4NS0yZjRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QrNIkDhV6UJZz_M3C1rtsmzrvRaLuawNM1x7Mpa2IYI=&emci=afa48688-ef48-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=a954fd85-2f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=43402272) | Happy Labor Day, Peter! Please join me in taking a moment today to honor the workers who keep Vermont running day in and day out, and the labor movement that has shaped our great nation into what it is today. **Organized labor and unions built America’s middle class – and I know this firsthand.** When I was in graduate school, I joined the GEO UAW Local 2322, a unit of teaching assistants, associates, and project assistants at the University of Massachusetts at Amherst. Then, as a teacher, I was a member of the National Education Association, which gave us the collective power to negotiate for health insurance, retirement, paid sick leave, and fair pay. If it weren’t for the labor movement, many of the things we take for granted in our work life today would not exist. From the minimum wage to workplace safety requirements to reasonable hours, the labor movement drastically improved the quality of life for the working poor in this country. That’s why I have _always_ supported the work of labor organizers as they strive for a better system that works for _all_. As income inequality, corporate greed, and anti-labor Republicans continue to threaten the livelihoods of working families, **we must come together to strengthen the middle class and protect the right to organize – and that’s exactly what I’m fighting for in Congress. ** Happy Labor Day! – Becca
---

* * *

name: Team Emilia
subject: We’re standing with Ohio’s workers, today and everyday.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emilia Sykes for Congress
body:
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69179389/425348914/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNDQ2MWVjNS0zMDRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NvmII25wKcE0czWe1dtWXgAYYsuoQJ5WuhzcMbhezhw=&emci=f14a9454-2e4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=14461ec5-304b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=11703046) Peter – Happy Labor Day!

This Labor Day, all of us here on Team Emilia wanted to take a moment to celebrate workers and organized labor organizations across Ohio.

**Emilia is proud to stand with labor because she knows by investing in working families,_we build an economy that works for all of us._** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69179390/425348915/-1657784636?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NTEyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNDQ2MWVjNS0zMDRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=NvmII25wKcE0czWe1dtWXgAYYsuoQJ5WuhzcMbhezhw=&emci=f14a9454-2e4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=14461ec5-304b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=11703046) Workers will always have Emilia’s support in ensuring their right to organize, and she stands with them in the fight for better wages, good benefits, and safe working conditions. In Congress, she’s working alongside her colleagues to pass the _Protecting the Right to Organize (PRO) Act_ and fight for our middle class.

Wishing you a safe and happy Labor Day,

– Team Emilia

PS. We also wanted to take an opportunity to thank you for your support in August! Thanks to folks like you, our team was able to reach our monthly fundraising goal and keep our campaign on the path to victory. **This is a people-powered team, so none of it would be possible without you, Peter._Thank you!_**
---

name: Joe Biden
subject: Our workers are the heart and soul of our country
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
Today, we honor the dignity of the American worker. We remember that America wasn’t built by Wall Street. It was built by the middle class, and unions built the middle class. | | |
---
Happy Labor Day, Peter. Today, we honor the dignity of the American worker. We remember that America wasn’t built by Wall Street. It was built by the middle class, and unions built the middle class. Over the last few years, I’ve had the honor of meeting with workers around the country. I’ve visited longshore workers in California, firefighters in Colorado, transit workers in New Jersey, welders in Wisconsin, and teachers in Virginia, among many others. Whenever I meet members of America’s labor community, I am reminded of what unions have fought for for decades. The eight-hour day, the weekend, safety standards in the workplace, sick days -- those are victories won by labor. And when unions win, workers across the board win. Our communities win. Families win. Our country wins. **That’s why I’ve said from the start that I would be the most pro-worker and pro-union president in American history. I hope I’ve kept that promise.** When I took office, I put more money in the pockets of hardworking Americans with the _American Rescue Plan,_ offering families much-needed breathing room. Through the _Bipartisan Infrastructure Law,_ the _CHIPS and Science Act,_ and the _Inflation Reduction Act,_ Kamala and I have enacted a bold, long-term economic agenda that has helped create 13 million jobs, while making historic investments in our economy and in our country’s workers. This is just the beginning, Peter. I’m committed to doing everything I can to give workers more power and raise wages. That includes ensuring that good-paying jobs are accessible to everyone, and that _all_ workers have good wages, benefits, and treatment in the workplace. **That’s because the middle class -- and the unions that built it -- are our country’s heart and soul. I’m not done fighting to build an economy that works for working families. And I know you’re not, either.** Thank you for everything, and happy Labor Day. Joe
Joe Biden

name: Team Malamed
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ZAK FOR CONGRESS
body:
#
Friend,

Happy Labor Day!

Today is about “Out of Office” messages, catching some sun with loved ones, and maybe enjoying a hot dog (or two!)

But today's well-deserved day-off is also a reminder of our commitment to working people in our district and across the country.

**We support the rights of workers to form unions, to bargain for fair wages and safer work conditions, and to speak out in the face of injustice.**

And in a rapidly-changing work environment where technology presents new opportunities and challenges, we must not lose sight of those who show up every day to make our great district run smoothly.

So whether you are headed to the beach or a Labor Day parade, we urge you to take a moment to remember the long road it took to get here and the work that lies ahead to make sure the rights of working peoples are protected and reinforced.

Stay safe, hydrated, and grateful!

-Team Malamed for Congress

[Support Zak Malamed](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zak-for-congress-1?amount=25?amount=25&refcode=em230904-laborday-el&refcode2=409_8507_-17KN9&akid=409%2E8507%2E-17KN9)
* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

Zak for Congress

Great Neck, NY 11022

Friend, This Labor Day, the grassroots advocacy group Mothering Justice is honoring the invaluable work of mothers, caregivers, and birthing people. The cost of raising a child in our country is among the highest in the world, and our social safety nets too often leave behind working families and mothers of color. That’s why I launched the Mamas’ Caucus in Congress in partnership with Mothering Justice―to fight for**policies that working mamas need, such as paid leave, affordable and quality childcare, reproductive justice and Black maternal health, and equal pay.** Mothering Justice**** advocates for these urgently-needed policies in Michigan and nationwide, and trains parents across the country to participate in the political process. [**Please donate to Mothering Justice this Labor Day to support their tireless advocacy for working mothers across the country.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953w1cdrvm2TShU3fAiYbO3lK0MV-nB2inDaPwrhTexMSJXjbuI-8XEgYunuMjTY5Z3rCMjjc5CX7i9DJa0-whebdTxF0rNvoaXSQVxm5oEYYHuBR3G_vFyMx3R0yIOMBzP0Mru2PQcVnWWK356yA7ggUqd1X2E5jO9SBRktlRwZ3mb38nijYEySlZtqeSscxMENr-gB-GOle9rLRqF5V0sPfvBnp9RrYgjoUpOx6wyQx2vjU2at12G9TReKTVn3-ASprWkYJKBjn6Z9sc31D3GstbzgntJirGvFO7OmxU4JRZJrOLo1wAbI3DVng9Vs63z5wDnXqg4x7kDdZ91fwVgWkO59WFv9xUFGW0De3Z5nAKzfjD9nKVjpF0TkIT-IWgY0l1GVJbeJ_1EccwoICyw3WXWxFe4IlpIDW2X74o-K_qByo4uiNJal7ichu_w3BZe0/3zb/ERFtDVTTT3qzI8-wm6h1-A/h1/SmpV_jBZolAE-oYZvuJl8pGUplN6GNcmyfGE4VYhC5E) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, with 100% going directly to Mothering Justice_ | | | [Chip in $15 now](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953w1cdrvm2TShU3fAiYbO3lK0MV-nB2inDaPwrhTexMSJT3iXiN8qaFtwOhfnPfy9R-EOno8YjMcI5eu-mZCd1ZYnDJOeLjFzov2dZpHdfTrzRMJYhyCe1rcNTheyROvFYenUfFR6IDF03FvVaBNuHbLgh60rdnJyFrxvl09VAkMH12TOH2iXP58KDVho6vWFyEo55OlRNsHjl1i1tYWl1yL8_IbdHEuvAAWWh8tNHn4V3DO5FhcPEL0Cl4t1SdaGhHhnltToJTQrqAWkSStRnbHcstaIMGCwzn5K3CaC8PgkKFXeVN3BqcjaezxvoMsl9sZN9qWvgpNlYdkH07a9qdjWuFuPhU_Ab_u3q11DBjXQJarc92_0PcYWZOMMezhny2fDx3sn96macFBZjxagpzb8fw_rmELFWpO2xBkFt7RQuFs9n7znQhAPZV81n54YyTpgF_MYptr1euXpMW0YxXkylX4Y0jNlJSMtYV4siZMH_bf-XJ7BAapu8Ys_JEvFS4/3zb/ERFtDVTTT3qzI8-wm6h1-A/h2/owfxRCYs3-9biCxjr-uZnTKrAqr_-bSsty1Y5XXfLL4)

\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Rashida Tlaib******
Date: Fri, Sep 1, 2023
Subject: This Labor Day, support Mothering Justice’s advocacy for working mothers
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
As we head into Labor Day weekend, let’s lift up working mothers.
**Moms make just 62 cents for every dollar dads make**. We often face bias, career interruptions and lower pay after having a child. Mothers of color―who are disproportionately low-wage workers―face additional disparities and struggles in providing for their families.
To center moms in policy-making, I founded the Mamas’ Caucus in Congress in partnership with the grassroots group Mothering Justice.
**Mothering Justice pushes for policies that working mamas need, such as paid leave, affordable and quality childcare, reproductive justice and Black maternal health, and equal pay.**

In addition to advocating for and implementing urgently-needed policies, Mothering Justice runs fellowships across the country to empower parents and caretakers with the tools needed to engage in the political process in their communities.

clean_body: This Labor Day, we’re fighting for policies that working mothers need. | | | Friend, This Labor Day, the grassroots advocacy group Mothering Justice is honoring the invaluable work of mothers, caregivers, and birthing people. The cost of raising a child in our country is among the highest in the world, and our social safety nets too often leave behind working families and mothers of color. That’s why I launched the Mamas’ Caucus in Congress in partnership with Mothering Justice―to fight forpolicies that working mamas need, such as paid leave, affordable and quality childcare, reproductive justice and Black maternal health, and equal pay. Mothering Justice** advocates for these urgently-needed policies in Michigan and nationwide, and trains parents across the country to participate in the political process. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, with 100% going directly to Mothering Justice | | | | | | | | | | Thank you so much. Together, we’re building a country where everyone can thrive. \- Rashida \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: Rashida Tlaib**** Date: Fri, Sep 1, 2023 Subject: This Labor Day, support Mothering Justice’s advocacy for working mothers To: dpwillis67@gmail.com Friend, As we head into Labor Day weekend, let’s lift up working mothers. Moms make just 62 cents for every dollar dads make. We often face bias, career interruptions and lower pay after having a child. Mothers of color―who are disproportionately low-wage workers―face additional disparities and struggles in providing for their families. To center moms in policy-making, I founded the Mamas’ Caucus in Congress in partnership with the grassroots group Mothering Justice. Mothering Justice pushes for policies that working mamas need, such as paid leave, affordable and quality childcare, reproductive justice and Black maternal health, and equal pay. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, with 100% going directly to Mothering Justice | | | | | | | | | In addition to advocating for and implementing urgently-needed policies, Mothering Justice runs fellowships across the country to empower parents and caretakers with the tools needed to engage in the political process in their communities. Together, we can make sure that working-class mothers have a seat at the table where policies are made. In solidarity, Rashida * Rashida Tlaib for Congress PO Box 32777 Detroit, MI 48232 | Paid for by Rashida Tlaib for Congress | | Sent via . name: Indiana Dems
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP03/1/67635/images/IDP%20logo.jpg]
Peter,

Happy Labor Day to you and your family from the Indiana Democratic Party!

[https://lh4.googleusercontent.com/VuQxsJK1WYMnH3ZYWmxcp666DxWL9xNT5hDaH-RTz8jwgBJs0mKOZYriMntNkJXHdLbKbNnF132P1CFQ9OJ1eP1_4DUfdEjVuWzPxmXJpwHMkZRBvW5uZl1w3mq4b6epnelAN8cgf0CR5wwfTbnKp8k]

As Democrats, we know that labor unions and working people are the backbone of our country. With Republicans seeking to curtail union rights, it’s more important than ever to fight back and support workers.

From workplace safety to better wages and fair hours, unions built America’s middle class, and it’s critical that we protect them and expand the ability for workers to join or form a union.

We hope you have a wonderful day with friends and family.

name: John Fetterman
subject: The Union Way of Life is sacred.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
This long Labor Day Weekend is brought to you by — you guessed it — the labor movement.
# [](https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/388?refcode=em20230904-8542-header&t=1&akid=8542%2E434096%2EzgM_ke)
Hi Derek!
This long Labor Day Weekend is brought to you by -- you guessed it -- the labor movement. So were the two day weekend, the eight hour workday, and nearly every other workplace protection that we take for granted.
**The Union Way of Life is sacred.** It's what built this nation, and I'm committed to working to expand and protect it.
Workers built this country, and they keep it running every single day. This Labor Day Weekend, I'm thankful for them, and for everyone joining the fight to protect workers everywhere.
Solidarity forever,
❤️ John
John Fetterman
U.S. Senator from Pennsylvania
PS: If you're able, **[please consider chipping in here to support my work in Washington.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20230904-1?refcode=em20230904-8542&refcode2=8542_434096_zgM_ke&akid=8542%2E434096%2EzgM_ke)**

name: Wesley Bell
subject: I’ll always fight for Missouri’s workers
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Happy Labor Day, Peter!

Today, we honor the workers who organized to improve working conditions for people across the country. From the 40-hour work week to minimum wage and safer working conditions to the creation of Social Security and implementation of child labor laws, we have a lot to thank the labor movement for.

I know that the path to a strong middle class starts with empowering workers and building unions. That's why I'll always stand with workers across Missouri who are organizing to fight for better wages and working conditions.

It’s time Missourians had a senator who will fight for every worker’s right to organize.

Unlike Josh Hawley, I know that Missouri’s workers are the backbone of our state. Workers are coming together to demand dignity and respect at every level across our state at this moment. It’s not just happening in factories or construction sites, but in law offices, fast food restaurants, hospitals, and local coffee shops.

Every worker deserves jobs with dignity and respect. In the Senate, I’ll fight to pass legislation that ensures workplaces are paying workers fairly, providing benefits like quality health insurance, and creating safer working conditions.

Today, please join me in honoring the accomplishments of the workers who came before us.

Have a safe and happy Labor Day,

Wesley

clean_body: By Tom Knighton Bearing Arms We write a fair bit about armed citizen stories. We're far from alone as well, with most of the pro-gun media covering various stories about regular folks using firearms to defend themselves from violent criminals. But there's debate about such incidents. There's no doubt they happen. We couldn't write about them | | | | | | | | The longtime frontman of Smash Mouth, the multi-platinum rock band from the 90s, has passed away. Steve Harwell led Smash Mouth from its inception in 1994 until his retirement in 2021 due to health issues. The band is known for hits like “Walkin' on the Sun” and “All Star.” A New York Times report did | 'Third-World Country Stuff': Homeless People Left 500 Pounds Of Feces On Streets, WY Mayor Says | | | | | NATO Member Romania 'Categorically Denies' Ukraine's Report Of Russian Drones Detonating 'On The Territory' | Weekend Media Wrap, Vol. 7: What You Missed If You Weren't Glued To The Sunday Shows | Pence Responds To Ramaswamy's Israel Remarks: He Goes 'One Direction Then Goes Back Another Direction' | Smash Mouth Singer Steve Harwell Enters Hospice Care At Only 56 Years Old | P.O. name: Greg Landsman
subject: Recognizing Ohio’s workers this Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Landsman for Congress P
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69182065/425357979/-1268629450?refcode=em_msg_2023.09.04_b1_gl_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDg5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMTFlZmU1Zi0zNTRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c7Rj1xLQLLONCIxcaEf5r43w6D0jZgMD4TOYXbajexc=&emci=12fe8b83-314b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=d11efe5f-354b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=10283101)

Friend, Happy Labor Day!

I was raised to always stand with workers, and I’m honored to champion our working families every day in Congress. **Our economy is at its strongest when we have strong unions** – _so this Labor Day, I hope you’ll join me in thanking those who fight for better wages and working conditions day in and day out._

I also wanted to take a moment to thank you for your support. Last month, thanks to folks like you from across Southwest Ohio and beyond, our campaign was able to not only reach _but surpass_ our August fundraising goal. It wouldn’t have been possible without you, **so thank you.**

Appreciate you,

_Greg_

name: Cinde Warmington
subject: Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Cinde Warmington
body:
| Hi there, today is Labor Day, when we come together to celebrate the labor movement and recommit to fight for the ideals it promises.

For three years, I have been the last line of defense on the Executive Council in the fight for the rights of all Granite Staters to be able to thrive. In my role, I was able to help our state employees get the biggest wage increase they’ve seen in decades, while pushing to deliver resources for job development, affordable housing, and child care across New Hampshire.

As your governor I will fight to make sure everyone in our state has the opportunity to get a good education, put a roof over their heads, have food on the table, and retire with dignity.

Now, this Labor Day, I want to hear from you. I’m asking you to join me in doubling-down on our commitment to working families. **_[Will you add your name to my Labor Day petition to show we’re not going to stop fighting for New Hampshire’s working families?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69182289/425358979/1064042850?source=EM_NBI_CW_20230904_PT_saborday_1.1_1MO_candidate_na_na_laborday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxODVlMzI4NC0zNjRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Awann-_7Ww1v_li-r6T7wNJO_h5jfmVKk7lG1AyBFjw=&emci=555456f1-e148-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=185e3284-364b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=45626166)_** | | **[Sign On: Support Working Families](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69182290/425358980/1064042850?source=EM_NBI_CW_20230904_PT_saborday_1.1_1MO_candidate_na_na_laborday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxODVlMzI4NC0zNjRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Awann-_7Ww1v_li-r6T7wNJO_h5jfmVKk7lG1AyBFjw=&emci=555456f1-e148-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=185e3284-364b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=45626166)**
---
This cause is personal to me: When my parents were first starting out, my father worked in a shipyard and my mother waited tables, but they were still able to buy a small house and provide for me and my three siblings.

I’ve been able to live my slice of the American Dream, but for too many working families in New Hampshire today that Dream is getting farther and farther out of reach.

And that’s exactly why I’ve always been a staunch advocate for the labor community. Unions built the middle class and attacks on unions are attacks on working families everywhere.

If you agree, join me in taking a stand for our shared values:

**_[https://go.cindewarmington.com/a/labor-day](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69182292/425358981/1064042850?source=EM_NBI_CW_20230904_PT_saborday_1.1_1MO_candidate_na_na_laborday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxODVlMzI4NC0zNjRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Awann-_7Ww1v_li-r6T7wNJO_h5jfmVKk7lG1AyBFjw=&emci=555456f1-e148-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=185e3284-364b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=45626166)_**

Thank you,

Cinde

body:
| | | It’s Labor Day. A day to acknowledge the work of generations of Americans who organized for fairer working conditions.

This Labor Day, Their Future PAC also wants to take a moment to acknowledge how far we still need to go to create a fair and just working environment for future generations.

Fair pay, decent hours, health benefits, a retirement plan, child labor protections... these things sound rudimentary, but countless hard-working Americans still can’t count on these basics. We have seen child labor protections rolled back in at least 14 states, just in the past two years. As a pediatrician and mom, this is horrifying. If we don’t replace our leaders, I fear what will happen in the coming years.

**We must demand better.**

Their Future PAC is working to build a brighter future for our kids. Stick with us as we continue this work.

subject: 🌟 A View From The Campaign Trail
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Suzanne Crouch
body:
This week, I had the honor of connecting with Hoosiers from all walks of life. Here's a snapshot of our adventures.

https://www.crouchforindiana.com/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

Friends and fellow Hoosiers,

Happy Labor Day! Today, we honor the dedication and resilience of Indiana's workers. Our state has always been powered by the hard work and determination of its people. As your Governor, I️ will be committed to ensuring that the fruits of your labor are recognized and rewarded.

Last week, I had the honor of connecting with Hoosiers from all walks of life, each with their own unique stories and dreams for our state. Your unwavering support has been our guiding light, and I'm grateful for each and every one of you who believe in our mission to make Indiana an even better place to call home.

Let's keep the conversation going. Please reach out to share your questions, comments and concerns about Indiana as I️ run for Governor.
EMAIL ME (https://crouchforindiana.com/connect/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

** Gibson County Lincoln Day Dinner
------------------------------------------------------------
https://www.facebook.com/CrouchForIndiana?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

It was a true delight to speak at the Gibson County Lincoln Day Dinner.

Abraham Lincoln's legacy reminds us of the strength of character and the enduring principles upon which our great nation was founded.

** Campaign Event in Hamilton County
------------------------------------------------------------
This past week's fundraiser in Noblesville was an evening filled with camaraderie, shared visions for Indiana's future, and the generous support of individuals like you.

** Visit to Grand Universe in Westfield
------------------------------------------------------------
https://www.facebook.com/CrouchForIndiana?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D

I️ had a great visit to Grand Universe in Westfield this week!

This state-of-the-art facility is inspiring young minds to explore the world of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM).

** Last but not least...
------------------------------------------------------------

As we travel across Indiana, one thing becomes abundantly clear: our state is filled with hardworking, dedicated people who care deeply about their communities. I am committed to being a governor who listens, who understands the unique needs of each region, and who works tirelessly to make our state even stronger.

Thank you for being an essential part of this journey. Your passion, your commitment, and your belief in a better Indiana keep us inspired and motivated every single day.

Onward and upward,

** ICYMI: Read more about my plan to #AxeTheTax
------------------------------------------------------------
I️ recently unveiled my plan to #AxeTheTax (https://secure.winred.com/friends-of-suzanne-crouch/hf-axe-the-tax?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) by eliminating the individual income tax. I️ wrote an Op-Ed in the Indy Star (https://www.indystar.com/story/opinion/2023/08/30/indiana-income-tax-bidenflation-suzanne-crouch-governor-candidate/70707154007/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D) describing my plan — one that will put more money back in your pocket (where it belongs) even as Bidenflation works to empty it.
https://www.indystar.com/story/opinion/2023/08/30/indiana-income-tax-bidenflation-suzanne-crouch-governor-candidate/70707154007/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D
Read The Full Article (https://www.indystar.com/story/opinion/2023/08/30/indiana-income-tax-bidenflation-suzanne-crouch-governor-candidate/70707154007/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

** Get Involved!
------------------------------------------------------------
Ready to make an impact? By joining my campaign, there are several ways that YOU can be a catalyst for change! Tap the "Get Involved" button below to get hands-on with the campaign, request yard signs, host an event and more.
GET INVOLVED (https://crouchforindiana.com/get-involved/?utm_source=Suzanne+Crouch+for+Indiana&utm_campaign=7b3dee295a-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_09_03_06_53&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-7b3dee295a-%5BLIST_EMAIL_ID%5D)

** Join me at one of my upcoming fundraisers!
------------------------------------------------------------
For more information or to RSVP, contact Heather at:
heather@crouchforindiana.com (mailto:heather@crouchforindiana.com) or 615-438-3531 (tel:6154383531) .

** Show Your Support - Get Suzanne Crouch Merchandise!
------------------------------------------------------------

As our campaign gains momentum, I'm excited to introduce a way for you to show your support and wear your pride for Indiana on your sleeve (literally)!

clean_body: This week, I had the honor of connecting with Hoosiers from all walks of life. Here's a snapshot of our adventures. Friends and fellow Hoosiers, Happy Labor Day! Today, we honor the dedication and resilience of Indiana's workers. Our state has always been powered by the hard work and determination of its people. As your Governor, I️ will be committed to ensuring that the fruits of your labor are recognized and rewarded. Last week, I had the honor of connecting with Hoosiers from all walks of life, each with their own unique stories and dreams for our state. Your unwavering support has been our guiding light, and I'm grateful for each and every one of you who believe in our mission to make Indiana an even better place to call home. Let's keep the conversation going. Please reach out to share your questions, comments and concerns about Indiana as I️ run for Governor. EMAIL ME () Gibson County Lincoln Day Dinner It was a true delight to speak at the Gibson County Lincoln Day Dinner. Abraham Lincoln's legacy reminds us of the strength of character and the enduring principles upon which our great nation was founded. Campaign Event in Hamilton County This past week's fundraiser in Noblesville was an evening filled with camaraderie, shared visions for Indiana's future, and the generous support of individuals like you. Visit to Grand Universe in Westfield I️ had a great visit to Grand Universe in Westfield this week! This state-of-the-art facility is inspiring young minds to explore the world of science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM). Last but not least... As we travel across Indiana, one thing becomes abundantly clear: our state is filled with hardworking, dedicated people who care deeply about their communities. I am committed to being a governor who listens, who understands the unique needs of each region, and who works tirelessly to make our state even stronger. Thank you for being an essential part of this journey. Your passion, your commitment, and your belief in a better Indiana keep us inspired and motivated every single day. Onward and upward, ICYMI: Read more about my plan to #AxeTheTax I️ recently unveiled my plan to #AxeTheTax () by eliminating the individual income tax. I️ wrote an Op-Ed in the Indy Star () describing my plan — one that will put more money back in your pocket (where it belongs) even as Bidenflation works to empty it. Read The Full Article () Get Involved! Ready to make an impact? By joining my campaign, there are several ways that YOU can be a catalyst for change! Tap the "Get Involved" button below to get hands-on with the campaign, request yard signs, host an event and more. GET INVOLVED () Join me at one of my upcoming fundraisers! For more information or to RSVP, contact Heather at: heather@crouchforindiana.com (mailto:heather@crouchforindiana.com) or 615-438-3531 (tel:6154383531) . Show Your Support - Get Suzanne Crouch Merchandise! As our campaign gains momentum, I'm excited to introduce a way for you to show your support and wear your pride for Indiana on your sleeve (literally)! SHOP () DONATE () ============================================================ Copyright © 2023 Suzanne Crouch for Indiana, All rights reserved.

Peter, we just wanted to wish you a happy Labor Day from all of us here at Clean Energy Democrats!

This Labor Day, let us honor the workers who organized to improve working conditions across the country, as well as celebrate our recent victories to pave the way for historic developments of clean energy infrastructure, built by union workers.

A little over a year since the Inflation Reduction Act, we have already seen the creation of 170,000 new clean energy jobs, and it is projected to create more than 1.5 million additional jobs over the next decade. This transition away from fossil fuels is putting us on a path to reduce emissions by 50-52% below 2005 levels by 2030.

If we want to create a safer planet for generations to come, we need to focus on good-paying, union jobs in clean manufacturing. This is not only life-saving but directly powers our nation’s workforce.

Have a safe and happy Labor Day,

Clean Energy Democrats

name: Rick Coplen
subject: Family Financial Independence
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Rick Coplen
body:
|
---
_**On this Labor Day, please help me defeat Scott Perry and start putting working people first again!**_

**Scott Perry voted against a $15 minimum wage.** I will vote to raise the minimum wage to $15 now and to a living wage as fast as we can get there. This will save taxpayers billions by no longer subsidizing low wages. It will also bolster our economy by increasing consumer spending in local small businesses. And it will lift the self-esteem of workers at the margins of our economy who will become **FINANCIALLY INDEPENDENT** , many for the first time in their lives.

**Scott Perry opposes collective bargaining** to level the playing field between workers and employers. I support this basic right that created the post-war middle class and is the best hope for millennials and others to receive what they earn.

**Scott Perry supports loan forgiveness for corporations** who donate to Congressional campaigns, but he opposes loan forgiveness for students working their way out of too much debt. I support substantial forgiveness of student loans as a way to achieve **FAMILY FINANCIAL INDEPENDENCE** for millions.

**Bottom line:** I will work tirelessly for an economy built on giving all working people a greater share of the wealth they create.

No one who works full time should need taxpayer help to have a decent life. This is what a “living wage” means to me – a wage that enables all working people to make a living and have a living.

[If you agree, please help me defeat Scott Perry and make a living wage the law of the land.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69182609/425360333/2145924871?amount=10&source=RC_EM_FR_WL_230904_1_1&refcode2=a1a624ea-374b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&contactdata=UODL9HO1zBGNTbCYLtVAEWRab5iLlV89Ge+ammp6KCqCN1gB0bXWN0D5zKvdTh+V&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODExOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMWE2MjRlYS0zNzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sr47Z2C6ZzygeNWV2eKi2Em78iCbPHMyLxmhZ3tjQAU=)

Since 1894, Labor Day has been a national holiday. For 129 years, we have honored all who labor with their backs and their brains to pave the roads, raise the food, maintain the electric grid, teach our children, repair everything, deliver everything, keep the water flowing, protect our money, and a thousand other jobs that added together equal American prosperity.

**Prosperity belongs to all of us, and all of us should prosper, not just corporate benefactors.**[Help me replace Scott Perry’s warped values with priorities that put working people first.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69182636/425360487/2145924871?amount=10&source=RC_EM_FR_WL_230904_1_1&refcode2=a1a624ea-374b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&contactdata=UODL9HO1zBGNTbCYLtVAEWRab5iLlV89Ge+ammp6KCqCN1gB0bXWN0D5zKvdTh+V&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODExOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMWE2MjRlYS0zNzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sr47Z2C6ZzygeNWV2eKi2Em78iCbPHMyLxmhZ3tjQAU=) | **[DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69182643/425360500/2145924871?amount=10&source=RC_EM_FR_WL_230904_1_1&refcode2=a1a624ea-374b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&contactdata=UODL9HO1zBGNTbCYLtVAEWRab5iLlV89Ge+ammp6KCqCN1gB0bXWN0D5zKvdTh+V&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODExOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMWE2MjRlYS0zNzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sr47Z2C6ZzygeNWV2eKi2Em78iCbPHMyLxmhZ3tjQAU=)**
---

Elon Musk didn’t make all those cars. Jeff Bezos didn’t deliver all those packages. Mike Lindell didn’t stuff all those pillows.

Let’s celebrate today on behalf of all the working women and men who did.

Onward,
Rick

clean_body: | On this Labor Day, please help me defeat Scott Perry and start putting working people first again! Scott Perry voted against a $15 minimum wage. I will vote to raise the minimum wage to $15 now and to a living wage as fast as we can get there. This will save taxpayers billions by no longer subsidizing low wages. It will also bolster our economy by increasing consumer spending in local small businesses. And it will lift the self-esteem of workers at the margins of our economy who will become FINANCIALLY INDEPENDENT , many for the first time in their lives. Scott Perry opposes collective bargaining to level the playing field between workers and employers. I support this basic right that created the post-war middle class and is the best hope for millennials and others to receive what they earn. Scott Perry supports loan forgiveness for corporations who donate to Congressional campaigns, but he opposes loan forgiveness for students working their way out of too much debt. I support substantial forgiveness of student loans as a way to achieve FAMILY FINANCIAL INDEPENDENCE for millions. Bottom line: I will work tirelessly for an economy built on giving all working people a greater share of the wealth they create. No one who works full time should need taxpayer help to have a decent life. This is what a “living wage” means to me – a wage that enables all working people to make a living and have a living. = | | = | = | = | = | = | = Since 1894, Labor Day has been a national holiday. For 129 years, we have honored all who labor with their backs and their brains to pave the roads, raise the food, maintain the electric grid, teach our children, repair everything, deliver everything, keep the water flowing, protect our money, and a thousand other jobs that added together equal American prosperity. Prosperity belongs to all of us, and all of us should prosper, not just corporate benefactors.= | = Elon Musk didn’t make all those cars. Jeff Bezos didn’t deliver all those packages. Mike Lindell didn’t stuff all those pillows. Let’s celebrate today on behalf of all the working women and men who did. Onward, Rick | | | | | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Friends of Rick Coplen | =

name: Sean Barry for Congress
subject: The significance of Labor Day
body:
I hope you're enjoying your long weekend | | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDOH8vS-H2wVKd-2YocowzUKsqJ2Uw2Dw7Hu4rQilhmmHhPjBa14JlvtykRTmyNFCOsJvrG7UVdn2EDz1h5FwP4UNq4JkYCuD44-72-9qlQVBjC3_HoKeIJb6m67pbnSC6T1u1-Dasla2nQF6kpGpGdLzBDswBIAOmNeiExiWtz6dkS9gwcNXIlqmb6qsAlk2Z9VkA5LVurLWJi28qvtB2zVsD-rQa5kKocPoQkLaZO0tPZIZUvSn-7vpovq2Dxi9KQ/3zb/6yX4UavSQeeUznWESPDHJQ/h0/zpIAaP5JyMaZsTEG6i-RcXb9FJ2-_-WTzY7roVvyziw)
---
Friend, Happy Labor Day! I hope you enjoy a well-deserved day off. Our district is full of hardworking men and women who aren’t getting a fair shake in Washington. Many of our leaders are wealthy and work to secure their own selfish interests instead of serving the working class Americans who make up our community. I want to put an end to this. Our workers deserve better representation. Representation that works every day to honor the sacrifice of labor before me, to coordinate with those still fighting to ensure fair pay and safe workplaces. To make sure we have a brighter tomorrow, and that we can take care of our own today In Congress, I’ll work to increase the minimum wage, secure further protections for union rights, ensure big corporations meet basic living standards, and protect medicare and social security so our workers know they will retire comfortably. A functioning democracy requires a strong and powerful working class and a government that represents them. I’m running to serve you, not the wealthy and powerful. Thank you for all your support, I hope you have a great day. Sean Barry Candidate for Congress (TX-29)
---

name: Lucas Kunce
subject: America’s greatest asset
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lucas Kunce for Missouri
body:
#
Hey Peter, it’s Lucas Kunce.
**I wanted to take some time today to talk about our country’s greatest asset.** A force that built our country. A force that defended our country. And a force that created the middle-class way of life in our country.
As our nation found itself under attack in World War II, they mobilized in the workplace and on the battlefield, becoming the key to turning the tide against fascism.
In the face of a political status quo that violently defended segregation, voter suppression, and discrimination in countless forms, they united with civil rights leaders to fight equality and justice — and continue to today.
And when union-busters and corporate political machines tried to break them, they banded together to demand dignity for everyday people, securing safer workplaces, 8-hour workdays, weekends, and benefits like paid leave, healthcare, and retirement for millions of Americans — all while building our country from the ground up.
So, any guesses?
**Our country’s greatest asset is the American worker** — and I want to join you this Labor Day in honoring the critical role they’ve played in creating our way of life.
**But celebrating their accomplishments is not enough.**
Massive corporations and the corrupt politicians they’ve bought off have launched an all-out assault on our workers. Through corporate PACs, powerful lobbyists, and state-by-state assaults on unions, now only a handful of massive companies dominate our economy in a system that’s rigged against working people.
It has destroyed family farms, made the costs of life-saving drugs skyrocket, suppressed wages, made it impossible for small businesses to compete, and shipped countless jobs overseas. And after they finish stripping our communities for parts, they leave behind forgotten towns and neighborhoods filled with boarded-up buildings and empty lots.
**It’s class warfare from the top — and this is not a war we can afford to lose.**
We need a new generation of leadership ready to lead, fight, and organize against corrupt corporate elites in both parties who’ve used division and obstruction to prevent every effort to empower American workers — efforts like passing the PRO Act, guaranteeing a living wage, breaking up Big Ag, Big Tech, the pharmaceutical cartels, and the rest of the monopolists whose goal is to destroy our way of life.
Thanks to years of corrupt political leadership in Missouri, our state has become the frontline in this fight. And that’s why I’m running for U.S. Senate to be a warrior for working people — with the support of the Missouri AFL-CIO, Missouri Fire Fighters, and so many other members of organized labor across this state.
I hope you’ll join us on the battlefield.
In solidarity,
Lucas Kunce

_**A note from Team Kunce**_ Without a single cent from corporate PACs, we’ve built a record-breaking, people-powered movement to defeat Josh Hawley and flip Missouri’s U.S. Senate seat. But we can’t afford to slow down now. GOP mega-donors and corporate elites are already spending MILLIONS to tear down Lucas and keep control of this Senate seat. **[So if you’re able to support Lucas’ grassroots campaign, please consider making a donation of any amount today:](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/36267?refcode=em20230904-3687&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=3687%2E614464%2EUOd1X7)****[Add a donation →](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/36267?refcode=em20230904-3687&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=3687%2E614464%2EUOd1X7)**
---

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lk-email-footer?refcode=em20230904-3687-footer&t=3&refcode2=3687_614464_UOd1X7&akid=3687%2E614464%2EUOd1X7)**
---

name: Mandela Barnes
subject: I grew up in Milwaukee in a proud union household.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by The Long Run PAC, P
body:
#
Happy Labor Day! Today is a special day for my family and the many other proud union households across the country, so I wanted to take a moment today to share my story with you.
My story is a Wisconsin story — one that wouldn’t be possible without my parents’ union jobs. I don’t come from a wealthy or well-connected family. I grew up in Milwaukee in a proud union household.
My grandfather worked as a steelworker at A.O. Smith after he came back from World War II. My dad worked 3rd shift at the GM factory. My mom was a public school teacher.
Those jobs were our ticket into the middle class. A job that lets you pay down your mortgage and put some money away to get you through tough times. A job that lets your kids go to college if they want to or lets you take care of your parents when they get sick without worrying about putting food on the table.
Those are the jobs that built our middle class and made me who I am today.
Middle and working-class Americans are exactly who President Biden has been fighting for. People like you and me who are working to support their families.
It’s no secret that President Biden, a son of Scranton, is the most pro-union, pro-worker president of our lifetimes.
He understands working people are the backbone of our economy, believes in and supports the power of collective action and organizing, and has created thousands of good paying union jobs across the country.
President Biden is actively investing in our communities and prioritizing working Americans by investing in our infrastructure and bringing manufacturing home to the U.S. The historic _Bipartisan Infrastructure Deal_ is creating good paying union jobs, growing our economy equitably, and investing in communities that have been too often left behind.
He has said himself, “The middle class built America, and unions built the middle class!” And he stands by that sentiment.
On this Labor Day, I am asking you to join me in supporting a leader who supports us and working families all over the country.
[**So, if you can, please split a contribution between President Biden’s reelection campaign and The Long Run today. Let’s show our support for the most pro-union president in history.**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-biden-split.r3wA0I&refcode=em230904-1-t3&t=1&akid=4801%2E196590%2ECew0Mf)
[CONTRIBUTE](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=longrunpac-em-biden-split.r3wA0I&refcode=em230904-1-t3&t=2&akid=4801%2E196590%2ECew0Mf)
We deserve to be represented by a president who shares our experiences and our values. Someone who believes workers deserve our respect, protection, and a fair wage.
Happy Labor Day.
Mandela

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you. We'd hate to see you go, but if you'd like you can [**unsubscribe here**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=3&akid=4801%2E196590%2ECew0Mf). You can also [**sign up to receive fewer emails.**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/signup/fewer-emails/?source=em-footer&t=4&akid=4801%2E196590%2ECew0Mf)
The Long Run PAC is funded by grassroots donors giving what they can, when they can. [**Make a contribution to The Long Run PAC today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/longrunpac-em-footer?refcode=em-footer&t=5&refcode2=4801_196590_Cew0Mf&akid=4801%2E196590%2ECew0Mf)
Submit this form to [**update your contact information.**](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/signup/update-contact-info/?source=em-footer&t=6&akid=4801%2E196590%2ECew0Mf)
Paid for by The Long Run PAC, P.O. Box 597
Milwaukee, WI 53201, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

People like you and me who are working to support their families. It’s no secret that President Biden, a son of Scranton, is the most pro-union, pro-worker president of our lifetimes. He understands working people are the backbone of our economy, believes in and supports the power of collective action and organizing, and has created thousands of good paying union jobs across the country. President Biden is actively investing in our communities and prioritizing working Americans by investing in our infrastructure and bringing manufacturing home to the U.S. The historic Bipartisan Infrastructure Deal is creating good paying union jobs, growing our economy equitably, and investing in communities that have been too often left behind. He has said himself, “The middle class built America, and unions built the middle class!” And he stands by that sentiment. On this Labor Day, I am asking you to join me in supporting a leader who supports us and working families all over the country. We deserve to be represented by a president who shares our experiences and our values. Someone who believes workers deserve our respect, protection, and a fair wage. Happy Labor Day. Mandela

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: It’s been a Hot Union Summer
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Happy Labor Day!

Justice Democrats will always stand with workers and fight to protect the
right to organize. Our progressive champions have joined workers on the
picket line this summer across the country to express solidarity.

We know a brighter, more equitable future is possible. But we’re going to
have to organize for it. We’re going to have to fight for it. And the
chance might feel incremental but every time workers band together to
organize is a big step in a positive direction.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/137594?refcode=em20230904-30654&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1001&akid=30654%2E1203867%2EUXJBZ4 ]If you can afford it, will you donate $3 right now to
help us recruit and elect more progressive champions who will fight for
workers' rights?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/137594?refcode=em20230904-30654&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=30654%2E1203867%2EUXJBZ4 ]Contribute $3

As always, thank you so much for your support.

In solidarity,

Team JD

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that
this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=1021&akid=30654%2E1203867%2EUXJBZ4 ]here. We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong.

Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the
[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=1022&akid=30654%2E1203867%2EUXJBZ4 ]Centers for Disease Control and your state public health department.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20230904-30654-footer&t=1023&refcode2=30654_1203867_UXJBZ4&akid=30654%2E1203867%2EUXJBZ4

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1024&akid=30654%2E1203867%2EUXJBZ4

name: Daniel Cameron
subject: What the Future Holds
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Daniel Cameron for Kentucky
body:
With your help, I’m optimistic for the future of Kentucky
To view this email as a web page, go [here](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/vm2/33ca55422052231c/40933/5bcfc7c5c9bba8f816f79d307c796b93/40933)

---
Friends, I am optimistic about our future. I can't say the same for Andy Beshear. Beshear is a shutdown governor who trampled on our God given freedoms. Never again. We must end the failed Biden-Beshear agenda now, once and for all. I'm counting on you to join us to bring that optimism back to the Commonwealth. You have my commitment that I will work day and night to deliver BOLD leadership to Kentucky. But I can't do it without you. I need your help.
---
[**FIGHT FOR KENTUCKY’S FUTURE**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5bcfc7c5c9bba8f816f79d307c796b93/40933/33ca55422052231c/57e39d1d7833a884c3d6b838967d376c/40933)
---
We have every reason to be optimistic because of the great teachers in Kentucky and the promise of our young people. We can be optimistic because of our communities and our people. We are optimistic that when we back the blue, our communities will be safe again.
---
[**GIVE KENTUCKY A REASON TO HOPE**](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5bcfc7c5c9bba8f816f79d307c796b93/40933/33ca55422052231c/90fde2af930d5469c3d6b838967d376c/40933)
---
I know that together our movement can turn around Kentucky. We will restore our values. We will catch up our kids in school and help them thrive again. We will hold criminals accountable and make our streets safe again. But I need your help. _**[Give $5, $15, $25, $50, or more TODAY!](http://ci.criticalimpact.com/go/1/5bcfc7c5c9bba8f816f79d307c796b93/40933/33ca55422052231c/be30db26cff07d63c3d6b838967d376c/40933)**_ With your help we have every reason to be optimistic again about the future of Kentucky. I'm counting on you. For Kentucky,Daniel Cameron
---
