name: Bob Casey
subject: For 141 years
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BOB CASEY FOR SENATE INC
body:
# [](https://go.bobcasey.com/go/181?t=1&akid=553%2E552475%2EfN7SgF)
Hi Peter,
On the first Labor Day, back in 1882, few protections for workers existed and their voices were often drowned out, but generations of workers organized and fought for fair wages and workplace protections. Their blood and sweat helped build the middle class.
These were men like my grandfather who, even at age 11, knew that working in the coal mines was a dark and dangerous way to make a living, but he took on the work as a young boy anyway to help his family make ends meet.
**Even today, that work is far from over.** Far too many of our neighbors and workers still lack even basic benefits like paid sick leave and family leave. Here in Pennsylvania and across the country, workers are still fighting for a liveable wage, decent benefits, and safe working conditions.
I’m proud of what we’ve accomplished in recent years, especially the passage of the Inflation Reduction Act, relief for millions of families, and the creation of millions of jobs. There is still more to do to deliver results for Pennsylvania workers and their families.
That’s why I’ll keep fighting in Congress to protect our unions and end the taxpayer subsidization of anti-union activity. For too long the deck in Washington has been stacked against workers.
This Labor Day, I hope I can count on you to fight alongside me and all those working every day to provide for their families and build a better life. We must show support for workers now more than ever.
Thank you, and happy Labor Day.
Bob Casey

name: Salud Carbajal
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
body:
Today, as I celebrate this Labor Day with friends and community members throughout the Central Coast, I am reminded of the many rights and privileges we enjoy because of the workers who made them possible.

Labor Day was established in the late nineteenth century at the urging of labor activists in order to recognize the great impact American workers have on our society. We owe much to these visionary labor leaders and are grateful for their efforts.

I hope you are able to enjoy today safely with friends and family. Thank you for joining me in acknowledging workers throughout our country, and best wishes for a Happy Labor Day!

Sincerely,
Salud Carbajal

Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
PO Box 1290, Santa Barbara, CA 93102
info@saludcarbajal.com; www.saludcarbajal.com

name: Nikki Budzinski
subject: Happy Labor Day, folks!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nikki Budzinski for Congress**
body:
|
#
---
Happy Labor Day, folks! Today is a recognition of the hardworking men and women who built our country and a chance to appreciate everything we have because of the labor movement. Before coming to Congress, I spent my entire adult life as a labor activist, fighting to get hardworking people the pay and workplace safety conditions they deserve. I’m proud to have worked on behalf of firefighters, first responders, meatpackers, and grocery store workers to ensure they have the power to collectively bargain for better wages, benefits, and safer working conditions. **Union jobs are the backbone of the middle class and essential to strong and thriving communities.** I am committed to working with all of my colleagues in Congress to champion workers' rights for people in Central and Southern Illinois and across the country. Earlier this year, I was proud to sign on as co-sponsor of the _Richard L. Trumka Protecting the Right to Organize Act_ – comprehensive bipartisan legislation to protect workers’ right to come together and bargain for higher wages, better benefits, and safer workplaces. [**So in honor of Labor Day, I hope you’ll join me in standing up for organized labor and protecting hardworking families by adding your name to my petition to call on my colleagues in Congress to join me in passing the _Richard L. Trumka Protecting the Right to Organize Act_ >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69183878/425364901/1197011967?refcode=AU_NJB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20230904_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzhjZTQxYS0zYzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ayXeHjj72jW5kZwI0bgRD13j2nyo1p2YB21WdbKCn24=&emci=838186ce-e448-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=0c8ce41a-3c4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=13891387) | [ADD MY NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69183879/425364902/1197011967?refcode=AU_NJB_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20230904_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjQwNyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYzhjZTQxYS0zYzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ayXeHjj72jW5kZwI0bgRD13j2nyo1p2YB21WdbKCn24=&emci=838186ce-e448-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=0c8ce41a-3c4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=13891387)

Let's take a moment to reflect on what we've achieved together: ✅ We've won health care benefits for over 1 million people in seven states; ✅ Together, we've fought for and secured paid family leave in five states, directly benefiting millions of workers; ✅ And let's not forget demanding fair wages for workers and winning nine ballot measures to raise the minimum wage! Yes, team, these victories are yours too, because none of this could have been possible without you standing shoulder to shoulder with us. When we align our efforts with unions, grassroots organizers, and hardworking Americans from coast to coast, WE PREVAIL! Looking ahead to November and beyond, the stakes could not be higher. From fighting for reproductive freedom, to securing fair wages and paid leave, and standing up to predatory lenders who take advantage of the vulnerable—our mission is broad and urgent. To emerge victorious in each of our campaigns, we need everyone — yes, that includes you, friend — to be a part of this collective endeavor. **[Can we count on you to keep the momentum going with a donation today?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/69183998/425365316/1622098051?&refcode=E3991-20230904-LaborDay-SL-Holiday&amount=15&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRlAvMS84NTQ3OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0YmQ4ODgzZS0zYzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=6i7L3XWjwytwD-FffRJ8R11WPys9lkhUgSpT8HiFlyE=)** [MAKE YOUR IMPACT! clean_body: | | Team, Labor Day is not just a long weekend; it's a testament to the incredible accomplishments that can be achieved when working people stand united. Let's take a moment to reflect on what we've achieved together: ✅ We've won health care benefits for over 1 million people in seven states; ✅ Together, we've fought for and secured paid family leave in five states, directly benefiting millions of workers; ✅ And let's not forget demanding fair wages for workers and winning nine ballot measures to raise the minimum wage! Yes, team, these victories are yours too, because none of this could have been possible without you standing shoulder to shoulder with us. When we align our efforts with unions, grassroots organizers, and hardworking Americans from coast to coast, WE PREVAIL! Looking ahead to November and beyond, the stakes could not be higher. From fighting for reproductive freedom, to securing fair wages and paid leave, and standing up to predatory lenders who take advantage of the vulnerable—our mission is broad and urgent. To emerge victorious in each of our campaigns, we need everyone — yes, that includes you, friend — to be a part of this collective endeavor. = = On this Labor Day, let's recommit to the values that bind us—justice, equality, and an unwavering belief in the dignity of working people. Together, we can and will shape a better future for all. In solidarity, Fairness Project Thanks to grassroots supporters like you, our team at the Fairness Project has won over 30 people-powered ballot measure campaigns since 2016, changing over 18 million lives by expanding health care, raising wages, guaranteeing paid leave, and ending predatory lending directly at the ballot box when politicians refuse to act. Your support allows us to make this progress possible.** = = Contributions or gifts to the Fairness Project are not deductible as charitable contributions for federal income tax purposes. Contributions to the Fairness Project support its many efforts nationwide and any focus is at the Fairness Project’s sole discretion. Contributions will not necessarily be used to support activity in any one state. You can request to get only the most important emails from our team , or you can . name: Shawn Wilson
subject: The gloves are coming off, Peter
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WILSON FOR LOUISIANA
body:
It was only a matter of time before they attacked me

It was only a matter of time, Peter – In case you missed it, national Republicans launched a six-figure ad campaign attacking me and smearing our campaign. But hey, I’m not worried — **this is the clearest sign yet that we’re ruffling some feathers on the GOP side.** In fact, _they’re_ the ones who are worried. They’re worried that we’ll close the gap in the polls, beat the odds to win this race, and upset their plan to flip another governor’s seat in the South. Our campaign is making an impact, and they know it. So here’s the deal: My campaign’s biggest filing deadline yet is tonight at midnight, and every dollar we raise will go directly toward countering the GOP’s bogus narrative. Can I count on you to chip in and help us get to work? **_[It was only a matter of time before they took the gloves off, and now, we need to punch back. | | Today, on Labor Day, we honor the American worker and those who fought for the social and economic rights of workers across the United States. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69184657/425367480/-2122444374?express_lane=true&amount=&refcode=2023-09-04-laborday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0ZjVhNmFjZS0zYzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=-QI-Lx9Zw2Wp8BC0hTnRaVdOdtloDUKATDcdUkBXnLY=) As the son of a union machinist, I know the difference a union makes in delivering fair wages and the strong workplace protections that every worker deserves. President Biden and Democrats in Congress have been working hard to put power back in the hands of workers. For too long, the economy has been rigged to favor powerful corporations, so now is the time to restore fairness in the workplace and make the economy work for workers. In the last few years, we've made enormous strides investing in an economy that creates opportunities for everyone, not just the mega-sized corporations. But we know our work is far from over. This year, I joined a group of Senators to introduce comprehensive legislation that would protect workers' right to organize and bargain for higher wages, better benefits, and safer workplaces in Oregon and nationwide. Unions built the middle class, and they are crucial to growing and reinforcing an economy that rewards hard-working people. I will never stop fighting for working families and ensuring they have every opportunity to succeed. name: Donald J. Trump
subject: RE: On this Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trump Save America Joint Fundraising Committee, a joint fundraising committee composed of and authorized by Donald J
body:
Crooked Joe has abandoned and betrayed the American worker.
| | | [ ](https://email.win.donaldjtrump.com/c/eJw8kUGPmzAQhX-NuYHw2DH2gUO3FlspUbVpD01PkWsPyaywQWA2yr-viLJ7_d7T6H0aF6eB-nvLmhcGcKWwMAAmvjGAvXw5_VT7P3tbHg76UFq1k7cthe8MIN8n_Ko-r1QYHQ0bbWwRWvAavC-w5coILYXRuri2XDv0Owm9ajRIbgx67Bs0HlzonYGCWqhB1KaWXHHY6QqlEo03wQQD_zw4JusbpSqMyQ3hPc9rnCo_xmJorzlPy2NSx6Bb0K8zVjdKM4atsTH3gaWLOJN35ftIKZf9msLsaKF0Kf0YI-WMyKBLfoDgdr1ojOOh1oFzJro1x3PEQGtkwj591QaXcZ09MmE9nd2TeRcnR5fEhP10-kzGlDFlJiyvgQsG6pwGCkzYY_f2A-_z8fT3AR8vEfb0enz9_fHrzRZzG6YbDQMtqmGyvmwTNrn_AQAA__8ZhI3i)
---
Friend,
On this Labor Day, I want to pay tribute to one group of Americans that has sadly been abandoned by the current regime in Washington: _the American worker._
Since the moment I first announced I would run for President of the United States, I’ve put hardworking Americans at the forefront of our campaign.
Even back in the 1980s, I used to say that the people I get along best with are the construction workers, the taxi drivers, the people who _truly_ power our cities. And I hated the fact that Washington, D.C. always put American workers dead last.
I couldn’t stand to see politicians put globalist special interests ahead of our own national interests, sign trade deals that enable manufacturers to shut down in the United States and relocate overseas, allow the entire world to exploit our country, and let illegal aliens steal jobs from American citizens.
The time had finally come for me to step into the arena and become a champion for the long-forgotten American worker.
I promised to become the **GREATEST JOBS PRESIDENT GOD HAD EVER CREATED**.
And in four short years, I can humbly say that I delivered on that promise and gave us the greatest and strongest economy in American history.
Now that I’ve left office, voters can see the stark contrast between our America First, pro-growth policies and the globalist Radical Left trainwreck of the Biden-Harris regime.
Inflation is ravaging our economy, energy prices are out of control, younger Americans have completely lost hope in ever owning a home, our Southern border has been demolished, taxes are rising, banks are failing and getting bailed out, millions of taxpayer dollars are being spent on witch hunts to imprison me as an innocent man, and at a time when our own citizens continue to cry out for help, Biden ignores the Silent Majority and sends more of your money to faraway lands.
But do not fear – when I take office in January of 2025, we will rescue our nation’s failing economy and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN (_AGAIN_)!
_Of course, I can’t conclude a message about our failing economy without saying that if you’re doing poorly, don’t even think about donating. Enjoy the holiday and remember that your favorite President will be back very soon to save our country._
**[Please make a contribution of even $1 to MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN.](https://email.win.donaldjtrump.com/c/eJw8kUGP2jAQhX-NcwtyZozjHHLo1mIPoKrtHsoNGXsCg2InSpxF9NdXQWyv33savU_j4thz92hF_SYArhxmASDwmwDYq7fjD73_s7fl4WAOpdVbdV9T-C4A8mOk_9XXlQ1Fx_1Ka1uEFrwB7wtqK92gUdgYU1xb70CGLRGh6XytOq-xOxvjuvqMpGRXcAsSUDZSVbqCrdmQ0lj7JjShgbMHJ5S8c9qEIbk-3PK0xHHjh1j07TXncX5O2gnYzeSXiTZ3ThOFtbEy90mlizSxd-Vt4JTLbklhcjxzupR-iJFzJhKwS76H4LYd1o2rgjShqgTulhxPkQIvUaB9-eoVzsMyeRJoPZ_ci3kXR8eXJNB-OX0lQ8qUskBbSahQgD6lnoNAe3vAcvyb8od-wudL0B7ff71_fP7-aYupDeOd-55nXQslL-uEVe5fAAAA___aH46s)**

clean_body: Crooked Joe has abandoned and betrayed the American worker. | | | Friend, On this Labor Day, I want to pay tribute to one group of Americans that has sadly been abandoned by the current regime in Washington: the American worker. Since the moment I first announced I would run for President of the United States, I’ve put hardworking Americans at the forefront of our campaign. Even back in the 1980s, I used to say that the people I get along best with are the construction workers, the taxi drivers, the people who truly power our cities. And I hated the fact that Washington, D.C. always put American workers dead last. I couldn’t stand to see politicians put globalist special interests ahead of our own national interests, sign trade deals that enable manufacturers to shut down in the United States and relocate overseas, allow the entire world to exploit our country, and let illegal aliens steal jobs from American citizens. The time had finally come for me to step into the arena and become a champion for the long-forgotten American worker. I promised to become the GREATEST JOBS PRESIDENT GOD HAD EVER CREATED. And in four short years, I can humbly say that I delivered on that promise and gave us the greatest and strongest economy in American history. Now that I’ve left office, voters can see the stark contrast between our America First, pro-growth policies and the globalist Radical Left trainwreck of the Biden-Harris regime. Inflation is ravaging our economy, energy prices are out of control, younger Americans have completely lost hope in ever owning a home, our Southern border has been demolished, taxes are rising, banks are failing and getting bailed out, millions of taxpayer dollars are being spent on witch hunts to imprison me as an innocent man, and at a time when our own citizens continue to cry out for help, Biden ignores the Silent Majority and sends more of your money to faraway lands. But do not fear – when I take office in January of 2025, we will rescue our nation’s failing economy and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN (AGAIN)! Of course, I can’t conclude a message about our failing economy without saying that if you’re doing poorly, don’t even think about donating. Enjoy the holiday and remember that your favorite President will be back very soon to save our country. | Thank you, | Donald J. Trump 45th President of the United States Contributions to Trump Save America JFC are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. Our country is hurting right now. Our border is in chaos, our dollar is losing value, our justice system is dead, our rights are being ripped from our hands, and our enemies abroad are plotting ways to exploit our weaknesses. We have serious matters to deal with - not televised charades orchestrated by Crooked Joe and his corrupt accomplices. I'm going to focus on the REAL ISSUES that matter to Americans. And that's how we're going to win back the White House in 2024 and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! If you 're doing poorly due to the very sad state of our country right now, then don't even think about donating! | Thank you, | Donald J. Trump 45th President of the United States Contributions to Trump Save America JFC are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. Paid for by Trump Save America Joint Fundraising Committee, a joint fundraising committee composed of and authorized by Donald J. Trump for President 2024, Inc. and Save America.

name: Team Padora
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Padora for Colorado
body:
Folks, We wanted to take a moment to recognize that today is Labor Day – a day that celebrates the long fight in this country toward greater quality of work in America. | | | [](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/u/click?_t=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_m=1eb329fedb944dea8196879616e0ff5b&_e=HFjRR-z4hOqSjzRHMGFqRZ4xglkHssy5_f8HKoX7AujCv0qNhMUR924JyZv3A-93lISCN7FZrs3VeJtX03k7gFTBsyc6JDyabf45Yq71HPqOt32wB-f2HGb0BgB7-wqXKBp4wyT1deDKeG9aa-VK3BM3Bda7jIqPMqqWT_-gOF3Bl_mdgiBwh_tNC2mlBUqMuNipu_OJZdtUCh9HeQQyUcdya5Dn3gElaRZhjr_1D0slkCR24fhS8DnK2rz7a85O_1_OXtDLi9PMmhDdyPI-Kc9H-Pye0Tpw6b_5TnNFABTDrJlhAsvzOoTRMAYYdJqdDpVOCcSVWIzHP7oZJTptpbpZs7lRgKfoWPm6DeZgt68w3IMpLOtKqHqcKqKJqZmw3kGZgss7NaFXneoL7IN5AQ%3D%3D) Folks, We wanted to take a moment to recognize that today is Labor Day – a day that celebrates the long fight in this country toward greater quality of work in America. Today we can honor the contributions and sacrifices of workers across this country who have built our communities into what they are today. And although the situation in America is far better for workers than it was before the labor movement began, not all workers are protected. We stand in solidarity with those who are still fighting for fair wages, safe working conditions, and successful unionization. We know that the fight for workers' rights is far from over, and John is committed to working with you to create a more equitable and just society for all. Once again, we thank you for your hard work, dedication, and support. We are inspired by your commitment to our shared values and look forward to continuing our work together. Happy Labor Day. – Team Padora **My story is not unique.** I was born and raised in Pennsylvania in rural, blue-collar, and old coal mining communities. I'm a proud husband and father to three amazing children. I witnessed firsthand how both major political parties have neglected rural communities and left behind working families. I'm running to build a movement and a government that works for working families. [**Join me.**](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/u/click?_t=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_m=1eb329fedb944dea8196879616e0ff5b&_e=HFjRR-z4hOqSjzRHMGFqRdbjXOXSRKQ5L08R4zEpsQT2uX_hTLww08kMi3Q0Y6X-GRoeu2I3UQi9gqwTnaUz9V2KYXeyLirWjIVwEhQljvw18X4lRM5pS7NkUXwoLxBYCyGalUMYaDT66Clv6Cc7jQhWTr88UaNkKKHleX7wOx_r9pOfwrgbU1o9aYOJDjbViAzAi40nVmzF3SZ6bZCzQQ%3D%3D) [DONATE MONTHLY](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/u/click?_t=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_m=1eb329fedb944dea8196879616e0ff5b&_e=HFjRR-z4hOqSjzRHMGFqRZ4xglkHssy5_f8HKoX7AujCv0qNhMUR924JyZv3A-93lISCN7FZrs3VeJtX03k7gFTBsyc6JDyabf45Yq71HPqOt32wB-f2HGb0BgB7-wqXKBp4wyT1deDKeG9aa-VK3BM3Bda7jIqPMqqWT_-gOF3Bl_mdgiBwh_tNC2mlBUqMuNipu_OJZdtUCh9HeQQyUcdya5Dn3gElaRZhjr_1D0s3ufeRThZR4h1KKO9uJgSkXTrQoFp5o8NkYGQHQsLyKjwNRTrk0PcBheYvEgR3yiH7gEsnAAtD8xiP8Bb98Z5JExSXh3mJk5XX3_pqJS3dZny4bWqWKPRehBesL5iWCZfRTN6m-ezZNjBm9Jw4qkd_DAISJb4miGmtX5bIdYr29mii5dUyyKXrtFjZiOIzUrQ%3D) [**Unsubscribe**](http://links.padoraforcongress.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=1632d2fd715245289db23a5b99e588cb&_s=1eb329fedb944dea8196879616e0ff5b&_t=gc5WHTPd55ci5Es3UuQd6I-MJwVMdu2k5zSrKznkhdAvp-3_XThSC8G7ZKTaMtEWjFRbc008Ej9O2sqbzrCgmMkNHLwlmgETHkVXsN1n9Xk%3D) Paid for by Padora for Colorado

name: =?utf-8?B?UmFxdWVsIFRlcsOhbg==?=
subject: Honoring Labor
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Terán for Congress
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upliftteran?refcode=X_090423_LaborDay [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upliftteran?refcode=X_090423_LaborDay]
Peter,
Through my experience as an organizer with Mi Familia Vota, an SEIU civic participation program, and working for UFCW Local 99 nearly two decades ago, I learned the power of unions.
In Arizona, the Republican extremists have continued to attack our labor movement by trying to stop them from improving working conditions, unionizing, and their efforts to build political power.
And when the same forces gutted our education system and health care and attacked our immigrant communities, Labor has not shied away. They have collectively worked with our progressive organizations to fight back. To stop the chipping away of our education, protect health care and access to abortions, and push back against the anti-immigrant sentiment.
Never once did our friends in labor say, “those aren’t our fights.” They said, “we have your back.”
We rise on the shoulders of Labor. Who, despite the adversities, have built strong unions.
From construction workers, grocery store workers, and those who respond to crises. Labor has organized their workplace and built political power.
Now we have to seal the deal on the work that has been done by the labor movement and by the young and old knocking on doors in 115-degree weather. We have to seal the deal and break the cycle of poverty and get to a stronger middle class -- because the other side won’t do it for us. It’s up to us.
We have to retake the majority in Congress and protect our majority in the Senate. We have to re-elect President Biden, and support progressives at the state and local level here in Arizona too.
We are stronger than we’ve ever been. We are more than allies -- we are family -- and we can get this done! Thank you!
Happy Labor Day,
Raquel Terán
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upliftteran?refcode=X_090423_LaborDay]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/655A566A4D6351583942505531424E6A756B655939795555664E6B68692F6D746E713875657771364A42586E5A654649766D373776636D324B436851376D3643/editor_images/RT%20Bio%20Picture.png] Raquel Terán is running to represent Arizona's 3rd Congressional District as the first Latina congresswoman from Arizona.
For nearly 20 years, she's been fighting for higher wages for workers, healthcare for all, and better schools for our children.
She's helped build broad and diverse coalitions that have beaten Republicans like Donald Trump, Joe Arpaio, and Kari Lake.
Now she's ready to take the fight to Congress, but we'll need your support to win. Chip in today! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upliftteran?refcode=x_subheader]
Terán for Congress
PO Box 1041
Glendale, AZ 85311
United States
Paid for by Terán for Congress

name: The Federalist Wire
subject: Joe Biden may be in deep trouble after this troubling report surfaced
body:
TheFederalistWire.com

DAILY NEWS | September 04, 2023

Joe Biden may be in
deep trouble after this troubling report surfaced
The heat of a presidential
election is no time for panic. But for President Biden, that's
all he's doing.

And Joe Biden may be in deep
trouble after this troubling report surfaced.
➞ Continue Reading

TRENDING NEWS

Massachusetts' Governor
makes a surprising decision that leaves many Democrats speechless

The state of
Massachusetts seems to be in a state of panic. Many are wondering
what will happen next.
➞ Continue Reading

President Biden just left a
key Democrat fighting for their life

Joe Biden's
disastrous policies have left leaders across the country begging
him for help. The problem is, that he refuses to answer the call.
➞ Continue Reading

Donald Trump made a
surprise announcement that left everyone stunned

Politics are like a
game of chess. And Trump just declared "check mate."
➞ Continue Reading

ABOUT
US
The
Federalist Wire brings you dependable news that highlights the US
political landscape as well as the economy. We're here for
citizens who want to stay in the know. If you're particularly
interested in politics or economics, our regular news updates
will help keep you informed. If you're not especially interested
in the topics but know it's important to keep up, then our
reports will be simple to understand.

You know that the political and
economic worlds often collide, and that's why we provide you with
specific coverage. Our news team strives to pinpoint and report
the stories that interest you most. We keep our eyes on domestic
and international matters, both political and financial, so we
can help you stay on top of the latest happenings.

DISCLAIMER
Your privacy is important to us.
It is The Federalist Wire's policy to respect your privacy and
comply with any applicable law and regulation regarding any
personal information we may collect about you, including across
our website, and other sites we own and operate.

name: Lynn Peterson
subject: My thoughts on Labor Day.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Lynn Peterson for Congress
body:
Unions have given us some of the fundamental benefits we take for granted.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69186338/425373886/-2024045026?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84MzU0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYmVhMzE0NC00MTRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wtkv1_mh6QpX-6UTn_sd1Qf1RDY0YaZI_YGx8K8SFmg=&emci=697684d7-eb48-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=ebea3144-414b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=29359352)
---

| | Happy Labor Day, Peter! **Today, we celebrate the incredible achievements of the American labor movement and renew our resolve for the challenges ahead.** When workers come together, they are a powerful force for change. Unions have given us some of the fundamental benefits we take for granted, like the establishment of a forty-hour workweek, normalizing weekends off, and ensuring the safety net of worker's compensation. They've also been instrumental in increasing transparency and equity to help close racial and gender pay gaps. **I will always stand in solidarity with our unions — and this year, that meant backing our nurses at the picket line and showing support for UPS workers.** **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69186339/425373887/974409837?refcode=2023-09-04-labor-day&recurring=&amount=6&amounts=10,30,50,100,200&refcode2=ebea3144-414b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84MzU0NSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYmVhMzE0NC00MTRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=wtkv1_mh6QpX-6UTn_sd1Qf1RDY0YaZI_YGx8K8SFmg=)** As more people realize the incredible role workers can play in shaping their own experience as employees, we are seeing a resurgence of unions! Even at large corporations, workers are coming together to stand up for their rights and make their workplaces better. **I'm running for Congress because our workforce deserves livable wages and workplace protections. I'm committed to doing everything in my power to fight for them.** In solidarity, _Lynn_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:

clean_body: Unions have given us some of the fundamental benefits we take for granted. | | | | Happy Labor Day, Peter! Today, we celebrate the incredible achievements of the American labor movement and renew our resolve for the challenges ahead. When workers come together, they are a powerful force for change. Unions have given us some of the fundamental benefits we take for granted, like the establishment of a forty-hour workweek, normalizing weekends off, and ensuring the safety net of worker's compensation. They've also been instrumental in increasing transparency and equity to help close racial and gender pay gaps. I will always stand in solidarity with our unions — and this year, that meant backing our nurses at the picket line and showing support for UPS workers. = As more people realize the incredible role workers can play in shaping their own experience as employees, we are seeing a resurgence of unions! Even at large corporations, workers are coming together to stand up for their rights and make their workplaces better. I'm running for Congress because our workforce deserves livable wages and workplace protections. I'm committed to doing everything in my power to fight for them. In solidarity, Lynn | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Lynn Peterson for Congress |

This Labor Day, and every day, Rep. Gabe Vasquez stands with New Mexico workers and unions. Here are just some of the pro-labor stances Gabe has taken in Congress this year:

🤝 Co-sponsored the PRO Act to defend every American’s **right to organize.**

✊🏽 Co-sponsored the Paycheck Fairness Act to **protect workers from wage discrimination.**

👷 Introduced the Energy Workers Health Improvement and Compensation Fund Act to require Big Oil Exec’s to **compensate medical costs for energy workers and their families.**

In addition to championing these bills, Gabe is calling on Congress to raise the minimum wage to $15/hour, cut taxes for working families, and repeal Trump’s tax plan that allows billionaires to pay less than the working class.

There’s a reason New Mexico unions overwhelmingly endorsed Gabe’s campaign in 2022! Gabe is on #TeamLabor 🤝 and we hope you are, too!

In solidarity,

Team Vasquez [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69186765/425375411/572230515?source=GVFC-090123-FR-EM-NAT&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MTdmOTI1YS00MzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lH8jLGCz8SF8cb0k1c-wFtYTyHMhi_IBROEUY2hKVdo=)

name: Eric Swalwell
subject: Take a break!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-sept2023-FR-september?refcode=headerfooter-header&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-sept2023-FR-september?refcode=headerfooter-header&amount=25]
Happy Labor Day, team!

A five-day work week, eight-hour work days, safety mandates, a minimum wage, vacation time — the list goes on. The Labor Day holiday commemorates these rights and more, so it's important we take time to honor the achievements fought for by the trailblazers of the labor movement.

While none of this would have been possible without their movement, we know the hard fought battle is still not over.

We must continue to demand equal pay for equal work. We must continue to stand by workplace protections for our LGBTQ+ neighbors. We must continue to fight for a living wage. And we must continue to protect workers’ right to organize.

So, friend, let us take today to show our gratitude for the courageous efforts of the labor movement, while pledging to never stop the fight for better working conditions for all.

— Eric Swalwell

So, friend, let us take today to show our gratitude for the courageous efforts of the labor movement, while pledging to never stop the fight for better working conditions for all. — Eric Swalwell DONATE TO ERIC >> [] secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-sept2023-FR-september?refcode=headerfooter-footer&amount=25 [secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-sept2023-FR-september?refcode=headerfooter-footer&amount=25] Text ERIC to 43033 to get text updates! On your phone? Press here to sign up in just two clicks >> [] Democrat Eric Swalwell proudly represents California's 14th Congressional District. He needs your support in order to keep protecting what's important: our healthcare, our paychecks, our future, and our democracy. DONATE TO ERIC >> [] Eric runs a people-powered campaign that relies on grassroots supporters like you, and if he's going to win re-election, he's going to need your help. name: Sharice Davids
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
| It’s Labor Day, a day to celebrate the hard-working Americans who, within the labor movement, fought to improve the lives of millions of workers.

Benefits such as retirement plans, sick leave, set working hours, health plans, and so much more are due to the hard work of generations of Americans who would not accept anything less.

I’m proud to have helped pass the Inflation Reduction Act, legislation that has already resulted in KS-03 adding the most clean energy jobs in the nation, as well as the Chips and Science Act to improve American manufacturing.

**As your representative in Congress, standing up for working families will continue to be my top priority.**

Growing up, my mother was a union steward for the American Postal Workers Union. I know the importance of fighting for everyone to earn a livable wage and have better working conditions. I will always stand with the working families of KS-03 as we strive to create opportunity & prosperity for our communities.

Thank you again for standing with us in this fight.

Sharice

subject: PLEASE READ: We need your help
body:
All the latest news from the front lines of True the Vote
| | | September 04, 2023 | [Read Online](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/7lGnFEBV30RHklOg_mstw6N_-cQbeN93UNyrRA0hq12myX3ErthgD_STgN62j7sxZ772OWnzNhXsWWqFLcgw49Mwl5MySjZToXC_XpebaN72RMM-VddLpc1c3bsSc79sB_S3t8awNPyMBjfRH3iDf3tQfBtwZn8AnRklKhchU6nsGUEMF8cuCkYF0IMjD6I1FgAQdB431AQnwNGNmnKYwR2kxQiQ1Do4ctPKht_ugJ0xhLz712LVI7h9UON8FJdWi8eo21cvxoXH5rSxqoTOooaGp_tpRhfouQKH9Cu_Fr8hfiQOidWIOz3MvCuhen-PggxiGfIzytHHR2byLITV_1zVjMAizURLPZpW5dUsYAUzaxx3LNFCwQXCp93sHx4AgnDaUcDFKO5dOyWRFKrbv58vJNwl0fIMc-G1pzntzKCAsNXqFvcDHqX9HJXHMDUYrTHD-2KyISoD_vKdPGay5Sduak22__RvYjmcknzp8Y8x-LSRn3IX4MwWYrxZF4VNND79iFPZhCuYSQ0OWlXwD7czUTV-DIQhGgIcwXLBAkWxXiss4MDzL-0DHZWP2WPj/3zb/9_PdutlRSxu_5LZK3HpY4w/h0/7wPR5Y9kkrGhq48UJ2RNpet74Xboco2CsrmC2kYVN0U)
---
|
# PLEASE READ: We need your help
---
| | | |
---
Hi Friend -
I hope this week's update finds you enjoying some well-deserved rest and relaxation on Labor Day 2023. Interestingly, Labor Day first became a national holiday in 1894. It began as a Union-driven agenda of planned protests to end capitalism and adopt socialism ... but that’s not what America wanted. So when you celebrate Labor Day, remember what it symbolizes - one more failed effort to abridge our freedoms.
Our history is littered with repeated attempts to destroy this experiment in liberty that we're blessed to call home. Yet, God's divine providence continues to guide us, to redeem us, time and time again, through eventful acts of those He calls to His purpose.
It is with that sentiment in mind that I come to you today. I wonder if this is how it feels for soldiers to write to their loved ones. I never had the honor of serving in the military and certainly don't mean to equate our challenges with those faced in combat. So, please hear my heart through my words. My experiences are all I know, but I think they are worth sharing. I'm not particularly eager to write about any of this, but we've reached a stage where I must, at least somewhat, try to describe the situation we find ourselves in.
I started True the Vote in 2009 and was immediately targeted for takedown by political apparatchiks who derive their power through control and therefore fear honest elections. The attacks against us have never stopped.
Prior to founding True the Vote, I’d run companies in the private sector for over 20 years, never been in a lawsuit. Within weeks of starting True the Vote, we were sued twice by institutional leftist organizations in an attempt to stop the rising threat we represented. >From 2010 through 2013, at the hands of the Obama administration, I fought twenty-three separate audits and investigations from five different federal government agencies, one of which was the IRS. We struck back, sued the IRS, and it took ten years in court, but we won. If you want to learn more, search online for “ _Catherine Engelbrecht”_ and _“Congressional testimony 2014”_. I reference it here only because some of you didn't know of True the Vote before 2020. We’ve fought for election integrity for over fourteen years. We've got a deep history. We bear lots of scars. I know the battlefield very well - and it has never been as dangerous as it is today.
Nary a day goes by without some kind of attack. Libelous news stories. Calls, emails, and social media threats. Property vandalized (happened again last week). False reports sent to banks and various government agencies and the outfall of those reports. False imprisonment. Everpresent personal security concerns.
Then there’s the lawfare. We are defendants in three lawsuits in Georgia. One involving the movie ‘2000 Mules’, one filed by the Georgia State Election Board, and one filed by Stacy Abrams, Marc Elias, and Fair Fight to stop us from assisting citizens in keeping voter rolls clean. We've also provided legal defense to citizens who spoke out and became targets. Lawfare is not about justice, it is tactic to drain time and resources and erode Constitutional rights. Our adversaries are extremely well funded. [We raise our funds right here, one dollar at a time.](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/qOpxPyqH3iJyXA2JSR62HfjZt-BPNqgA3ijwg_5Jj_OlHnCTnA95yf3sUe8HbeyMcSJ-6s4zE6j7cXPO7LStuMPWP9ysirak2NstXCAaOEeFCApHadEg3osZTOLgU4YSnFaMAb7c23POcdPhOM8xK4FKhGR2GFyAAWgLbiHibQ-26yIF-ziEBojG46xl7NxS/3zb/9_PdutlRSxu_5LZK3HpY4w/h1/vUnEuYpsmcZ4MIx2eZAte-HrbQSkQexE6OU7y_FJ9bc)
There's so much more I could write about the depths to which the Uniparty has gone to destroy us, but you get the picture. True the Vote still stands today only by God's grace, delivered in part through your continued support.
Now, we are preparing for 2024, focused on three non-negotiable goals:
1. Election processes must follow the law.
2. Voter rolls must be accurate.
3. Citizens, sheriffs, and legislators must be equipped to participate.

Our plans to deliver on each goal are extensive, proactive, and could easily be outcome-determinative
Which brings me back to this feeling of a soldier writing home. [We need your help.](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/qOpxPyqH3iJyXA2JSR62HfjZt-BPNqgA3ijwg_5Jj_OlHnCTnA95yf3sUe8HbeyMcSJ-6s4zE6j7cXPO7LStuMPWP9ysirak2NstXCAaOEeFCApHadEg3osZTOLgU4YSnFaMAb7c23POcdPhOM8xK4FKhGR2GFyAAWgLbiHibQ-26yIF-ziEBojG46xl7NxS/3zb/9_PdutlRSxu_5LZK3HpY4w/h2/dmi1nIaiZLarhYeQbGbN8_Az7DqvmNar-FdELGI7LEc) It is essential to the liberty movement that True the Vote stay in the fight, but **we are taking massive incoming.** We are spread thin, triaging the legal and research needs of others, being strategic stewards of every dime, and we are doing it all prayerfully, believing in the call that is True the Vote. PS - About a month ago, the Wall Street Journal ran a shockingly biased editorial accusing us of all manner of nonsense. One of our supporters, Larry MacIntyre, wrote a response to WSJ. Thank you Mr. MacIntyre! [Here was the WSJ hit piece](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/Qvk83lMrxpyahZ2UDrJlsW72Or6tXWgKQzJoAXjGOrZzZCpr-JaeUCqFJe3VOWTRFmZeqOPswu1g88_0XFTMsPH6ik84PlDt-ewBX5Xxzgfnjy5nUXxB_Og-dIAN8j3gRQRGQItutXxkMvuUzGZmXLjHDX7FIXEDLt6wzmIj5WkzIE4sNK3xtYAAdhLkJNApWUMz86PCr0vJnQiMMr7P53v6frdA-jGhJ99My6z_t1XrKBGGUkn6C3chmSgAX2009lImUXdX6EE5YsvDssYxUf2GoPP25ABGFbq6top7lWTjTQrmKKOZHilK7XqMnFYZMtpA9clBR9dMRsBjm923_w/3zb/9_PdutlRSxu_5LZK3HpY4w/h5/i-QyGMa2-ibVWcPndHOcNnEIx1uCgKW6eLHf35ofdqs). Here's Mr. MacIntyre's bullseye response:
| | _To: Editor, Wall Street Journal_ _From: Lawrence MacIntyre, long-time subscriber_ _I know you won’t publish this, but at the very least I hope it will be referred to the writer who produced the July 23 editorial titled “2000 Mules, But no Evidence.”_ _As a long-retired daily newspaper editorial writer, I was very disappointed by what appears to be a deliberate attempt to mislead your readers. You lead off the piece by asking, “why is True the Vote refusing to work with law enforcement?”_ _If true, that would be a good question. But it is false, and I’m pretty sure the writer of this piece did enough research beforehand to know it was misleading. Correspondence documenting all that has transpired between True the Vote and the GBI can be readily found on the internet.__In March 2021 True the Vote founder Catherine Engelbrecht and her business partner Gregg Phillips provided their geo-tracking data to the FBI showing that 279 cell phones had made multiple trips to Atlanta-area drop-boxes. A month later, they met with officials of the Georgia Bureau of Investigation and provided them with the same data on a promise of confidentiality.__Five months later, they received a letter from GBI Director D. Victor Reynolds stating that while their data was “curious,” it did not rise to the level of probable cause.__Reynolds also released his full letter to the Atlanta Constitution, thus violating the confidentiality agreement.__Three weeks later, Engelbrecht’s attorney, James Bopp Jr., sent Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp a letter advising him True the Vote would no longer cooperate with the GBI because it had shown it could not be trusted to maintain confidentiality.__Bopp told the governor that Reynolds’s letter distributed to the media revealed “information that was provided confidentially to the Federal Bureau of Investigation. It should never have been taken from the FBI files and publicly released....This action will have reverberations that reach far beyond Georgia.”_ _So, to answer your opening question, that certainly appears to be a good reason not to work with law enforcement in Georgia.__And finally, your editorial repeats the GBI’s deliberately misleading attempt to demean the accuracy of the geo-tracking methods used by True the Vote to identify drop-box mules. The GBI letter declared it could not investigate people who made “multiple trips to within 100 feet of a voter drop box.”_ _Anyone who uses their smart phone as a navigation device in their car knows that today’s phones can be tracked much more accurately than 100 feet. My cell phone can even tell me which lane I’m in and precisely how many feet I am from the next intersection. So, it’s not too hard to believe that the cell phones carried by the mules came pretty darn close to those voter drop boxes.__True the Vote and Catherine Engelbrecht did not deserve such a cheap shot at their credibility.__Thank you for your attention.__Sincerely_ _Lawrence B. MacIntyre_ _Greenwood, IN_

clean_body: All the latest news from the front lines of True the Vote | | | September 04, 2023 | | PLEASE READ: We need your help | | | | Hi Friend - I hope this week's update finds you enjoying some well-deserved rest and relaxation on Labor Day 2023. Interestingly, Labor Day first became a national holiday in 1894. It began as a Union-driven agenda of planned protests to end capitalism and adopt socialism ... but that’s not what America wanted. So when you celebrate Labor Day, remember what it symbolizes - one more failed effort to abridge our freedoms. Our history is littered with repeated attempts to destroy this experiment in liberty that we're blessed to call home. Yet, God's divine providence continues to guide us, to redeem us, time and time again, through eventful acts of those He calls to His purpose. It is with that sentiment in mind that I come to you today. I wonder if this is how it feels for soldiers to write to their loved ones. I never had the honor of serving in the military and certainly don't mean to equate our challenges with those faced in combat. So, please hear my heart through my words. My experiences are all I know, but I think they are worth sharing. I'm not particularly eager to write about any of this, but we've reached a stage where I must, at least somewhat, try to describe the situation we find ourselves in. I started True the Vote in 2009 and was immediately targeted for takedown by political apparatchiks who derive their power through control and therefore fear honest elections. The attacks against us have never stopped. Prior to founding True the Vote, I’d run companies in the private sector for over 20 years, never been in a lawsuit. Within weeks of starting True the Vote, we were sued twice by institutional leftist organizations in an attempt to stop the rising threat we represented. >From 2010 through 2013, at the hands of the Obama administration, I fought twenty-three separate audits and investigations from five different federal government agencies, one of which was the IRS. We struck back, sued the IRS, and it took ten years in court, but we won. If you want to learn more, search online for “ Catherine Engelbrecht” and “Congressional testimony 2014”. I reference it here only because some of you didn't know of True the Vote before 2020. We’ve fought for election integrity for over fourteen years. We've got a deep history. We bear lots of scars. I know the battlefield very well - and it has never been as dangerous as it is today. Nary a day goes by without some kind of attack. Libelous news stories. Calls, emails, and social media threats. Property vandalized (happened again last week). False reports sent to banks and various government agencies and the outfall of those reports. False imprisonment. Everpresent personal security concerns. Then there’s the lawfare. We are defendants in three lawsuits in Georgia. One involving the movie ‘2000 Mules’, one filed by the Georgia State Election Board, and one filed by Stacy Abrams, Marc Elias, and Fair Fight to stop us from assisting citizens in keeping voter rolls clean. We've also provided legal defense to citizens who spoke out and became targets. Lawfare is not about justice, it is tactic to drain time and resources and erode Constitutional rights. Our adversaries are extremely well funded. There's so much more I could write about the depths to which the Uniparty has gone to destroy us, but you get the picture. True the Vote still stands today only by God's grace, delivered in part through your continued support. Now, we are preparing for 2024, focused on three non-negotiable goals: 1. Election processes must follow the law. 2. Voter rolls must be accurate. 3. Citizens, sheriffs, and legislators must be equipped to participate. Our plans to deliver on each goal are extensive, proactive, and could easily be outcome-determinative Which brings me back to this feeling of a soldier writing home. It is essential to the liberty movement that True the Vote stay in the fight, but we are taking massive incoming. We are spread thin, triaging the legal and research needs of others, being strategic stewards of every dime, and we are doing it all prayerfully, believing in the call that is True the Vote. Keep the faith. Never quit. Ever onward- | PS - About a month ago, the Wall Street Journal ran a shockingly biased editorial accusing us of all manner of nonsense. One of our supporters, Larry MacIntyre, wrote a response to WSJ. Thank you Mr. MacIntyre! . Here's Mr. MacIntyre's bullseye response: | | To: Editor, Wall Street Journal From: Lawrence MacIntyre, long-time subscriber I know you won’t publish this, but at the very least I hope it will be referred to the writer who produced the July 23 editorial titled “2000 Mules, But no Evidence.” As a long-retired daily newspaper editorial writer, I was very disappointed by what appears to be a deliberate attempt to mislead your readers. You lead off the piece by asking, “why is True the Vote refusing to work with law enforcement?” If true, that would be a good question. But it is false, and I’m pretty sure the writer of this piece did enough research beforehand to know it was misleading. Correspondence documenting all that has transpired between True the Vote and the GBI can be readily found on the internet.In March 2021 True the Vote founder Catherine Engelbrecht and her business partner Gregg Phillips provided their geo-tracking data to the FBI showing that 279 cell phones had made multiple trips to Atlanta-area drop-boxes. A month later, they met with officials of the Georgia Bureau of Investigation and provided them with the same data on a promise of confidentiality.Five months later, they received a letter from GBI Director D. Victor Reynolds stating that while their data was “curious,” it did not rise to the level of probable cause.Reynolds also released his full letter to the Atlanta Constitution, thus violating the confidentiality agreement.Three weeks later, Engelbrecht’s attorney, James Bopp Jr., sent Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp a letter advising him True the Vote would no longer cooperate with the GBI because it had shown it could not be trusted to maintain confidentiality.Bopp told the governor that Reynolds’s letter distributed to the media revealed “information that was provided confidentially to the Federal Bureau of Investigation. It should never have been taken from the FBI files and publicly released....This action will have reverberations that reach far beyond Georgia.” So, to answer your opening question, that certainly appears to be a good reason not to work with law enforcement in Georgia.And finally, your editorial repeats the GBI’s deliberately misleading attempt to demean the accuracy of the geo-tracking methods used by True the Vote to identify drop-box mules. The GBI letter declared it could not investigate people who made “multiple trips to within 100 feet of a voter drop box.” Anyone who uses their smart phone as a navigation device in their car knows that today’s phones can be tracked much more accurately than 100 feet. My cell phone can even tell me which lane I’m in and precisely how many feet I am from the next intersection. Let’s remember the reason we have days off work – like holidays and weekends – is because of the labor movement. [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69187249/425376960/693975070?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMGEzYTg3ZC00NDRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1JEZQNDgWcHDEV1qPfhD7pXPHTXw5pQk2dHRyyM2Yiw=&emci=85caf1a1-fb48-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=d0a3a87d-444b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=15820338)

Happy Labor Day, Peter!

Today is more than just a well-deserved day off. Let’s remember, the reason we have days off work, like holidays and weekends, is because of the labor movement.

My mom was a unionized public school teacher. I remember when she joined with her union to negotiate for increased school funding, smaller class sizes, and improved benefits – including a union-negotiated pension and health insurance, which my parents continue to rely on. She showed me – in words and deeds – the power of collective bargaining and the importance of her union.

I am so grateful for what the labor movement has accomplished for workers here in Washington State and across the country, fighting for better wages, working conditions, and benefits.

On this Labor Day, I hope you’ll join me in honoring working people and the unions who represent them, like my mom’s did. Let’s remain mindful of the role of labor unions in growing and sustaining this country’s middle class. Tomorrow, let's get back to work fighting alongside them for a better future.

Thank you,

Kim

Rep. Kim Schrier is a doctor, scientist, and mom, fighting for Washington's 8th District in Congress. This is one of the most competitive districts in the country, so we need your help to keep Kim in Congress. Chip in now to help protect Kim's seat in 2024. | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69187250/425376961/-958695453?refcode=em23_230904_Labor_enask_gq_act___DKS_ettx&utm_campaign=em23_230901_Labor_enask_gq_act___DKS_ettx&utm_medium=email&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTExMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkMGEzYTg3ZC00NDRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=1JEZQNDgWcHDEV1qPfhD7pXPHTXw5pQk2dHRyyM2Yiw=)

name: Team Chrissy
subject: A message of gratitude for working people
body:
| | |
---
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69187228/425376931/-1072566757?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NDkxMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYzRiOWQ3Ny00NDRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4r6H8ix2dSxNW9fnACtM7Ovs-6H9NLI6eiSKqfRA6QY=&emci=1f99c7dc-7647-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=3c4b9d77-444b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=21278485)
---
Derek,

Did you know that Beyoncé and Michael Keaton were born on Labor Day? That the first Labor Day celebrated in the U.S. was in New York City in 1882 – 12 years before it became a federal holiday and 31 years before there was a U.S. Department of Labor?
Labor Day celebrates those who fought for the end of mandatory 12-hour days, seven-day work weeks, and child labor – all things that were common in the late 1800s. We also recognize and honor those who are fighting today for safe and equitable working conditions.
**Chrissy is one of those leaders****. **

She’s one of the reasons those in the military and across the federal government have paid parental leave and is working hard to get the same for everyone else. When she learned that truck drivers often don’t have access to convenient bathrooms during long trips (crazy, right?), she wrote a bill to change that.

It’s a great start, but there’s so much more to be done. Chrissy is grateful you’ve stood by her side as she’s fought for worker’s rights – and she hopes you’ll continue to do so.

As always, there’s work to be done.

–Team Chrissy

This Labor Day, we’re standing with our workers. |
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69187266/425376979/-1175830425?refcode=header&amount=5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzUvMS84MDk5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYjRiOWQ3Ny00NDRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c2h3CzQacCgSBs8BRMXyAp2f2-e476yzJNMAR0EmE3k=)
---
|
Happy Labor Day!

Each year, we reserve the first Monday in September to honor and celebrate the American labor movement’s fight for fair labor practices.

**But today, as thousands of brave Californians return to the picket line, we’re reminded that the battle for workers’ rights is _far_ from over.**

This year, California has seen at least 53 labor strikes involving 275,000+ participants – _and that doesn’t include the strikes that started in 2022._

This is not some fluke, friend. A decade of unaccountability from megacorporations has led to disparities in employees’ pay and abuse of employees’ rights nationwide.

**From the writers of the WGA to the artists of SAG-AFTRA to the hospitality workers of UNITE HERE Local 11 and beyond – Californians are saying “ENOUGH” and demanding the dignity, the respect, the safety, and the compensation they deserve.**

CADEM proudly stands with workers across our state as they fight to remind corporations and studios of the truth: **California is a union state.**

That’s why we’re working hard to elect California Democrats who will fight for workers, their families, and the middle class. The future of the American labor movement starts here with us.

In solidarity,

_CADEM_
---

Peter,
Happy Labor Day!

Labor Day is more than just a day off; it is a tribute to the foundation upon which our nation's prosperity is built. The labor and ingenuity of American workers have propelled us forward, creating opportunities, driving innovation, and fostering the American dream.

In every corner of our district and across the country, individuals show up each day with a commitment to excellence. It is this commitment that has led to our nation's growth and success. I am constantly inspired by the stories of triumph, perseverance, and hard work that I hear from my constituents.

As we gather with family, friends, and loved ones, let us take a moment to express our gratitude to those who contribute to the strength of our communities. Our workers, whether in the trades, in our local businesses, or on our farms, play an essential role in shaping the fabric of our society.

In Congress, I remain dedicated to championing policies that empower workers, promote economic growth, and preserve the values that make our nation exceptional.

Together, we can ensure that the American workforce continues to thrive and lead us toward a brighter future.

Wishing you all a joyful and memorable Labor Day.
---

clean_body: | | | | \- - -| Peter, Happy Labor Day! Labor Day is more than just a day off; it is a tribute to the foundation upon which our nation's prosperity is built. The labor and ingenuity of American workers have propelled us forward, creating opportunities, driving innovation, and fostering the American dream. In every corner of our district and across the country, individuals show up each day with a commitment to excellence. It is this commitment that has led to our nation's growth and success. I am constantly inspired by the stories of triumph, perseverance, and hard work that I hear from my constituents. As we gather with family, friends, and loved ones, let us take a moment to express our gratitude to those who contribute to the strength of our communities. Our workers, whether in the trades, in our local businesses, or on our farms, play an essential role in shaping the fabric of our society. In Congress, I remain dedicated to championing policies that empower workers, promote economic growth, and preserve the values that make our nation exceptional. Together, we can ensure that the American workforce continues to thrive and lead us toward a brighter future. Wishing you all a joyful and memorable Labor Day. Lori Chavez-DeRemer Congresswoman OR-05 | | | | | | | | Even as a Congresswoman, Lori’s most important title is mom. That’s how she knows the issues your family faces, and that’s why she fights everyday to make life better for every Oregon family, and every American. Aside from that, all you need to know are the issues she’s fighting for in Congress: If that sounds like the kind of agenda you can get behind, If you’d prefer to give by check, please use the below address: Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Congress 13203 SE 172nd Ave Suite 166 #399 Happy Valley, OR 97086 Forward This message to FIVE FRIENDS AND FAMILY to spread the word on how to achieve a better future for Oregon and America → ** Also text “LORI” to 71800 or click here to receive all of Lori’s messages | | | | Lori Chavez-DeRemer 13203 SE 172nd Ave, Suite 166 #399, Happy Valley, OR 97086 = \- =

I just introduced a [CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENT](https://links.nrsc-emails.com/r?u=K115QJUsYIJk1EqYiO2oYlmsC7rvl8SLY7VhYxcVvNB8Rmoch3OR0CPPCQDkFsxDXnpbpFUCeyNkht72W-ahrZczPgLvzFB4kVzzGA00ioI_20FH7xIZm9nWVK6TBC5vkdsofaeSyeRWYQ6ypY1EYCija6J5S6D1vzPmZam-UOp0Kohz7tniEs4K9RKd0WODVHNa9ThNLVWfMeETRVwYN7DahGJVBuv-EsPDWp1bKJWdV_W-tQTRSiuLQa8zwsREtwlarR-YpCd67rhl00efSIR7xykh8ZoSL4Y6IjVXNwJl6c1z1ufqcWhjcTxTWXaWYgENVIMYiKEnL9FKrwDjKMoZWkqgKspp9dzD-eOM4v0yko7A3Uc3Xd1yWTtIecgXwS_yOn4499882dGt4ptMYExrZqO8_9-qfY7uC289WPXbTvCBkNcNJDPym8Il64qYSjNHWd-6-qeIFrVoGg76qM1amXOygoE-oIXULESTXojnJJo-EjdAFxYlIazpPh9lXbVQjcFNI_9oWPXM0sAzKAu7skCxBrCv65YSsBryySU7BSC7bK6fKyZ4k9EIu8YH2RUBSrY610ZaLQmnCfE7tQ&e=9df031a836523745eb62a73b31fdf0c5&utm_source=hdnrsc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20230213_hd_rs_term_limi_45_90&n=5) to impose [TERM LIMITS](https://links.nrsc-emails.com/r?u=K115QJUsYIJk1EqYiO2oYlmsC7rvl8SLY7VhYxcVvNB8Rmoch3OR0CPPCQDkFsxDXnpbpFUCeyNkht72W-ahrZczPgLvzFB4kVzzGA00ioI_20FH7xIZm9nWVK6TBC5vkdsofaeSyeRWYQ6ypY1EYCija6J5S6D1vzPmZam-UOp0Kohz7tniEs4K9RKd0WODVHNa9ThNLVWfMeETRVwYN7DahGJVBuv-EsPDWp1bKJWdV_W-tQTRSiuLQa8zwsREtwlarR-YpCd67rhl00efSIR7xykh8ZoSL4Y6IjVXNwJl6c1z1ufqcWhjcTxTWXaWYgENVIMYiKEnL9FKrwDjKMoZWkqgKspp9dzD-eOM4v0yko7A3Uc3Xd1yWTtIecgXwS_yOn4499882dGt4ptMYExrZqO8_9-qfY7uC289WPXbTvCBkNcNJDPym8Il64qYSjNHWd-6-qeIFrVoGg76qM1amXOygoE-oIXULESTXojnJJo-EjdAFxYlIazpPh9lXbVQjcFNI_9oWPXM0sAzKAu7skCxBrCv65YSsBryySU7BSC7bK6fKyZ4k9EIu8YH2RUBSrY610ZaLQmnCfE7tQ&e=9df031a836523745eb62a73b31fdf0c5&utm_source=hdnrsc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20230213_hd_rs_term_limi_45_90&n=6) on Senators and House Members serving in the United States Government.
Imposing TERM LIMITS is one of the only ways to fix how broken Washington has become. [Our great Founding Fathers never envisioned](https://links.nrsc-emails.com/r?u=K115QJUsYIJk1EqYiO2oYlmsC7rvl8SLY7VhYxcVvNB8Rmoch3OR0CPPCQDkFsxDXnpbpFUCeyNkht72W-ahrZczPgLvzFB4kVzzGA00ioI_20FH7xIZm9nWVK6TBC5vkdsofaeSyeRWYQ6ypY1EYCija6J5S6D1vzPmZam-UOp0Kohz7tniEs4K9RKd0WODVHNa9ThNLVWfMeETRVwYN7DahGJVBuv-EsPDWp1bKJWdV_W-tQTRSiuLQa8zwsREtwlarR-YpCd67rhl00efSIR7xykh8ZoSL4Y6IjVXNwJl6c1z1ufqcWhjcTxTWXaWYgENVIMYiKEnL9FKrwDjKMoZWkqgKspp9dzD-eOM4v0yko7A3Uc3Xd1yWTtIecgXwS_yOn4499882dGt4ptMYExrZqO8_9-qfY7uC289WPXbTvCBkNcNJDPym8Il64qYSjNHWd-6-qeIFrVoGg76qM1amXOygoE-oIXULESTXojnJJo-EjdAFxYlIazpPh9lXbVQjcFNI_9oWPXM0sAzKAu7skCxBrCv65YSsBryySU7BSC7bK6fKyZ4k9EIu8YH2RUBSrY610ZaLQmnCfE7tQ&e=9df031a836523745eb62a73b31fdf0c5&utm_source=hdnrsc&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=20230213_hd_rs_term_limi_45_90&n=7) a Federal Government that only served a small group of political insiders and special interests at the top, gaming the system while RUNNING OVER the American people.
It’s finally time we do something about the entrenched politicians in Washington. I want to know what YOU think about my Constitutional Amendment to impose TERM LIMITS on Senators and House Members.

name: Hillary Scholten
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Scholten for Congress
body:
I am proud to stand with working families and support the right to organize.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69188562/425381037/1237824078?refcode=BLU-HSC-20230904-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NTY0MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NWM4ZmI5Mi00NjRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=4o6eBdWdj2H3dVZ6bACc03snhBW2GfnCp8ETWNT-Xhg=)
---
| Happy Labor Day, all!

My mom was a public school teacher and MEA member. Among so many other things, she taught me the importance of hard work and how unions helped build the Michigan economy and establish the rights of our working families. Labor Day is a perfect opportunity to honor the legacy of the workers' rights movement in our country.

In these tough times, workers need the power of organizing more than ever. I am proud to stand with working families and support the right to organize. I've fought for working families throughout my career, and I continue that advocacy in Congress.

I hope you and your loved ones have a safe and happy Labor Day!

With endless appreciation,

clean_body: I am proud to stand with working families and support the right to organize. | = | Happy Labor Day, all! My mom was a public school teacher and MEA member. Among so many other things, she taught me the importance of hard work and how unions helped build the Michigan economy and establish the rights of our working families. Labor Day is a perfect opportunity to honor the legacy of the workers' rights movement in our country. In these tough times, workers need the power of organizing more than ever. I am proud to stand with working families and support the right to organize. I've fought for working families throughout my career, and I continue that advocacy in Congress. I hope you and your loved ones have a safe and happy Labor Day! I hope you're enjoying your Labor Day Weekend, folks. I wanted to take a minute and thank all the hard-working Americans who work so hard to keep our infrastructure and economy one of the strongest in the world. Today, we celebrate American ingenuity and the tireless work that our Founders put into building this country. Not just the Founders, but the millions of people who have contributed along the way. Thanks for all you do to keep **OUR** nation moving forward. Now go and enjoy some rest and relaxation. Sincerely, **Will Hurd** _Candidate for President & Former TX Congressman_

name: Team AOC
subject: Hot Labor Summer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
But we need to keep fighting.
#
This Labor Day, we wanted to call out some wins for the labor movement in 2023:
• According to a 2023 survey, **71% of Americans approve of labor unions — the most since 1965.** 1 A record-high 61% also say unions help rather than hurt the U.S. economy.
• More than 200 strikes have occurred across the U.S. so far in 2023, involving more than 320,000 workers, as compared to 116 strikes and just 27,000 workers in 2021.2
• **New York State Nurses Association members won a 19.1 percent wage increase** and the creation of more than 170 new nursing positions after a 3-day strike at Montefiore Medical Center in Alexandria’s district.
• The National Labor Relations Board saw **2,510 union representation petitions filed in fiscal year 2022 — a 53% increase over the previous year.** 3 The NLRB also ruled to make union-busting a lot harder: if bosses commit unfair labor practices in the run-up to a union election, the NLRB will order the employer to immediately recognize and bargain with the union.
• 86% of Teamsters voted in favor of a UPS ratification contract — **the highest vote for a contract in the history of the Teamsters at UPS** — and drivers will now earn an average of $170K/yr in pay and benefits by the end of 2028.
• 97% of the United Auto Workers voted to authorize a strike as their contract is renegotiated — **meaning nearly all of the 150,000 workers at Ford, General Motors, and Stellantis will strike if a fair deal cannot be reached.** 4
It’s been a big year for the labor movement. We have momentum to grow and strengthen unions like no time in recent history. **Team AOC is happy to stand with workers fighting for better protections in the workplace.**
[Stand with Team AOC and workers around the country today with a Unionize Your Workplace t-shirt, made by unionized garment workers in the U.S. Use code LABOR at checkout to receive 15% off.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/20728?utm_source=em20230904-4929&t=1&akid=4929%2E2526796%2E0tnhkR)
Yours in service,
Team AOC
1 - [**Gallup** : U.S. Approval of Labor Unions at Highest Point Since 1965](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/20729?t=2&akid=4929%2E2526796%2E0tnhkR)
2 - [**Cornell School of Industrial & Labor Relations**: Labor Action Tracker](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/20730?t=3&akid=4929%2E2526796%2E0tnhkR)
3 - [**National Labor Relations Board** : Election Petitions Up 53%, Board Continues to Reduce Case Processing Time in FY22](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/20731?t=4&akid=4929%2E2526796%2E0tnhkR)
4 - [**United Auto Workers** : 97% OF UAW’S BIG THREE MEMBERS VOTE YES TO AUTHORIZE STRIKE](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/20732?t=5&akid=4929%2E2526796%2E0tnhkR'S%20BIG%20THREE%20MEMBERS%20VOTE%20YES%20TO%20AUTHORIZE,Ford%2C%20General%20Motors%20and%20Stellantis.)

clean_body: But we need to keep fighting. This Labor Day, we wanted to call out some wins for the labor movement in 2023: • According to a 2023 survey, 71% of Americans approve of labor unions — the most since 1965. 1 A record-high 61% also say unions help rather than hurt the U.S. economy. • More than 200 strikes have occurred across the U.S. so far in 2023, involving more than 320,000 workers, as compared to 116 strikes and just 27,000 workers in 2021.2 • New York State Nurses Association members won a 19.1 percent wage increase and the creation of more than 170 new nursing positions after a 3-day strike at Montefiore Medical Center in Alexandria’s district. • The National Labor Relations Board saw 2,510 union representation petitions filed in fiscal year 2022 — a 53% increase over the previous year. 3 The NLRB also ruled to make union-busting a lot harder: if bosses commit unfair labor practices in the run-up to a union election, the NLRB will order the employer to immediately recognize and bargain with the union. • 86% of Teamsters voted in favor of a UPS ratification contract — the highest vote for a contract in the history of the Teamsters at UPS — and drivers will now earn an average of $170K/yr in pay and benefits by the end of 2028. • 97% of the United Auto Workers voted to authorize a strike as their contract is renegotiated — meaning nearly all of the 150,000 workers at Ford, General Motors, and Stellantis will strike if a fair deal cannot be reached. 4 It’s been a big year for the labor movement. We have momentum to grow and strengthen unions like no time in recent history. Team AOC is happy to stand with workers fighting for better protections in the workplace. Yours in service, Team AOC 1 - 2 - 3 - 4 - . | ---|--- | | ---|---|--- Email us: mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com

Happy Labor Day, team

As many of us take time today to celebrate the unofficial end of summer with friends and family, let us pause for a moment to reflect on the workers, unions, and laborers who fought, suffered, and died for entire generations of working people.

My parents were farm workers, folks. So today, I am especially grateful to honor the memory of iconic Latino civil rights leader and labor activist **César E. Chávez** who, along with **Dolores Huerta** , helped organize farm workers like my parents into the first official farm workers union.

I stand on the shoulders of their sacrifice. I would never have been able to achieve my dream of being a college graduate, a physician, and a member of Congress without them.

So as we celebrate Labor Day today, I am as committed as ever to expanding protections for working people, including critical protections for farm workers as young as 12-years-old, and to creating thousands of union jobs through the Inflation Reduction Act.

But our work is just beginning! Good working conditions and protections for unions is a never-ending struggle, and one that I hope you’ll join me in.

In Solidarity,

Dr. Raul Ruiz

| | Patriot,

---
A judge ruled that all of my court proceedings in Atlanta will be TELEVISED for the entire country to watch.

---
The Communist Democrats would love nothing more than for me to be stuck in court in a televised spectacle as a way to keep me off the campaign trail.

---
_But I refuse to play into the Left’s hands._

---
_That’s why I have waived my in-person formal arraignment and simply pleaded**“NOT GUILTY”** to the indictment in the Georgia witch hunt..._

---
[](https://link.donaldjtrump.com/u/click?_t=c26d591425684a3289b90149d613a03c&_m=298147a71c254534ad0f726d165a7ae8&_e=cIED-C0WS38jDNYyh_UVXKeqe9KSrROtw3gAnT47viY9JvZKr41UgbgWSdiQjDOfrqhbJphrJ4P1IEYBkAqq8K4I_QvXTxslekMXgsjcLNORrAxH_R8Bm91SMujfrm5oUvJv4Q4Iw9-ILS1zuiRe8TdkU7dgFodSsd74BS08RxsHwHtvfANhWSHK0o17NtgfzIqksn8Lvedq74TdlkxB6vnoaf17vWjB4_ShzEXcd76mJSBJI9kQb452pYaeC47dR6k0oEGyQjffs26NTGNp_5xnDAybYioFq-mSfwndTa79RbeB_6X7JakqA_L3dK74opTtYGPOVjmysjAUflsI4oWfoFZ6GQ0neeFQaNiTNCoxG79E6L-aB5wXPbI3J0NPpHbBd3raRDAhqHwMHGx0oA42WL4-eroWm27zUMOjeiKSM6wdQf_3yJ1qkYTSOla82EUi1ODcf-Sst0zp2JGwwObFKIL9aM3FGWwzOBftw5tUW0k1benFx1QDmUL-erB8)
---
_**Why would I fly down to Atlanta just to hear over a dozen _FALSE_ CHARGES brought against me on live TV?**_

---
Our country is hurting right now. Our border is in chaos, our dollar is losing value, our justice system is dead, our rights are being ripped from our hands, and our enemies abroad are plotting ways to exploit our weaknesses.

---
We have __serious__ matters to deal with – not televised charades orchestrated by Crooked Joe and his corrupt accomplices.

---
_I’m going to focus on the REAL ISSUES that matter to Americans. And that’s how we’re going to win back the White House in 2024 and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!_

---
_If you’re doing poorly due to the very sad state of our country right now, then don’t even think about donating!_

---

clean_body: I have WAIVED my arraignment. . . | | | | | Patriot, A judge ruled that all of my court proceedings in Atlanta will be TELEVISED for the entire country to watch. The Communist Democrats would love nothing more than for me to be stuck in court in a televised spectacle as a way to keep me off the campaign trail. But I refuse to play into the Left’s hands. That’s why I have waived my in-person formal arraignment and simply pleaded“NOT GUILTY” to the indictment in the Georgia witch hunt... Why would I fly down to Atlanta just to hear over a dozen FALSE CHARGES brought against me on live TV? Our country is hurting right now. Our border is in chaos, our dollar is losing value, our justice system is dead, our rights are being ripped from our hands, and our enemies abroad are plotting ways to exploit our weaknesses. We have serious matters to deal with – not televised charades orchestrated by Crooked Joe and his corrupt accomplices. I’m going to focus on the REAL ISSUES that matter to Americans. And that’s how we’re going to win back the White House in 2024 and MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN! If you’re doing poorly due to the very sad state of our country right now, then don’t even think about donating! | | Thank you, Donald J. subject: Help Us Get Out The Vote!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BECKY EDWARDS FOR CONGRESS
body:
We need to get after every last vote in order to help Becky win this primary. Will you forward this email to everyone in your network? | | | | |
---
Dear Friends,
We have accomplished so much in the last few months. And it is all leading to tomorrow, election day!
We need to get after every last vote in order to help Becky win this primary. **Will you forward this email to everyone in your network and encourage them to vote for Becky?**
| [Find Your Voting Location](https://beckyforutah.us14.list-manage.com/track/click?u=9cc818aa94e94ffd36a8547b3&id=438b270813&e=6484bb6dd5)
---
When you’re chatting with your family and friends, here are some important points about Becky and her candidacy:

1. Becky is the only candidate with a proven legislative track record of showing up for her constituents.
2. Becky has visited all 13 counties in CD2–showing a commitment to serving every corner of our district.
3. Becky is a tremendous listener who always took citizen feedback and incorporated it into her legislating.

We are going to win this election–with your help. **Turn in your ballot today and encourage everyone you know to vote!**
Team Becky

|
---

I hope our fight for common-sense conservative values in Maryland's 6th District will unite us! Thank you, Mariela Roca | | | | | | |

name: Dr. Cornel West
subject: This is a fight for dignity
body:
The labor movement is a living, breathing, fighting force shaping the contours of our present and future.

| | | [ ](https://www.cornelwest24.org/?e=2c4ab4fbefc09c0d9c12f8df438b31c5&utm_source=cornelwest&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=labor_day_2024&n=1)
---
My dear brothers and sisters in the struggle, As we commemorate Labor Day this year, let us not forget its origins. It was born in the crucible of toil and strife, sprouting from the sweat and sacrifice of our laboring forebears. Labor Day is not just a holiday for barbecues; it is a clarion call to honor the worker, the backbone of this American experiment! In these turbulent times, with workers stretching their spirits across picket lines and standing tall in the face of oppressive corporate machinery, we bear witness to a modern-day David and Goliath. This year, strikes erupted across the land—from sanitation workers in Tennessee to Oregon nurses to Starbucks workers to Hollywood actors and screenwriters—sounding the trumpet that enough is enough! **The labor movement, my friends, is not merely an artifact of history; it is a living, breathing, fighting force shaping the contours of our present and future.** It is incumbent upon us, the orchestra of democratic voices, to stand in unwavering solidarity with our brothers and sisters on the strike lines. They are not just fighting for a paycheck; they are fighting for dignity, for respect, for the very soul of a nation that often forgets the sanctity of its laboring class. **So this Labor Day, let us not just take a day off. Let us amplify our commitment to the labor movement, recognizing that when workers rise, we all rise! Stand strong, stand together, and embrace an unapologetic love of justice!** In solidarity and love, Dr. Cornel West
---

Build the Future.

name: Mike Thompson
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mike Thompson For Congress unsubscribe: https://support
body:
# [#]
Peter,

Happy Labor Day. Today we recognize and appreciate all the hard-working Americans who make our country what it is.

Together, we honor the progress of our nation’s labor movement, but our work is far from over. I am committed to fighting for our workers and supporting legislation that promotes a better quality of life whether it’s raising the minimum wage, improving working conditions, or expanding benefits.

Hard-working Americans are the backbone of our country — especially here in California.

As you celebrate and rest today, take a moment to thank our sisters and brothers in labor.

Wishing you a happy and safe Labor Day.

Mike Thompson
