name: Zoe Lofgren
subject: Happy Labor Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LOFGREN FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#]
This Labor Day, we recognize and celebrate all of the hard-working families that contribute to our economy and our country. All workers have the right to collectively bargain for higher wages, safer working conditions, and more benefits.

I am grateful for all the workers in our district and their contributions to our community. I am committed to fighting for the rights of our workers, that’s why my colleagues and I re-introduced the Farm Workforce Modernization Act that ensures farm workers labor needs are being met.

I will continue to fight to make sure every worker is being treated well and paid fairly at work. Thank you for joining me today in recognizing the diligence of all workers.

Wishing you a safe and happy Labor Day.

Thank you,

Zoe Lofgren
Congresswoman Zoe Lofgren is a strong fighter for California’s values in Washington. As the Chair of the California Democratic Congressional Delegation, she has been on the front lines of expanding and protecting the Democratic Majority. Zoe has dedicated her career to fighting for our diverse communities and needs your help. name: Joe Kerr
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joe Kerr for Congress
body:
---
| | Peter, Happy Labor Day! Today’s holiday is about honoring the ongoing labor movement, our nation’s working people, and the many leaders who have gotten us to where we are today.

I served as Fire Captain with the Orange County Fire Authority for over thirty years. I’m a proud fourth generation union member and longtime labor leader because I know that unions make us strong.

[Will you join me in supporting union candidates by adding your name today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201094/425392068/-184274715?refcode=JK_EM_PT_WL_230904_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODgwOThhZmQtNGY0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hwFRQsFUOL_KTY2MRI2W-lAwhyUxg7pipvSBeE4uTv8=&emci=85098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=88098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=8978519) | | **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201095/425392069/-184274715?refcode=JK_EM_PT_WL_230904_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODgwOThhZmQtNGY0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hwFRQsFUOL_KTY2MRI2W-lAwhyUxg7pipvSBeE4uTv8=&emci=85098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=88098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=8978519)**
---

29 years ago, I was asked to lead a new union of Orange County firefighters in response to the County bankruptcy of 1994. I served as their President for 17 years, Vice President of the OC Labor Federation (AFL-CIO) for 17 years and VP of the California Professional Firefighters for 5 years. I've been a teamster, district representative, and business agent. In all of these roles, I had the honor of a lifetime to fight alongside my peers for fair contracts and wages, good working conditions, healthy benefits, dignity, equal opportunity, and to advance working policies for working people.

As our campaign progresses, I’m proud to have earned support from labor organizations like the Orange County Employees Association, the International Association of Fire Fighters, and the Orange County Labor Federation (OCLF):
_"We are excited to recommend Joe Kerr for Congress in California's 40th District. Joe is a proven labor champion who will advance the fight for working families in Orange County. As the Executive Director of the Orange County Labor Federation and on behalf of more than 97 local unions and over 270,000 working men and women, I strongly encourage you to support Joe and his campaign to flip CA-40."_
**– Gloria Alvarado, OCLF Executive Director**
I’m ready to continue my fight for our unions in Congress to ensure that our workers are treated with the dignity and respect they deserve.

[Sign on now to support union candidates:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201096/425392070/-184274715?refcode=JK_EM_PT_WL_230904_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODgwOThhZmQtNGY0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hwFRQsFUOL_KTY2MRI2W-lAwhyUxg7pipvSBeE4uTv8=&emci=85098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=88098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=8978519) | **[SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201097/425392071/-184274715?refcode=JK_EM_PT_WL_230904_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc4NjEiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiODgwOThhZmQtNGY0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hwFRQsFUOL_KTY2MRI2W-lAwhyUxg7pipvSBeE4uTv8=&emci=85098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&emdi=88098afd-4f4b-ee11-a3f1-00224832eb73&ceid=8978519)**
---

I hope you enjoy the holiday with your friends and family!

Joe

name: Greta for AG HQ
subject: Straight from the Associated Press
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greta Martin for AG
body:
Fitch said it herself: she doesn’t support a fair labor division in Mississippi

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201130/425392080/-456841951?refcode=BEB_EM_FR_230904LD_logo&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQzNDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWZlNDM5MDQtNTA0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rYqYTHbZ2wXE1W5p8n20zppAo38T6zKoXU8eTEqEpTY=) [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201131/425392081/-456841951?refcode=BEB_EM_FR_230904LD_G&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQzNDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWZlNDM5MDQtNTA0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rYqYTHbZ2wXE1W5p8n20zppAo38T6zKoXU8eTEqEpTY=) Peter, what a heck of a way to celebrate Labor Day.

[If you’re already familiar with how Lynn Fitch has harmed workers in Mississippi and beyond, you can contribute to Greta’s campaign to restore the Attorney General’s office to the people here:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201132/425392082/-456841951?refcode=BEB_EM_FR_230904LD_l1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQzNDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWZlNDM5MDQtNTA0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rYqYTHbZ2wXE1W5p8n20zppAo38T6zKoXU8eTEqEpTY=) [CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201133/425392083/-456841951?refcode=BEB_EM_FR_230904LD_b1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQzNDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWZlNDM5MDQtNTA0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rYqYTHbZ2wXE1W5p8n20zppAo38T6zKoXU8eTEqEpTY=) As Greta went to bat for labor rights talking about her plan to establish a Division of Fair Labor, focusing on issues such as child labor, equal pay, wage theft and discrimination, and family medical leave, Mississippi’s current Attorney General used the opportunity to double down on why she thinks things are just fine the way they are.

After a teen was tragically killed as a result of our atrocious labor conditions, you would think our leaders might show a _bit_ more compassion for the situation at hand. But that’s just not Lynn Fitch’s style.

Instead, Fitch used the opportunity to slam the Biden administration and defend the status quo that’s led to preventable worker deaths. _**It’s time we send the message loud and clear that Mississippi’s workers aren’t pawns in Lynn Fitch’s political chess game.**_

Greta’s running to restore our attorney general’s office to the priorities it should have front and center – protecting everyday people and upholding our state laws. But Fitch has big money backers working to keep her in office, so it takes every single one of us fighting back.

[Can we count on you to stand with Greta as the labor champion we need in our AG’s office today by chipping in $10, $25, or whatever you can today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201134/425392084/-456841951?refcode=BEB_EM_FR_230904LD_l2&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQzNDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWZlNDM5MDQtNTA0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rYqYTHbZ2wXE1W5p8n20zppAo38T6zKoXU8eTEqEpTY=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | **[$5 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69201135/425392085/-456841951?refcode=BEB_EM_FR_230904LD_EL5&express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode2=EL5&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQzNDMiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWZlNDM5MDQtNTA0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=rYqYTHbZ2wXE1W5p8n20zppAo38T6zKoXU8eTEqEpTY=)**

| This Labor Day, I want to honor the hard-working union members I work with every day.

Today, I'm thinking of our hard-working firefighters, dispatchers, first responders, police officers, state patrol, and support teams fighting our devastating wildfires.

As commissioner of public lands, I couldn't be more honored to work alongside the hard-working union members who keep our state safe.

Washington has long been a leader in the Labor movement, serving as a blueprint for workers' rights and job creation. I'm fighting to continue this legacy because I know we can overcome our greatest challenges when we work together to uplift one another.

Happy Labor Day,

Commissioner Hilary Franz
---

name: Cori Bush
subject: Worker safety during heat waves:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
body:
This Labor Day, I’m pushing for workers’ rights and safety in response to the deadly heat waves ravaging our country. | |
---
Peter, This Labor Day, I’m pushing for workers’ rights and safety in response to the deadly heat waves ravaging our country. As climate disasters become increasingly common, workers frequently find themselves being placed in dangerous and unsafe positions -- without health and safety protections. This summer, two farmworkers died from working in extreme heat.[1] And just last week a UPS driver in Texas collapsed and died while making deliveries in the heat. Their truck was not air conditioned.[2] This is unacceptable. **The safety of our workers, especially our most marginalized workers, should always be our top priority.** For people who work outside, it’s essential to have access to cooling centers and drinking water. [**Will you join my call for Congress to establish life-saving cooling centers and drinking water facilities for at-risk populations and workers -- including farmworkers, construction workers, and delivery and postal workers?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnH0lZmHQyE4AEcMqGVhMRK3YuobwG6xKTZuLrgKFqRmAnVn_ibn4LAtXZ3jUeT9Jg0--tz7QVt9OPLTXzcNnME7nBiZmr7hQzFpDqc6uLl7pM4TegriqyCMqUsY5aXnyBpzTF7dGjFmd2I7sx8nrBrwuAm6fscylQKvhIM9mEyV62P49EsfCCsi2MkI5jO9eLFL7Rp_i3JYVKNOs8oF3Qx_TcGVg44TDyp1-m-yKR5ZETK2YtyMC2IwDVZCGChHoqly5vhwM8eLhU4_hl5MU99/3zb/DAIkvlniTq6xt15oX_nubQ/h0/AbBqvjhajAOygRGgDHEilEyir-AA0B2haNkEO-coyU0)**** **[SIGN ON](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnH0lZmHQyE4AEcMqGVhMRKYVMareuWi2xl2D1t0vAfpbULBC0LdV5oc7jE1XrqBwzKrXpIkpxaOWcaDu3kcanLuZV-Vihdca2I1OajuKCVar8E2gTMonFWrCUa8KL7nID_coWFKcWXgxGzk17aH5dGkO64HYHmE_7HecRF7DNWF2iFXVWf-FhaddZjCmHsqgpOvcLJzW2jD5jTeMhjLbYnuBHJvIja2sxMCJq-zvFrMqGH0JwCUnwOty8NFsDlLYYm1Ea3RlknAmgHyDChOREF/3zb/DAIkvlniTq6xt15oX_nubQ/h1/uEUXG3WxnIRHMKT3xZCks46e8jQ00AFJhlwnx5-P75M) ** Across the country, there have been accounts of forced work during climate disasters. In 2021, Amazon forced workers to work through tornadoes, killing some of my constituents. Following that tragedy, I helped launch an investigation into Amazon’s labor practices and introduced bills like the _Worker Safety in Climate Disaster Act_ , which would prevent workers from being fired for seeking shelter during extreme weather and offer paid time off for workers affected by disasters. **As we push for better worker safety standards, we need immediate action to save lives.** [**In response to the deadly heat waves, please join me this Labor Day to urge Congress to expand accessible cooling centers and drinking water facilities for workers.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnnH0lZmHQyE4AEcMqGVhMRKVXaG09xV2R9aYIg8gMYKchlDxlzQRa6PduAQHsvtiqWg6QMGHJaBLTV0LPvBFuVn_g4idd18ewkgdl9W0qvwySqHKnlysuPkd8ZmOrCdYbIUKw6sXWGfkR22LjVXoWXkQ6t7ungYYL11oAan96f0iThz6XHr9D5chEjpla2GjCRVruhwSAJqVn8cjjYD5zVOo9ctkskpbajiGit1N2yNzDoLYPwUfOdnr5ppQ2dOU4rCOnCCk7vM6R8SCvScvNrm/3zb/DAIkvlniTq6xt15oX_nubQ/h2/kumHbQ2vi7-IUBz-vwX8x9__Ezkk6faDi4xzmOa2vcA)**** Thank you for taking action. As a former low-wage worker myself, I’m honored to stand with low-wage workers as we push for the dignity and safety that regular, everyday people deserve. With love, Cori **[SUPPORT OUR MOVEMENT](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953zRz8uJg6mCFXRXLh9JJShwL9aaCa9zzNwD9Fg-eL4QOCWadblnrw6r_pk0wP2kPP8qbxTTvlCqMqhS5h5tAKUDCFk6fZPEigE6Q3IbgDdJwKELNiJRJ1l1phbzkTJUKDhpRqFgCNW3lFJbJRo33LI8dYWJMU6m6N6j3p7Szi5fw2WXDf5RgXkjByVbSij5Vx44-1vh8h-U-YihRlN89R78KB2m4pZHxUtPcDWtNad9CcPS9eYh1E6ddIfPosrH8Q8ZF5UfavYLi8CUrLp4FlgIPXRiaFKyfttzAXh4Ru6hVB8VW--2qL9UdSzT_BGg8lYn_7YmXy5ubBxAv7as-9DrqddvF4UzsrXUU0QmK-oCNw/3zb/DAIkvlniTq6xt15oX_nubQ/h3/zugYks3mI5B1mzAFXrNfyY5pIJWBpbqt9uSS62lCscU) ** [1] [South Florida mourns death of another farmworker as advocates fight for heat protections](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDEvBrroImTsrPwD3k3T4tQ5qUNYCrGWCOkBflxJgbq5L4Bfdnx5TMXSt4h5c22JG5VmhBvAR-zLFBCCZGxsvPSwld-w719UTSnhqIgOP02HmPLFRR0-3NF1v3IIJD-RaWoz5oU-srZXWd3s-LT-efUDHvHx0REtPc7GVUPhIrygkkIzqmJOV0KJMSZs3ONxNlM6ncvBek4DWx1QXQYEHSSvL-jbmhdrBEYxyDRUPXzEjfEik3sJJUJekRoBz7_vNV595MYnzO6Y4Im3MrCkGKS-f7wYYFpCf7T3myAdJMXasxzsMlssC5ZEy9u1Uq00toYvdizAomDvJ3xi_5A6T_B3Ygv2jy9NQBG2gbLG-vdP4AmZqALcRiZz4wib6MqMwXg/3zb/DAIkvlniTq6xt15oX_nubQ/h4/a6gdH6PaLhCYH2NekMHllahyqzXXiPKhlQc6JgxtrPM)
[2] [A 57-year-old UPS driver in Texas died after collapsing in the heat while making deliveries](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDHOgpLvNruWjaZjaaDvcKGwVRO16FtxlgIB9QwrwIyr3gHHxKflF7qt0pd06zSiE8PFG8cSDWQnqLOnx99Ryqlu6-HOutWiSSGbiGFDPCwZ4VEhgIVXD8CsF5KXcQTttEX2U1SGno6YbcGjhKVgdnax5h8KwgWhLQJavy52JKYs8BMuNYf5CaSiaLmOEAjzd8y5LfnqUb1j6pEi1z1R25Pbi67ewtdu8p2EzmgRHutNS5rub4rm-9VkM5lWmRlqbHOxHM-N1xynB2_eRv5owjwvL_OKDQG0roOsezvCNd3SIJGP0IMAWbmLezNvnNpNtgH8Kt4JmS8lmXjskw9Le8g2kZ9SUmj9QXjjYxmh84MoQ/3zb/DAIkvlniTq6xt15oX_nubQ/h5/racXV3c9sds7QNyJZtICcqNpY0Dp8K9hfIH4kKDKFgc)

subject: wishing you a happy Labor Day!

# [#]
Peter,

Few organizations understand community and democracy as well as labor unions. Working as an intern for my local AFL-CIO chapter in college and as the volunteer director for the United Democrats of Illinois coordinated campaign in 1996, I know that the hard work, organization, and strength of labor unions gets things done and keeps this nation moving. Today we celebrate and honor their work.

From fair and safe working conditions to overtime pay, the work of organized labor has helped make the American dream more accessible to so many more Americans and for this, we owe organized labor our gratitude and solidarity.

Wishing you a happy and safe Labor Day,

Liz
[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwg-fr_footer] MEET LIZ [https://www.lizforny.com][https://secure.ngpvan.com/g_p2NY9XEEy8HMoak6V-mg2] VOLUNTEER [https://secure.ngpvan.com/g_p2NY9XEEy8HMoak6V-mg2]
Liz Gereghty is a community leader, small businesswoman, and mom with a lifelong commitment to public service. Liz served on her local school board overseeing over a $100 million budget, working to improve school facilities and finding common ground to get things done for our kids. Liz is running for Congress to make the Hudson Valley more affordable, help small businesses thrive, and always fight for women’s rights.
PAID FOR BY LIZ FOR NY
PO BOX 733
GOLDENS BRIDGE, NY 10526

Electing state Democrats who will work alongside Democratic governors to secure fundamental freedoms in their states is our best path forward.
We gained trifectas in Michigan, Minnesota, Massachusetts, and Maryland last cycle, but our work is far from over. We’re going to need a surge of grassroots support if we want to gain ground in Republican-led states. Can we count on you to own a piece of this fight?

# [The Past, Present, and Future of Labor Unrest](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=136698084&utm_source=post-email-title&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM2Njk4MDg0LCJpYXQiOjE2OTM4NTQwNDksImV4cCI6MTY5NjQ0NjA0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ESoUJ_GAhOI2VjoNMEbMSm1IRYuEqrp0eh2k712EOVQ)
### Might rising inequality, worker shortages, and artificial intelligence be creating fertile ground for more frequent strikes?
| | [Vikram Mansharamani](https://substack.com/redirect/bc9b50a1-6b38-4f54-9c8c-c1ed1f943561?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=136698084&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM2Njk4MDg0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY5Mzg1NDA0OSwiZXhwIjoxNjk2NDQ2MDQ5LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTY2MTk5OSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.5t1a0nVgjsqr92-SVde3atlXA_Ds4Zw_WLqjxwTyYjs&utm_medium=email)

| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=136698084&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM2Njk4MDg0LCJpYXQiOjE2OTM4NTQwNDksImV4cCI6MTY5NjQ0NjA0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ESoUJ_GAhOI2VjoNMEbMSm1IRYuEqrp0eh2k712EOVQ&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1661999&post_id=136698084&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM2Njk4MDg0LCJpYXQiOjE2OTM4NTQwNDksImV4cCI6MTY5NjQ0NjA0OSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE2NjE5OTkiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ESoUJ_GAhOI2VjoNMEbMSm1IRYuEqrp0eh2k712EOVQ)

| | [](https://substack..1CgdA9JEXzCMkKwfuOJS_ytHgq42wxczukASkuG1FBY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/mansharamani/p/the-past-present-and-future-of-labor?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store)
---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/24fbef6f-5e6f-4669-9559-2a391078f77b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
As many of us fire up the grills for one last hurrah, it's worth pausing for a minute to think about the purpose of Labor Day, our federal US holiday to celebrate the social and economic achievements of American workers. According to the [US Department of Labor](https://substack.com/redirect/00fe921a-0e00-4aff-987c-6bee0d6e57ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), "The holiday is rooted in the late nineteenth century, when labor activists pushed for a federal holiday to recognize the many contributions workers have made to America's strength, prosperity, and well-being." The holiday is also a useful catalyst to reflect on the history of and outlook for labor unrest.
While there are too many strikes in the United States to review, I want to highlight three big ones: The Pullman Strike, which led to the recognition of Labor Day as a federal holiday; the Coal Strike of 1902, which led to the US government playing the role of mediator; and the Air Traffic Controllers strike of 1981, which led to a multi-decade structural decline in the role of unions in America.
[Labor Day was designated a national holiday by President Grover Cleveland amidst the Pullman strike of 1894](https://substack.com/redirect/97f56232-55b8-4b5c-a64c-15f82d8ea35e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which some have called the "[greatest strike in American history](https://substack.com/redirect/af04875f-2ce3-4af3-b16c-523aee0f3177?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." By the end of the three week strike, riots had spread nationally, federal troops were mobilized, workers lost more than $1 million in wages ([~ $35 million in today's dollars](https://substack.com/redirect/aef0b990-e5ba-4c62-bf7a-48c98995120f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)), damage exceeded $340,000 ([~ $12 million in today's dollars](https://substack.com/redirect/e389ee00-97a7-4d09-bb34-eb1c3463ba27?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)), railroads missed out on millions in revenues, and more than 40 died in clashes.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NWpByDP9y3W2qVlBbXLi6nfvatqrFfJW3OwFhLAif_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=136698084)
The federal government's role in labor dispute mediation really began with the [Coal Strike of 1902, in which anthracite coal miners organized by the United Mine Workers Union walked off the job](https://substack.com/redirect/0e005ff8-ae58-4826-9473-91e18a0b1f40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). President Teddy Roosevelt tried to bring both sides together while acting as a neutral mediator, but failed. It took JP Morgan and other businesspeople to secure an end to the 163 days strike. The miners secured a 10% pay increase (they had requested 20%) and 9 hour workday (down from 10, but not the 8 they sought).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5d72e792-6125-48fc-bfff-fc06a65f5037?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Air-traffic controllers on strike in Ronkonkoma, New York, in August, 1981. _PHOTOGRAPH BY DAVID HANDSCHUH, Source:[https://www.newyorker.com/culture/culture-desk/the-cost-of-defying-the-president](https://substack.com/redirect/f289a87c-2b6c-4b04-826a-981a6aebab91?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
The [Air Traffic Controllers strike of 1981](https://substack.com/redirect/9233b864-ae75-4da9-bc45-861ee70ea4b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which began on August 3, 1981 when around 13,000 air traffic controllers walked off the job after efforts to negotiate higher pay and shorter workweeks failed. [Thousands of flights were cancelled and President Ronald Reagan declared the strike illegal and said he'd fire anyone who wasn't back at work within 48 hours](https://substack.com/redirect/442d49dd-e8e4-4975-9301-71309f82a600?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). President Regan carried out his threat and added a lifetime ban on the Federal Aviation Administration ever rehiring a fired striker. More than 11,000 members of the Professional Air Traffic Controllers Association (PATCO) were fired.
[For decades after the Air Traffic Controllers strike, labor unions were less compelling and the number of strikes begins to trail off](https://substack.com/redirect/bfb63ea1-7ebf-449b-956b-e2103dc2677b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). But more recently, we've seen a rebirth of organized labor, driven, in my assessment, by two major developments: (1) high and rapidly rising inequality and (2) COVID disruptions that motivated worker demands for better working conditions.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NWpByDP9y3W2qVlBbXLi6nfvatqrFfJW3OwFhLAif_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=136698084)
I've long believed that capitalism is the greatest form of economic organization on planet earth and has been a powerful force in raising the quality of life for millions of people. With that said, it isn't perfect and has, from time to time, produced problematic levels of inequality, something many believe (including me) is the achilles heal of capitalism.
Karl Marx predicted that inequality would lead to the end of capitalism as it drove workers to unite and usher in a new world of harmony in which "from each according to their ability; to each according to their needs" became a prevailing mantra. In short, Marx believed capitalism would self-destruct through rising inequality and be displaced with a socialist utopia. It should be no surprise then that inequality drives unionization and collective bargaining efforts.
Marx believed capitalism would self-destruct through rising inequality and be displaced with a socialist utopia.
But over time, other factors have also played a major role in driving labor unrest. Whether it was hours per week, worker safety risks, or just some control over schedules, labor has long supplemented its desires for higher wages with a quest for more appealing conditions.
In fact, a recent CNBC piece highlighted how an almost perfect storm of conditions have driven labor unions to aggressively seek double-digit raises and better hours for their members:
> [Emboldened in the wake of shifting job security and grueling conditions during the Covid-19 pandemic, skyrocketing company profits, inflation, a decades-high approval rating for labor unions and growing disparity between worker pay and executive compensation, more workers across industries have taken a hard stance against companies for dramatic improvements in compensation and working conditions](https://substack.com/redirect/f714a512-5ad3-45ad-91d4-b684c3126e04?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The poster child of success in this effort has been the International Brotherhood of Teamsters' (IBT) success in negotiating with United Parcel Service (UPS). After the more than 340,000 union members working at UPS had authorized a strike to begin on August 1, the parties [avoided a work stoppage after agreeing to higher wages, better working conditions, more work flexibility, and even an additional holiday](https://substack.com/redirect/4d4872ba-5e9e-4cb4-98ee-e4d58a66c9d2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). By the end of the five year agreement, [average UPS driver compensation will be approximately $170,000 (including benefits)](https://substack.com/redirect/d4f37e34-af1c-4ed8-8ee8-3c443c9e3996?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/ac9d4e6a-7efb-4bd0-be22-35465297d50b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Teamsters employed by UPS hold a rally outside a UPS facility as an August 1st strike deadline against the company nears in Los Angeles, California, U.S. July 19, 2023. Photo by Mike Blake/Reuters. Source: [https://www.cnn.com/2023/07/25/business/ups-teamsters-deal-strike/index.html](https://substack.com/redirect/9fcb2ee6-b44f-4fd1-b920-e67042a1a884?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Collective bargaining in the aviation sector has also led to some big wage gains. Pilots at United Airlines, for instance, were able to secure a 40% raise over 4 years, while those at [Delta achieved a 34% raise over 4 years ](https://substack.com/redirect/7ce4bd45-0ed7-41ff-b37d-94746f588b8a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)and [American Airlines pilots obtained a 40% bump](https://substack.com/redirect/57899807-1a2a-4fb3-a0bd-2b95c97a7029?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The combination of pilot shortages, strong demand for air travel, and COVID related changes in working conditions gave the unions strong leverage to negotiate historic deals for pilots.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NWpByDP9y3W2qVlBbXLi6nfvatqrFfJW3OwFhLAif_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=136698084)
Similar dynamics have played out in many other sectors and labor unions have been noticeably active this year. Perhaps it's because after decades of falling real wages, inflation and tight labor markets have driven workers to seek better? The Cornell University School of Industrial and Labor Relations has reported that [more than 320,000 workers have participated in more than 230 strikes so far this year](https://substack.com/redirect/f714a512-5ad3-45ad-91d4-b684c3126e04?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). (That compares to around 240,000 workers in around 420 strikes last year.) Not all of these disputes have been resolved...
Think about what's happening in Hollywood. In May, the Writers Guild of America (WGA) began a strike against the Alliance of Motion Picture and Television Producers (AMPTP), about compensation related to streaming services and the risks to writers emerging from generative artificial intelligence. [Then in July, the Screen Actors Guild and American Federation of Television and Radio Artists (SAG-AFTRA) went on strike given their own dispute with AMPTP](https://substack.com/redirect/d7819212-6823-4e44-837a-22093410bfbb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). This is the first time since 1960 that both writers and actors have simultaneously walked out. And while there has been some negotiation in both of these disputes, both the [writers](https://substack.com/redirect/71f56cf4-3b4a-43d8-b3f8-a2dfebe37a4b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [actors](https://substack.com/redirect/a1add067-991c-4ae3-b281-23cdc555a07f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) seem to be digging in for a prolonged fight. [SAG-AFTRA is even contemplating an additional strike against video game makers](https://substack.com/redirect/9ac5ffb8-d520-4c6d-9d75-974da9399ce5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8aec36ad-5a6f-4cab-9d92-f693c0a20f66?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
SAG-AFTRA members walk a picket line in support of the SAG-AFTRA and WGA strike outside Warner Bros. Discovery Headquarters on Aug. 31, 2023 in New York City. Source: [https://abcnews.go.com/US/hollywood-labor-disputes-headed-crunch-time-amid-ongoing/story?id=102861883](https://substack.com/redirect/d0c2854d-9997-4484-9812-5701ff2ff19a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
It also appears that more strikes are possible. The[ United Auto Workers (UAW) for instance, represents more than 150,000 workers at General Motors, Ford, and Stellantis. They are in the midst of contract negotiations and have a deadline of September 14th to come to an agreement.](https://substack.com/redirect/e7fae10d-eedb-4264-8a20-70ee20402dca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Likely emboldened by other union successes, UAW President Shawn Fain has recently struck a combative tone and has [filed an unfair labor practices claim against General Motors and Stellantis with the National Labor Relations Board](https://substack.com/redirect/c3b07bb5-8c56-4210-93d6-c930aa326d97?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). All of this suggests a likely strike of UAW members later this month.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NWpByDP9y3W2qVlBbXLi6nfvatqrFfJW3OwFhLAif_4?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=136698084)
A look back at history may provide a glimpse of what's to come. Professor Joshua Freeman, a distinguished professor at the City University of New York who previously taught at Columbia University and has written extensively about the history of labor in America, gave a briefing on the History of Labor in the United States in July 2020 for the US State Department. During his presentation, he shared a fascinating fact that got little attention but struck me as potentially important: "[The greatest strike wave in US history took place in 1919. One out of every five workers in the entire country went on strike...this actually overlapped with the worse pandemic in US history, the influenza epidemic of 1918, 1919](https://substack.com/redirect/59a91097-06e2-4e73-9a5b-119520d093c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)."
[](https://substack.com/redirect/c48f18d9-7902-4e07-8791-6750488746b9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Could it be that the US is about to enter another "strike wave"? Could the risks to labor from the rise of artificial intelligence create a rapidly rising need for collective bargaining, bringing workers together in unexpected ways? Or could artificial intelligence simply increase inequality to the point that workers feel the need to unite and overthrow those in control? Is what's happening in Hollywood a warning sign of what's to come?
And even if formal union membership is not the mechanism for collective bargaining, might we get ad hoc groupings of workers for particular causes? Recall the [Fight for 15](https://substack.com/redirect/212f1645-ee56-41ed-9f0f-28b4f4733b26?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the campaign launched by fast-food workers seeking a $15/hr minimum wage. Even though it was supported by a union, growth in membership wasn't the objective, political action was the goal. And it within a few years, they achieved their objective in several states. Is political action replacing collective bargaining as an objective of organized labor?
Finally, it's interesting to note that the United States and United Kingdom had similar labor organization efforts in the late 19th century. In the America, this led to unionization that focused on collective bargaining to promote workers' wages and better working conditions. The British effort went in a different direction, one that targeted political action, and led to the formation of the [Labour Party](https://substack.com/redirect/1ffea94f-3d08-4a33-8e30-f0e002d40686?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Even though American labor unions have historically chosen to align with established political parties, might they soon decide to step out on their own? Might current conditions in America be sowing the seeds of a new Labor Party in the United States?
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/946ced3d-3933-4abc-8921-c18b517f3865?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
There will always be a balancing act between the returns to labor and the return on capital. Let's not forget, of course, that while wages are considered a cost in production, they are also the ultimate source of revenue for a company as well:
> _[AN APOCRYPHAL tale is told about Henry Ford II showing Walter Reuther, the veteran leader of the United Automobile Workers, around a newly automated car plant."Walter, how are you going to get those robots to pay your union dues," gibed the boss of Ford Motor Company. Without skipping a beat, Reuther replied, "Henry, how are you going to get them to buy your cars?"](https://substack.com/redirect/3ffaccd4-efdc-4d84-ae00-d051fefb5c49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
As _The Economist_ magazine notes, "[The original Henry Ford, committed to raising productivity and lowering prices remorselessly, appreciated this profoundly--and insisted on paying his workers twice the going rate, so they could afford to buy his cars](https://substack.com/redirect/3ffaccd4-efdc-4d84-ae00-d051fefb5c49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." It's also the reason that General Motors was once known as "[Generous Motors](https://substack.com/redirect/1d6f23e3-d0d5-4a7a-8a4a-bfcd63271f81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" and paid multiples of the minimum wage to their employees.
But as warned by many investment funds, past performance is not an indicator of future outcomes. The world is changing rapidly. Artificial intelligence is replacing labor and increasing output. Accelerating inequality is intensifying the pressures for collective bargaining. And COVID has changed the nature of work for many, driving a desire for greater autonomy and control over working conditions. Yet at the same time, a shortage of workers in many industries has improved the relative power of labor and created a situation where real wages for many industries are rising for the first time in decades.
These cross-currents have created profound uncertainty in labor markets, and while it's impossible to know the outlook for labor unrest, one thing appears almost certain: organized labor is unlikely to disappear anytime soon.

Might rising inequality, worker shortages, and artificial intelligence be creating fertile ground for more frequent strikes? | | | Sep 4 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- As many of us fire up the grills for one last hurrah, it's worth pausing for a minute to think about the purpose of Labor Day, our federal US holiday to celebrate the social and economic achievements of American workers. According to the , "The holiday is rooted in the late nineteenth century, when labor activists pushed for a federal holiday to recognize the many contributions workers have made to America's strength, prosperity, and well-being." The holiday is also a useful catalyst to reflect on the history of and outlook for labor unrest. While there are too many strikes in the United States to review, I want to highlight three big ones: The Pullman Strike, which led to the recognition of Labor Day as a federal holiday; the Coal Strike of 1902, which led to the US government playing the role of mediator; and the Air Traffic Controllers strike of 1981, which led to a multi-decade structural decline in the role of unions in America. , which some have called the "." By the end of the three week strike, riots had spread nationally, federal troops were mobilized, workers lost more than $1 million in wages (), damage exceeded $340,000 (), railroads missed out on millions in revenues, and more than 40 died in clashes. The federal government's role in labor dispute mediation really began with the . President Teddy Roosevelt tried to bring both sides together while acting as a neutral mediator, but failed. It took JP Morgan and other businesspeople to secure an end to the 163 days strike. The miners secured a 10% pay increase (they had requested 20%) and 9 hour workday (down from 10, but not the 8 they sought). | | ---|---|--- Air-traffic controllers on strike in Ronkonkoma, New York, in August, 1981. PHOTOGRAPH BY DAVID HANDSCHUH, Source: The , which began on August 3, 1981 when around 13,000 air traffic controllers walked off the job after efforts to negotiate higher pay and shorter workweeks failed. . President Regan carried out his threat and added a lifetime ban on the Federal Aviation Administration ever rehiring a fired striker. More than 11,000 members of the Professional Air Traffic Controllers Association (PATCO) were fired. . But more recently, we've seen a rebirth of organized labor, driven, in my assessment, by two major developments: (1) high and rapidly rising inequality and (2) COVID disruptions that motivated worker demands for better working conditions. I've long believed that capitalism is the greatest form of economic organization on planet earth and has been a powerful force in raising the quality of life for millions of people. With that said, it isn't perfect and has, from time to time, produced problematic levels of inequality, something many believe (including me) is the achilles heal of capitalism. Karl Marx predicted that inequality would lead to the end of capitalism as it drove workers to unite and usher in a new world of harmony in which "from each according to their ability; to each according to their needs" became a prevailing mantra. In short, Marx believed capitalism would self-destruct through rising inequality and be displaced with a socialist utopia. It should be no surprise then that inequality drives unionization and collective bargaining efforts. Marx believed capitalism would self-destruct through rising inequality and be displaced with a socialist utopia. But over time, other factors have also played a major role in driving labor unrest. Whether it was hours per week, worker safety risks, or just some control over schedules, labor has long supplemented its desires for higher wages with a quest for more appealing conditions. In fact, a recent CNBC piece highlighted how an almost perfect storm of conditions have driven labor unions to aggressively seek double-digit raises and better hours for their members: . The poster child of success in this effort has been the International Brotherhood of Teamsters' (IBT) success in negotiating with United Parcel Service (UPS). body:

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: Happy Labor Day, Peter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
Together, we’ll build a workforce that protects, uplifts, and supports all working people, no matter what.
#
Happy Labor Day, Peter!
Since 1882, working people in America have taken Labor Day to honor not just the work they’ve done, but the battles they’ve won.
The power of organized labor has never been understated — we’ve moved mountains in the fight for progress. **And that is why I will continue to fight for a better life for us all.**
**Every day, I am honored to stand and fight alongside working people all across America.** Together, we’ll build a workforce that protects, uplifts, and supports all working people, no matter what.
Without labor unions, we wouldn’t have the weekend, overtime pay, workplace safety rules, child labor laws, and so much more. Working people have secured every major labor law we have in America.
**And even now, Republican legislatures are working hard to roll back these laws on behalf of their big corporate donors.** In several states, Republicans have been playing the long game to strip away child labor laws and expand roles for minors — _even going as far as to let them serve alcohol in bars and restaurants as young as 14 years old._ 1
**Peter, we must all come together to fight for pro-worker legislation by supporting our collective progressive agenda, uplifting Democrats, and continuing to build a movement rooted in love and compassion.[Can you donate $3 to our campaign to fuel our efforts?](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/45479?refcode=em20230904-2879&t=1&akid=2879%2E420207%2Ey-k0jc)**
**[Donate ››](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/45479?refcode=em20230904-2879&t=20&akid=2879%2E420207%2Ey-k0jc)**
In solidarity,
Pramila Jayapal
* * *
1\. [https://www.usnews.com/news/best-states/articles/2023-06-26/states-are-loosening-child-labor-laws](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/45353?t=21&akid=2879%2E420207%2Ey-k0jc)

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20230904-2879-footer&t=22&refcode2=2879_420207_y-k0jc&akid=2879%2E420207%2Ey-k0jc)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=23&akid=2879%2E420207%2Ey-k0jc)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=24&akid=2879%2E420207%2Ey-k0jc)

subject: We owe it all to you.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stand with Sanchez
body:
I’ve said it once, and I’ll say it a million times: I’ll never stop fighting for the working class.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69207015/425397016/919917611?refcode=BLU-SAN-20230904-1-EM&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85ODI2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjZTk3ODZiYy01ODRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2jnWem_qKxbS9gY6KAOhfwFmKs6VaqzxXRyIZztQnoM=)
---
| Peter,

Labor Day marks a time of rest and a celebration of the American labor movement. It invites us to reflect on generations of the relentless activism unions and union workers put in to give us the wages, benefits, and power that have sustained our country.

This is personal to me. I was raised in a working-class immigrant family. My career – becoming a first-generation college graduate, a labor rights attorney, and a sitting Congresswoman for California’s 38th District – are all fruits of my parents’ labor.

These days, I am inspired to action by workers across the country demanding their fair share amidst Republican opposition and legislative attacks. Millions of workers are seeing the impacts of rising costs and stagnant wages, and it demands urgent attention.

I’ve said it once, and I’ll say it a million times: I’ll never stop fighting for the working class.

I am proud to be a founding member of the Labor Caucus. As your Congresswoman, I’m working every day to improve working conditions and protect workers’ rights in our community and across the country.

On this Labor Day, I want to send my sincere thanks to workers across the country who keep us moving forward.

Happy Labor Day!

Linda
---

name: Kim Nguyen
subject: Happy Labor Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kim Nguyen for Congress
body:
| | **Happy Labor Day!**

Today, we celebrate the incredible strength and determination of all of the workers who power our nation. Labor Day is an opportunity to recognize the victories of the labor movement – and to recommit ourselves to fight for workers’ rights.

Unions have shaped our nation for the better. When working people come together and bargain for better wages and safer conditions, **we all benefit.**

When I’m in Congress, I’ll be a fearless champion for workers’ rights. I’ll work to secure better jobs and higher wages – and I’ll fight tooth and nail to protect the right to organize.

**That’s why I’m proud to share that I’ve been endorsed by the Orange County Labor Federation (OCLF), which represents _over 90 local unions_.**

The OCLF joins unions like the Los Angeles/Orange County Building & Construction Trades Council and the International Brotherhood of Teamsters Joint Council 42 in supporting my campaign.

**It’s clear that I’m the choice of organized labor in this race.**

On this Labor Day, let's celebrate the labor movement and continue fighting for fair wages, safe working conditions, and economic justice for all.

Thanks for being a part of this fight.

In solidarity,

Kim | | **[CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69207061/425397344/786111081?contactdata=XfOgyhkSpjBYp9zCuoMrgQEuqGndUObAcuAlEnGkKig/vGF6XXJjNGDpDplyydQvBJLWTh9jc7z97bw6i7T8ibmYjK89o/k8CXaqzDEtBxs=&amount=10&refcode=KBN_EM_FR_3MO_230904_1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM4MzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZmIwY2FhNmQtNTk0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=7aiPtx07A_cAYpuXPsbNrJsEpO-qa2SUGiIP8jjPv_4=)**

At just 25, she became the youngest person ever elected to the Garden Grove City Council. name: Yolanda Bejarano
subject: Power for working people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Arizona Democrats**
body:
We need your input! |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69207095/425397415/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC60_20230904_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWZhN2I2ODctNTk0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=k0CbjlXtl6Rcs31P2zTh8l7uUqrjsnA2B9O3V6r0D74=)

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69207096/425397416/1028146167?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_GEN_GEN_AC60_20230904_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNWZhN2I2ODctNTk0Yi1lZTExLWEzZjEtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=k0CbjlXtl6Rcs31P2zTh8l7uUqrjsnA2B9O3V6r0D74=) Folks — Happy Labor Day! The labor movement is stronger today than it has been in decades because workers have come together to amplify their voice to demand the protections and wages they deserve. From LA to New York, Amazon to UPS, workers are standing up and standing together against corporate billionaires in every industry. For the first time in over sixty years, SAG-AFTRA and the Writers Guild of America are on the picket lines together to raise the bar for actors and writers everywhere, putting the labor movement center stage. I couldn’t be more proud of the workers in Arizona and across the country who are standing up for what’s right and what they deserve. Before I began my time as party chair, I served as the field director for the Communications Workers of America which represents over 700,000 communications and media employees across various backgrounds, sectors, and industries in the country. In that role, I helped empower workers to recognize their worth and influence, not only on the job but in electoral politics as well. In both Democratic politics and in our labor movement, the key to making lasting, impactful change is how we organize. By organizing every group and mobilizing every community, not only are we bringing diverse voices and experiences to the table, we’ll ensure we can build power with and for workers for the long haul, to turn out in every election to elect champions for working people. **I can assure you, friend, we won’t ever let the calls of Arizona’s workers go unheard.** Thank a union today! In solidarity, Yolanda Bejarano Chair, Arizona Democratic Party | | **We’re coming up on our last big deadline of the summer, and we could use your help. Could you pitch in $5 (or more!) this week to help us expand our strategy to reach voters in Glendale? subject: 💙 Happy Labor Day! 🧡
disclaimer_text: Paid for by David Kim for Congress - CA34_
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/davidkimforca/climatechange-13910710?e=fee98d637f)
---
| | [ ](https://davidkimforca.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c05fe848bb5af5e7b9d690b2f&id=0f42c41d3c&e=fee98d637f)
---
| | Dear friend: This Labor Day, I want to uplift and support the work of Labor Unions here in Los Angeles and everywhere, starting with Unite Here! and their massive Hotel Strike and Boycott. Hotel workers have been engaging in protest activity for months, after authorizing a strike in early June. Since then, around 60 hotels have failed to renew their contracts with Unite Here! Local 11 and are subject to boycott.
---
|
---
|
---
---
| 🏨 While enjoying your hard-won three-day weekend this year, check the list of hotels under boycott before visiting or dining: [https://www.unitehere11.org/2023-contract-fight/](https://davidkimforca.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c05fe848bb5af5e7b9d690b2f&id=dd68110e03&e=fee98d637f) ✍️🎬 Writers and actors are also fighting for fair contracts, with WGA West on strike since May 2023, and SAGAFTRA following in July. [https://twitter.com/WGAWest/status/1653242408195457025?s=20](https://davidkimforca.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c05fe848bb5af5e7b9d690b2f&id=2bd9f4f3d6&e=fee98d637f) 📬📦 Finally, the International Brotherhood of Teamsters has reached a deal with UPS, averting a strike and resulting in what they consider the “most historic collective bargaining agreement in the history of UPS." [https://teamster.org/2023/07/weve-changed-the-game-teamsters-win-historic-ups-contract/](https://davidkimforca.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c05fe848bb5af5e7b9d690b2f&id=20a5b95af4&e=fee98d637f) It’s important to take time this Labor Day to recognize and understand that we are all stronger and more protected when we stand together, and that corporations cannot exist without us. It is our labor that produces value for these companies, and it is our labor that holds up the American economy. So why is it not our voices that dictate policy? That’s why I’ve made People-Centered Politics—the belief that political campaigns should not be financed by corporate donations—a core pillar of my campaign. Learn more by visiting [Davidkimforca.com/platform/](https://davidkimforca.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=c05fe848bb5af5e7b9d690b2f&id=98f6074089&e=fee98d637f)

Let's stand in solidarity and with a heartfelt appreciation for the collective strength of workers!
---

name: Jared Moskowitz
subject: Honoring Labor Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jared Moskowitz For Congress P
body:
|
---
Hey friends, happy Labor Day — I hope you’re having a restful day.

As we are enjoying today with friends and family, I want to take a moment to honor the hard working folks who have advocated for workers’ rights. We’ve come a long way, but our work is not done.

Labor Day reminds us that union workers gave us 40 hour work weeks, safer working conditions, and minimum wage laws. It also reminds us that supporting our workers and our unions — both in our words and in our legislation — is how we all move forward.

Floridians deserve more than the bare minimum — we deserve liveable wages, paid family leave, and protection against discrimination. Floridians deserve leadership that works for working families, not against them. And it’s what I’m fighting for in Congress everyday.

I am committed to this fight, and I am proud to represent working families in our district and across Florida.

Have a safe and restful Labor Day, friends.

_Jared_

name: Dr. Brian Williams
subject: still so much work to be done
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Brian Williams for Congress
body:
| | Peter,

I want to wish you and your family a happy Labor Day.

Unions have driven so many of our nation’s advancements towards an equitable workforce. The 40 hour workweek, safe working conditions, paid leave, and minimum wages are the legacy of organized labor.

As we celebrate the hard-fought advances won by the working people who’ve come before us, we must also stand in solidarity with workers across the country who are still on the frontlines demanding a fair deal. As corporations and CEOs make record profits, we must hold them accountable to their workers who drive their success. So today, I hope you take a moment to reflect on the workers who continue to fight these battles – from Americans struggling to make liveable wages to our income inequality gap – we still have much work to do.

I wish you a happy and safe Labor Day.

– Brian

---

subject: It's time to make DC balance the budget.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Binkley For President 2024
body:
| |
---
| | | [ ](https://binkley2024.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5894b488eac5a196e1e5d6896&id=0db660db26&e=c1403f41d3)
---
| | | Dear Friend,

Happy Labor Day! For 120 years, we've set aside the first Monday in September to **celebrate and honor the hard working Americans** who keep our economy running.

But our economy, once the envy of the world, is in trouble. Under "Bidenomics," the **value of our hard-earned wages is falling fast.**

**Rampant inflation and high interest rates** are eating away the average family's ability to cover their monthly bills. The dream of home ownership is slipping away from our younger generations, with the**average mortgage now $400 higher today** than it was just two years ago.

And since Biden took office, **the U.S. dollar has lost 16 percent of its value**.

Let’s be real. Nearly every one of the GOP candidates on the debate stage in August has served in a powerful office for the past 20 years, yet **none of them has a plan** **to rein in the overspending that has sent** **inflation to its highest levels in 40 years**!

[My plan](https://binkley2024.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=5894b488eac5a196e1e5d6896&id=4a3cf7acfa&e=c1403f41d3) will **balance the federal budget in just 7 years** and will include a balanced budget amendment, among many other safeguards, to limit government spending.

Do you agree that it's time to **make Washington play by the same rules** as every household and small business in America? It's time to make D.C. balance the budget.

We **need to raise $20,000** this Labor Day Weekend to extend our media reach and spread this message. We've made good progress, but we still have a ways to go.

Please consider donating $100, $50, or even $25 to my campaign today.

Let's take this economic rescue plan all the way to the White House!
---

| Sincerely,

name: Working Families Party
subject: It’s a Labor Year
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St
body:
Check out our Labor Day video: | | | It’s not just a Labor Day, Peter, it’s a Labor YEAR. In the last 12 months we’ve seen over 400 strikes — where hotel workers, health care workers, writers, actors, and more have taken to the picket lines to demand better pay, better benefits, and better working conditions for all. Workers create value. They are the lifeblood of every company, and it is the labor of workers that makes the economy go ‘round. But CEOs and stockholders are hoarding profits for themselves — while workers are left with a smaller and smaller piece of the pie. That’s where unions come in. Unions build power for working people, protect workers, and help close the wealth inequality gap. **That’s why we firmly believe in the right to organize.** So today on Labor Day, we’re launching a new video to talk about the importance of workers coming together, across our differences, to demand better working conditions for all. And we’re including a guide for any worker who wants to unionize their workplace. [**Click here to watch our Labor Day video and share with the workers in your life.**](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/atcYNHk4Eh2YdGnwBh-YDE-osrDsyUs4KS0eRiq-k7RtnjvXK2BNxEZnUkvhBH8GrZZMWL53hay9I3zb0L6zRBXAmAe5-I7Tj48DvBi3brWDwYDmNOdTimBO-nLP1eG_IbvHrRosMcmnszpTFZCIosyuZg_IWDDdVD20L-NqdBuos_dRoLbMtTmpQ4bkGJlXmBBozzG5nWvZTgZwayM0wfzulJK-tmyN8M0b039FR-tRh9hLAbeRACMRKrarmYpU/3zb/P-muJp4SQYqEBOFQ5An_dw/h0/v0-1rjQ1UodVQIhRKh3aJoBPJ4MxES_rI-PWMYjgfco) clean_body: Check out our Labor Day video: | | | It’s not just a Labor Day, Peter, it’s a Labor YEAR. In the last 12 months we’ve seen over 400 strikes — where hotel workers, health care workers, writers, actors, and more have taken to the picket lines to demand better pay, better benefits, and better working conditions for all. Workers create value. They are the lifeblood of every company, and it is the labor of workers that makes the economy go ‘round. But CEOs and stockholders are hoarding profits for themselves — while workers are left with a smaller and smaller piece of the pie. That’s where unions come in. Unions build power for working people, protect workers, and help close the wealth inequality gap. That’s why we firmly believe in the right to organize. So today on Labor Day, we’re launching a new video to talk about the importance of workers coming together, across our differences, to demand better working conditions for all. And we’re including a guide for any worker who wants to unionize their workplace. Happy Labor Day, From all of us at the Working Families Party Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. name: Friends of the Earth
subject: Your Signature Is Needed: Stop Arctic drilling from threatening endangered polar bears
body:
| | | | | [](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1964784?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---|---|---

| | Friend, Labor Day might unofficially mark “the end of summer”, but it certainly doesn’t feel that way in many parts of the country that are experiencing record-high temperatures and extreme heat warnings. And in the Arctic, global warming is hitting __TWICE__ as hard with the region warming **2x** the rate of the rest of the Earth. This is bad news for vulnerable polar bears who are left watching their ice habitats melt around them while they cling to survival. To make matters even worse for these beloved animals, Big Oil and Gas are threatening to bring in **dirty, toxic pollutants** and industrial machinery to the already-fragile Arctic. The drilling and fracking alone is terrible for the environment, releasing **harmful greenhouse gas emissions** into the atmosphere — but the massive equipment also puts innocent polar bears in danger of being _**RUN OVER**_ or _**BURIED ALIVE**_ under snow. The fossil fuel industry has done enough damage to the planet, and we cannot let these greedy corporations push endangered polar bears to _**EXTINCTION**_. [**Please, Friend, can you take just a minute out of your Labor Day to help protect defenseless polar bears and fight back against Arctic drilling? Add your name now! >>**](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1964785?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==) | [SIGN THE PETITION](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1964786?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
With the recent federal ruling that approved new oil and gas drilling in the Arctic, the U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service (FWS) confirmed a _**95% probability**_ that the increase in fossil fuel activity will cause _**“SERIOUS INJURY OR DEATH”**_ to the endangered Southern Beaufort polar bears who live there — _the most imperiled polar bear population in the entire world!_
And the noise and air pollution brought in by Big Oil and Gas don’t just harm polar bears and wildlife in the Arctic. The expanded drilling activity is an environmental hazard that impacts the entire landscape, including the sensitive ecosystem that local residents depend on for survival, predominantly Indigenous tribes.
Friend, while you might be soaking up the sun and warmth today, the Arctic region is in peril and at further risk of being destroyed by fossil fuel greed. As one of our top environmentalists, we’re really hoping that you care enough about the planet to take action right now by adding your name to our petition calling on FWS to reject increased drilling and fracking in the Arctic. [**We can’t do this without you, Friend. Will you join us in the fight against Big Oil’s environmental destruction by signing our petition right away?**](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1964787?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
[SIGN THE PETITION](https://us.engagingnetworks.app/page/email/click/10012/1964786?email=RepJ2ZGQKX6xm5Aey7FwClESkN1MQhKC&campid=UI6tX83ZqbKZkArzVWMSmA==)
---
**Thanks for using your voice to protect the planet,
Friends of the Earth**
|

The expanded drilling activity is an environmental hazard that impacts the entire landscape, including the sensitive ecosystem that local residents depend on for survival, predominantly Indigenous tribes. Friend, while you might be soaking up the sun and warmth today, the Arctic region is in peril and at further risk of being destroyed by fossil fuel greed. As one of our top environmentalists, we’re really hoping that you care enough about the planet to take action right now by adding your name to our petition calling on FWS to reject increased drilling and fracking in the Arctic. == == Thanks for using your voice to protect the planet, Friends of the Earth | Contact Us: Friends of the Earth U.S. Washington, D.C. | Berkeley, CA 1-877-843-8687 foe.org/about-us/contact/

name: Maxwell Frost
subject: in solidarity with workers everywhere
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MAXWELL FROST FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/ylaofL_TdK310PiJpw8-lA~~/AAPPAAA~/RgRm2M7tP0SVaHR0cHM6Ly9hY3QuY2FtcGFpZ25kZXB1dHkuY29tL2xhYm9yZGF5P2NkbWM9MlV3cURuZmg0S3JxYkNJQ2EySHBmOTJtelMyJnJlZmNvZGUyPTJVd3FEbmZoNEtycWJDSUNhMkhwZjkybXpTMiZyZWZjb2RlY2RtYz0yVXdxRG5maDRLcnFiQ0lDYTJIcGY5Mm16UzJXA3NwY0IKZPDtSfZk26rSd1IUZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb21YBAAAAGk~) Happy Labor Day, Peter!

To all of the working folks, labor union members and leaders, and everyone fighting to advance the labor movement in Florida and across the country: **_Thank you. You are the heartbeat and driving force of our nation’s economy, and I’m with y’all all the way._**

name: Gabe Amo
subject: Join us on Election Night!
body:
https://www.gabeamo.com/ [https://www.gabeamo.com/]

Hi Friend,

I hope you’re doing well and enjoying the long holiday weekend! I just wanted to make sure that you got an invitation to join our Election Night Watch Party taking place TOMORROW! #TeamGabe will be at The Guild in Pawtucket starting at 8 pm on Election Night. I hope you’ll join us!

[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/73666D6A6564677A51364156704E52736334336573757779785333714E76496E30542B6C744843703841624550753641447A66326F746C694244515767507144/editor_images/GAFC%20Election%20Night%20Watch%20Party%20Invitation.png]

Date: Tuesday, September 5th, 2023
Time: 8 PM -12 AM
Please RSVP to kate@gabeamo.com [kate@gabeamo.com] or 401-545-0788.

Thank you for your support. I hope to see you soon.

With gratitude,
Gabe

**GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

subject: Neither McConnell freezing, nor Biden rambling, is the problem
body:

| |
---|---|---
| | | [Open in app](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/neither-mcconnell-freezing-nor-biden?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store) or [online](https://substack..v9QV71g3lldddja3wlp9qWBIpi36LDOS__qpkmsVkMk?)
