[Neither McConnell freezing, nor Biden rambling, is the problem](https://substack..v9QV71g3lldddja3wlp9qWBIpi36LDOS__qpkmsVkMk?)
America's institutions--not only the octogenarian careerists who have co-opted them--chain our futures to the failures of the past.
| | [Listen to preview](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=135504115&utm_source=podcast-email&play_audio=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM1NTA0MTE1LCJpYXQiOjE2OTM4NjQ4NjIsImV4cCI6MTY5NjQ1Njg2MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.OS3UvaP4sYUKsagGey_NURPPnOxWFW3rLWCG0ClgOpQ&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email#play)|
---|---|---
---
---
[](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | Shahid Buttar| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=135504115&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM1NTA0MTE1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY5Mzg2NDg2MiwiZXhwIjoxNjk2NDU2ODYyLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.PbF_HdqO1YMnw9YIVL6XAlC36r5v3zSMSjzS2mDuGqM&utm_medium=email&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=135504115&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM1NTA0MTE1LCJpYXQiOjE2OTM4NjQ4NjIsImV4cCI6MTY5NjQ1Njg2MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.OS3UvaP4sYUKsagGey_NURPPnOxWFW3rLWCG0ClgOpQ&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=135504115&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM1NTA0MTE1LCJpYXQiOjE2OTM4NjQ4NjIsImV4cCI6MTY5NjQ1Njg2MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.OS3UvaP4sYUKsagGey_NURPPnOxWFW3rLWCG0ClgOpQ)
This Sunday, _The Guardian '_s David Smith published an editorial about gerontocracy in Washington. It explores whether our nation's ruing class is "[too old to govern](https://substack.com/redirect/ef1bd09d-306f-4b7b-ad9e-96df79d7c95e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)," noting how both parties share an interest in sweeping under the rug the advancing age of their respective leaders, who [Larry Jacobs](https://substack.com/redirect/4ccd90c6-3836-45e5-92eb-ced935756880?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), director of the Center for the Study of Politics and Governance at the University of Minnesota, described as "decrepit."
### A "senior moment" is no laughing matter
The spectacle of the Senate Majority Leader [freezing at the podium](https://substack.com/redirect/64b24a53-e36a-4fd5-9497-5849c99918e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) last month--and again [last week](https://substack.com/redirect/b4ace448-62d4-4388-85d5-4af654d7190d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--inspired any number of [clever](https://substack.com/redirect/0f6e8c76-96ef-48e9-be85-ac43d040a54d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [memes](https://substack.com/redirect/a92e6dbd-efed-4e19-aef7-5eb5182ecc99?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and a pointed reflection from at least [one presidential candidate](https://substack.com/redirect/a7ec92e3-193e-4cd3-9f9f-089cab9ff8e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (with whom I agree on little else).
But despite the righteous outrage channeled towards a figure who has laughed all the way to the bank for decades while enriching one industrial complex after another, 81-year-old Mitch McConnell is not the problem.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/471d606a-2e53-4913-822b-5120638446e8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Nor is Joe Biden, the 80-year-old president whose administration has abandoned his campaign promises while doubling down on [fossil fuel extraction](https://substack.com/redirect/549c2a23-ec16-411b-a69b-39af76c8554b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), senseless [wars](https://substack.com/redirect/88625b72-c1f8-4191-aed5-8e0c5e2d3faf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) serving no purpose beyond padding the profits of weapons manufacturers, and [restrictions](https://substack.com/redirect/e7c5394c-1d54-4979-992c-50b409b7a0bf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on labor rights enabling an upward [transfer of wealth](https://substack.com/redirect/beefc56c-d21f-40c6-91d5-eec8d6a076f5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that continues across the United States and the rest of the world.
Nor is Dianne Feinstein, the 90-year-old oligarch whose career representing California in the Senate has [enabled](https://substack.com/redirect/d0dfcec9-61e6-4943-ba69-397161ad7238?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) GOP policy priorities for decades. She went conspicuously [missing](https://substack.com/redirect/02cf0217-8fdf-4ebc-909d-7cb54ae03d09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for several weeks this spring, imperiling judicial appointments and largely bringing the Senate to a [screeching halt](https://substack.com/redirect/4283061d-d1c3-4313-b5ed-9bfe4708b120?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), before making oblivious [public claims](https://substack.com/redirect/db43e773-7ec5-4f0a-b4e1-207024e4bd07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that ran perhaps the greatest risk of proving Nikki Haley [right](https://substack.com/redirect/a7ec92e3-193e-4cd3-9f9f-089cab9ff8e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Nor is Donald Trump, the 77-year-old former President and would-be tyrant whose mendacity and racism found support in a fascist [cult of personality](https://substack.com/redirect/e663a932-8c36-42b5-a202-6c713f0f4306?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that continues to grow despite--and perhaps due to--his mounting criminal prosecutions and vilification by a press establishment that more or less [created him](https://substack.com/redirect/bcc46c86-eabf-4de5-a3f7-8a5e75baeea2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and placed him in the White House in the first place.
Each of these figures are senior citizens. Responding to a _Washington Post_ [editorial](https://substack.com/redirect/8f96e804-7521-497f-8a45-25a1c41c7c85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) exploring whether Biden's age should be a factor in the 2024 election, one reader praised Biden, suggesting that his "running [for re-election] is one of the bravest, most selfless things I've ever seen in politics." Another concerned reader replied, [observing ](https://substack.com/redirect/224dce4c-620d-4de0-b2d0-faa915d5e702?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)that "There is nothing 'selfless' about holding public office well into your 80s and refusing to leave until carried out feet first."
Other readers who wrote to the _Washington Post_ noted how long-serving officials refuse to make space for younger voices. But the dynamic they observe is offensive for many other reasons, for better or worse.
To describe Washington as a gerontocracy is frankly charitable....[T]heir individual incapacity is just part of the problem, ultimately akin to the part of an iceberg that looms above the water.
This newsletter is supported by readers, not advertisers. Want to help inform others? Share this post with them.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=135504115&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTM1NTA0MTE1LCJpYXQiOjE2OTM4NjQ4NjIsImV4cCI6MTY5NjQ1Njg2MiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.OS3UvaP4sYUKsagGey_NURPPnOxWFW3rLWCG0ClgOpQ)
### Gerontocracy invites institutional--not just individual--incapacity
Some voices looked beyond McConnell's senior moments each of the last two months, Biden's frequent [disorientation](https://substack.com/redirect/d3151ebd-ff59-4af6-868f-098ab7ef090a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and recurring [refusals to respect](https://substack.com/redirect/5aa511ca-85ce-429e-a563-d26f54ef988e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the right of women to set and maintain their own boundaries, Feinstein's confusion about her own month-long absence from the Senate, and Trump's equally advancing age and legendary incoherence to identify the ultimate problem as gerontocracy: rule by the old.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8175dc4e-9cf8-4706-b4bb-3b50e2110b2b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
But to describe Washington as a gerontocracy is frankly charitable.
Concerns about gerontocracy center on mental fitness, and the capacity of people as they advance in age to execute their duties. Each of the figures above have revealed their unfortunate incapacity.
But their individual incapacity is just part of the problem, ultimately akin to the part of an iceberg that looms above the water.
### The GOP has no monopoly on gerontocracy
Voices who [connected](https://substack.com/redirect/66b3def4-551f-43f1-a245-7686bd07497a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) McConnell freezing to Biden frequently losing his train of thought made one important--and accurate--observation. For better or worse, the crisis illustrated by their shared incapacity is bipartisan.
The president is barely younger than the Senate Majority Leader, and seemingly equally prone to what some might fondly describe as "[senior moments.](https://substack.com/redirect/bd9729b0-c65e-4546-9397-93b993fa54a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" He is joined by any number of other aging figures in Washington, from Feinstein to 83 year-old Nancy Pelosi, the former Speaker of the House.
Why are so many of the most powerful figures in the world so old that they won't likely ever face the consequences of their own policy decisions? The heads of both corporate political parties in Washington are so reliant on paid staff that their decisions stretch the bounds of democratic theory premised on electoral accountability. If the people making the decisions are not themselves electorally accountable, how can the electorate "vote our way out of this?"
The rot exposed by this ossification is not merely individual, nor is it only politically bipartisan. Its constitutional dimension is even worse than the sum of its parts.
To receive new posts and learn more about Washington's layers of bipartisan corruption, sign up for a (free or paid) subscription.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..AKdItj5Y_fV1M45DWDZSLAQR5tuaWBi1ArlDQSClc6E?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=135504115)
### Institutional incapacity ossifies governance
When the Founders crafted the constitutional design under which we have lived for the past 250 years, they specifically intended some institutions to "cool the passions" of the Republic. Some, like scholar Charles Beard, have described their construct as specifically intended to limit the capacity of the "tyranny of the majority" to legislate economic redistribution.
#### The courts were constructed to slow democracy
Not only were the courts charged with guarding rights from the "tyranny of the majority," but the Supreme Court soon claimed the power to strike down acts of Congress (or state legislatures) that violate one or another constitutional principle. Over the Court's long history, many of those princples were effectively invented by Justices appointed for life with no avenue for public accountability.
Sound familiar?
In an earlier era, those principles included the contrived idea that any attempt at guarding workers presaged economic redistribution, and therefore unconstitutionally exceeded the legitimate powers of government. That was the rationale behind the infamous _[Lochner v. New York](https://substack.com/redirect/f91b2aeb-0ffc-4bed-ace3-3c6290c60224?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ decision that in 1905 invalidated labor reforms passed by the State of New York to protect the health & safety of bakers.
In _Lochner_ , The Justices imagined laissez faire economic policy to be a constitutional mandate, imposing their will on the People of the United States despite the actions of our elected representatives. They abandoned any semblance of democracy in the process. That was a hundred years ago.
Many liberals have seized on the [subsequent history](https://substack.com/redirect/44682f5c-4765-4fa0-93d3-c489f8bfa47d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--when FDR cleverly intimidated the Court by threatening to add new seats, ultimately leading the Court to repudiate its untenable position 22 years after _Lochner_ , in _[West Coast Hotel v Parrish](https://substack.com/redirect/a6142cfe-78bf-4b17-95ab-ec958afd1b8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ --as a recipe for reigning in the arbitrary and reactionary conservatism of the Roberts Court and its contemporary attack on principles including reproductive freedom.
That linear thinking is both lazy and also [dangerous](https://substack.com/redirect/9c8fb361-689e-4542-a7bd-6c8ec2d86111?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) since it ignores the contemporary context and would ultimately lead a leap from a frying pan into a fire. But I digress....
#### The Senate was designed as a counterweight to the House
Like the Supreme Court, the Senate was crafted as an institution intended to "cool the passions" of the Republic, which were supposed to principally emerge from the House of Representatives.
The House is composed of members elected every two years, apportioned across the states in proportion to their populations. Because Members have among the shortest terms of any elected office, they are presumed to be maximally responsive to concerns that emerge within their constituencies.
The House was supposed to represent We the People of the United States.
In contrast, the Senate was designed to represent the states. Senators are elected every six years in order to insulate them from the day-to-day tussle of power in Washington, and popular controversies that the Founders envisioned flipping seats in the House. Unlike the House, which is largely limited to proposing legislation, the Senate has the power to reject executive appointments, reject judicial appointments, ratify treaties, and block bills proposed either by the President or by the House.
The institutional brakes embodied in the Supreme Court and the Senate are arguably problematic in themselves. Any structure designed to "cool the passions" of a people who supposedly run the show ultimately reeks of paternalism.
They also limit innovation and stagnate collective decision-making. That's especially dangerous during any time when our nation confronts multiple crises--climate chaos, global pandemics, social fractures--to which the established order has no credible response.
But whatever one thinks of the idea of brakes built into the Constitution, or their unfortunate impacts during times of change requiring institutional nimbleness, they are made even more dangerous by the transformation of the House of Representatives into an ossified chamber of self-protecting careerists.
### Every branch in Washington now plays the same role
The House was supposed to represent the passions of We the People, but has instead become trapped by the same individual careerism--and institutional ossification--that infects the other branches. This structural co-optation of the House turned the entire constitutional design on its head long ago.
The [re-election rate in Congress is astronomically high](https://substack.com/redirect/f74f5dd6-9859-4fdd-a64f-d1f9154bd691?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That is driven not by the passions of the voters, but particularly by political inertia fueled, in turn, by many factors: name recognition, the [franking privilege](https://substack.com/redirect/d55f79e5-46ee-442d-a94a-91ba23cb1b40?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (through which Members--but not challengers--can reach voters for free through the U.S. Postal Service), the tendency of corporate incumbents to [smear](https://substack.com/redirect/5c06c4f0-a920-4f47-be5c-808d5fd30589?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) their opponents, and the media's foolish willingness to frame elections as horse races without any attention to policy, the public failures of incumbents, or the substantive alternatives offered by challengers.
The House is in no way poised to reflect the passions of We the People that the other branches were constructed to cool.
The re-election rate in Congress also represents the co-optation of democracy by capital, in at least three dimensions...

body:
I wanted to follow up.
| | | Hi team -- It's Christie, Executive Director at the DSCC. I just reviewed this community’s recent survey responses, and I wanted to follow up personally.

Grassroots Democrats like you are central to everything we do here at the DSCC. Your support shapes our strategy, fuels our ground game, and drives our campaigns.

But your feedback is every bit as crucial -- it helps us make sure we’re meeting people where they are and talking about the issues they care most about. And as one of our **_most active supporters**_ , you're exactly the kind of Democrat we need to defend President Biden's Senate majority next year. **That’s why I’m reaching out today:**

**[Will you accept my personal invitation by taking this survey to become a Grassroots Democratic Strategist and join an _exclusive_ focus group? I'm reviewing the results tonight, so click here right away to start >>](https://go.dscc.org/go/15561?ask=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&amounts=5%2C10%2C25%2C50&code=DPE-ND-20230904-Christie-ActiveDemsGroup23RFv1-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-ND-20230904-Christie-ActiveDemsGroup23RFv1-Pros-sl1&akid=11825%2E9716520%2Evbkt1g)**

Friend,
As the President has said time and again, 2024 is our LAST CHANCE to save our country from the clutches of tyranny.

Friend,
**My name is Debbie Mucarsel-Powell. I’m an immigrant, mother, and former Congresswoman. And now, I’m running to defeat Sen. Rick Scott and serve as Florida’s next U.S. Senator.**
Rick Scott has failed us in Florida, and I refuse to sit back and watch him push my state and country backward. I’m prepared to fight to protect our rights, our economy, and our very democracy — and even more, I’m prepared to win.
But this race will be one of the most competitive Senate races in 2024, and we’re up against a deep-seated Republican incumbent who spent $64 million of his personal fortune to buy this seat last time around. The only way I can compete — and win — is with the support of grassroots Democrats like you.
**I've set an ambitious $75,000 daily goal to defeat Rick Scott, and I need your help to reach it.[_Please, will you split a donation between my campaign and Kirsten Gillibrand right now to flip Florida blue and expand our Senate majority?_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69208943/425404498/-1736033032?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk4414-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OWUyODM0Ni03MzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k9JeMvGydrdS558qkL1QlUx5bV6Gi1VmO5eOaMKPnAE=)**

**When I was 14, my mom, my three sisters, and I left our home in Ecuador to find a better life in America.** We lived in a one room apartment. My mom worked double-shifts during the day and took classes at night to learn English. I picked up shifts at the local doughnut shop to help make ends meet.
Those years weren’t easy. But thanks to my mother’s relentless belief in a brighter future for her children, I went on to graduate from college, become the first South American immigrant to serve in Congress, and write the bill to expand Medicare Advantage coverage.
**My story — coming from a one room apartment to walking the halls of Congress — is only possible in America. But Rick Scott has spent his time in Washington doing everything he can to _destroy_ the country that gave my family a shot at a better life.**
Rick Scott has a net worth up to nearly a quarter of a billion dollars, yet he’s proposed tax increases on working families and the middle class. He’s pushed for a nationwide abortion ban. And he wrote the GOP plan to end the Social Security and Medicare that so many rely on.
**That’s why I’m running to defeat him next November. Experts say that Rick will be one of the most vulnerable senators on the ballot — which means that if we build a powerful grassroots team full of people like you, friend,_we can take him down._**
**So please, I’m personally asking:[_Will you become one of my very first grassroots donors by splitting a gift between my campaign and Kirsten Gillibrand today? Every dollar gets us one step closer to defeating Rick Scott and defending our Senate majority._](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69208948/425404503/-1736033032?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk4414-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OWUyODM0Ni03MzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=k9JeMvGydrdS558qkL1QlUx5bV6Gi1VmO5eOaMKPnAE=)**

clean_body: I’m prepared to fight to protect our rights, our economy, and our very democracy — and even more, I’m prepared to win. | | | = Friend, My name is Debbie Mucarsel-Powell. I’m an immigrant, mother, and former Congresswoman. And now, I’m running to defeat Sen. Rick Scott and serve as Florida’s next U.S. Senator. Rick Scott has failed us in Florida, and I refuse to sit back and watch him push my state and country backward. I’m prepared to fight to protect our rights, our economy, and our very democracy — and even more, I’m prepared to win. But this race will be one of the most competitive Senate races in 2024, and we’re up against a deep-seated Republican incumbent who spent $64 million of his personal fortune to buy this seat last time around. The only way I can compete — and win — is with the support of grassroots Democrats like you. I've set an ambitious $75,000 daily goal to defeat Rick Scott, and I need your help to reach it.= If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Debbie Mucarsel-Powell and Kirsten Gillibrand will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = When I was 14, my mom, my three sisters, and I left our home in Ecuador to find a better life in America. We lived in a one room apartment. My mom worked double-shifts during the day and took classes at night to learn English. I picked up shifts at the local doughnut shop to help make ends meet. Those years weren’t easy. But thanks to my mother’s relentless belief in a brighter future for her children, I went on to graduate from college, become the first South American immigrant to serve in Congress, and write the bill to expand Medicare Advantage coverage. My story — coming from a one room apartment to walking the halls of Congress — is only possible in America. But Rick Scott has spent his time in Washington doing everything he can to destroy the country that gave my family a shot at a better life. Rick Scott has a net worth up to nearly a quarter of a billion dollars, yet he’s proposed tax increases on working families and the middle class. He’s pushed for a nationwide abortion ban. And he wrote the GOP plan to end the Social Security and Medicare that so many rely on. That’s why I’m running to defeat him next November. Experts say that Rick will be one of the most vulnerable senators on the ballot — which means that if we build a powerful grassroots team full of people like you, friend,we can take him down. So please, I’m personally asking:= If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation between Debbie Mucarsel-Powell and Kirsten Gillibrand will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = Thank you for joining me in this fight for a better Florida and a better future for all of us. With gratitude, Debbie Mucarsel-Powell | * | = A note from Kirsten: Friend, I can’t thank you enough for standing by my side in the critical fights ahead of us. As Democrats face our toughest Senate map in decades, I need your help more than ever. While Republicans only have to defend 10 seats in 2024, Democrats have to defend 23 – and if the GOP flips just two of them, it will deliver Senate control back to Mitch McConnell. Now, experts at FiveThirtyEight are saying that the GOP has a clear path to the majority. As right-wing special interests gear up to pour millions into flipping the Senate, we need to fight back right now. But I refuse to take a single cent from corporate PACs, which means I’m relying on your grassroots support. = And if you’d like to become a core member of my team and receive exclusive texts from my campaign, = Thank you, Kirsten Gillibrand

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: These are the plans I'm fighting for. Please let me know if you support this agenda.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
If you agree with these plans, please let me know. Every signature helps show the naysayers that these plans have strong support.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=42%2E4032634%2E5o3n2t)
---
I’m running for re-election because although we’ve made tremendous progress as a movement, there’s still so much more to do. [**Here are some of the plans that I’m fighting for below. If you agree with these plans, please click here to let me know that you endorse them. Every signature helps show the naysayers that these plans have strong support.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/wfs-endorse-elizabeths-agenda/?source=EMZ16I_ENG_E_20230904_V1S1_AK_F14&t=8&akid=42%2E4032634%2E5o3n2t)
* Pass a wealth tax to make the wealthy finally pay their fair share.
* End the filibuster to ensure abortion rights and enact gun safety laws across the country.
* Significantly reduce the cost of child care for every American family, making it as low as $10 a day.
* Rein in big banks and pass stronger regulations to prevent another economic crisis.
* Make the Supreme Court follow a basic code of ethics.
* Expand Social Security benefits by $2,400 a year and fully fund the program all the way to 2096.
* Create fast and free federal tax filing, so Americans don’t have to go through profiteering middlemen like TurboTax.
* Crack down on corporate profiteering and price gouging so that working families don’t pay the price for their greed.
* Pass the PRO Act to make it easier for workers to form a union.
* Ban members of Congress and their spouses from owning and trading individual stocks.

Those who profit from the status quo often say that these plans are impossible, and that we can never build a movement strong enough to make them happen. But we've proved them wrong before, and we’re going to keep proving them wrong. [**These plans are possible — we just need to build our momentum, grow our grassroots movement, raise our voices, and fight side by side. So will you please add your name to let me know if you agree with the policies that I'm fighting for?**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/wfs-endorse-elizabeths-agenda/?source=EMZ16I_ENG_E_20230904_V1S1_AK_F14&t=9&akid=42%2E4032634%2E5o3n2t) | [**I ENDORSE**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/wfs-endorse-elizabeths-agenda/?source=EMZ16I_ENG_E_20230904_V1S1_AK_F14&t=10&akid=42%2E4032634%2E5o3n2t)
---
Thanks for being a part of this,
Elizabeth
P.S. Do you have any feedback on these plans or for our campaign in general? [**Please share with me here. I appreciate hearing your thoughts.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/wfs-share-your-feedback/?source=EMZ16I_ENG_E_20230904_V1S1_AK_F14&t=11&akid=42%2E4032634%2E5o3n2t)

---

---

---

| | Friend, Happy Labor Day! We hope everyone is having a great long weekend spent with friends and family. Team Kelly's momentum on the campaign trail continues to build! After a busy weekend in the North Country with over a dozen stops, Kelly was in Manchester joining Alderman Joe Kelly Levasseur and Rep. Will Infantine for a business walking tour of the downtown stopping to hear about issues affecting the city. Kelly even made a special stop in to Willie B's barbershop on the West Side to say hello to Willie. Keep up with our campaign updates on our [Facebook](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/74Of8JMRw/c?w=kC_Dr_mGtWT5seIjfxxMO8Dzj5SYbrPXTu6GoA9OISo.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZmFjZWJvb2suY29tL0tlbGx5QXlvdHRlRm9yTkgiLCJyIjoiZmM3MGMxYzktMWNjNy00ODNhLTlmMDYtYmI2NDg3ZmYxMjNiIiwibSI6Im1haWwiLCJjIjoiMmIwOGU3MDMtYTYyNy00NmYzLWJlOGEtNWQ1NjU0NDI2MjAyIn0) page. Best,Team Kelly
---
| | |
---
| | News Highlights
---
| |
---
| | Ayotte told NHJournal Craig’s support for the rail plan raises questions about her fiscal responsibility. “Study after study shows that this $800 million dollar rail system is fiscally unsustainable,” Ayotte said. “New Hampshire taxpayers will inevitably foot the bill for this project that Mayor Craig deems necessary because young people don’t want cars?!” [Read the full article from the NHJournal here.](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/74Of8JMRw/c?w=Gbcby37FXzX0E1UdIdREzR3kpKF9bmXQd0fiBSw-Cu4.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tYW5jaGVzdGVyaW5rbGluay5jb20vYXlvdHRlLWVuZG9yc2VkLWJ5LW1lcnJpbWFjay1yZXB1YmxpY2FuLWRlbGVnYXRpb24vIiwiciI6ImYzN2IyNWU3LTUzZTMtNDBjMC04OGIzLTIyZDE5YzMzZThhZCIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjJiMDhlNzAzLWE2MjctNDZmMy1iZThhLTVkNTY1NDQyNjIwMiJ9)
---
| |
---
| | Ayotte raised more than $500,000 at her first fundraiser in Derry last month. The campaign believes its the largest haul for any New Hampshire candidate for governor from a single event. More than 100 attended... major attendees included House Speaker Sherman Packard, R-Londonderry; Majority Leader Jason Osborne, R-Auburn; and Trump Super PAC executive and 2022 congressional nominee Karoline Leavitt of Hampton. [Read the full article from the Union Leader here.](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/74Of8JMRw/c?w=ukF3fE2ZTftWepGOLGsz6UZWGTxVpy9m44JlkjzAaBU.)
---
| | |
---
| | Letter to the Editor Highlight
---
| |
---
| | "As our attorney general, Kelly Ayotte worked to get police officers the resources they needed to be successful. New Hampshire has some of the best police practices and procedures in the nation, largely due to the work of Kelly Ayotte... As governor, you can count on Kelly Ayotte to ensure that New Hampshire is the safest state in the nation." [Read Rep. Dennis Mannion's full letter in the Union Leader here.](https://shoutout.wix.com/so/74Of8JMRw/c?w=KzX3ls6xIjvz9cg3_OROpgzowVTk1eZKlxPlDG1tOUQ.)
---
| | |
---
| |
### Photos from the Trail
---
| |
---
| | Saw this great homemade shirt at Moose Fest last week. Thank you for the support, Richard!
---
| |
---
| | It was great stopping by the Village Gun Store in Whitefield last week to catch up with owner and US Army Veteran Josh Dagnese.
---
| |
---
| | Thank you Rep. Will Infantine and Alderman Joe Kelley Levasseur for joining me in Manchester to meet with business owners and hear about the issues affecting the Queen City.
---
| |
---
| | Great stopping in to see my friend Willie at his barbershop in Manchester. Thank you for your friendship and support, Willie!
---

name: CaucusRoom
subject: Joe Doesn't Have Time for East Palestine
body:
What you need to know today. No images? [Click here](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-e-ajltity-trhiujldw-f/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-r/)
## **Happy Labor Day!**
**Wartime Elections** Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said 2024 elections in Ukraine amid the country's war with Russia are possible **[as long as the United States and Europe help pay the cost.](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-y/)** **Never Detained** An **[illegal immigrant has been arrested six times in two months](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-j/)** since he arrived in New York City on multiple theft and assault charges. **All Car Are Bad** Pete **[Buttigieg is appointing a group of"experts" to advise him on "transportation equity,"](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-t/)** including several who argue that cars cause climate change and promote racism and therefore should be phased out. **Joe Doesn't Have Time** **[Biden cited his hectic schedule](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-i/) **when he was asked why he had not yet visited East Palestine, Ohio, months after the town was devastated by a train derailment.

**Still Missing** Hawaii officials said**t[here are still 385 people missing after the Maui wildfire](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-d/) **in an update Friday, nearly unchanged from the week prior. **Let****'s [have a good laugh](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-h/)!**
* Both are **[skills](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-k/).**
* Not how **[this works](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-u/).**
* **[Do it](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-p/) **yourself.
* This **[seems correct.](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-x/)**

**Countdown to the 2nd Republican Primary Debate!** [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-m/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-c/)[](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-q/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-ajltity-trhiujldw-a/) | CaucusRoom, LLC

name: Virginia Update (via DLCC.org)
subject: re: GOP’s plan to take over Virginia
body:
If we gather enough support between now and Election Day, we stand a chance at flipping the Virginia House and maintaining our Senate majority – but this won’t be an easy fight, and we’re running out of time. | | |
---
| | **Our Republican counterparts released a memo detailing their plan for a far-right takeover in Virginia.** The GOP is dead set on helping Virginia Republicans clutch onto their _razor-thin majority_ in the House of Delegates and chip away at _our_ Senate majority.
* They’re partnering with far-right Gov. Glenn Youngkin to push an ultra-conservative agenda on the Commonwealth – **banning abortion, restricting voting laws, and pushing for anti-LGBTQ+ policies.**
* They’re intensifying their spending – Republicans poured **$750,000 into Gov. Youngkin’s PAC** , Youngkin ended last quarter with over **$6 million cash on hand** , _and_ Virginia Republicans have spent **six figures on attack ads** in 12 key swing districts.
* **_And_** , they’re spewing lies about Democrats’ stance on crime, education, and the economy. We need to set the record straight.

**We can’t sit idly by while Republicans forge a path to power. The GOP is dead set on helping Virginia Republicans clutch onto their razor-thin majority in the House of Delegates and chip away at our Senate majority. They’re partnering with far-right Gov. Glenn Youngkin to push an ultra-conservative agenda on the Commonwealth – banning abortion, restricting voting laws, and pushing for anti-LGBTQ+ policies. They’re intensifying their spending – Republicans poured $750,000 into Gov. Youngkin’s PAC , Youngkin ended last quarter with over $6 million cash on hand , and Virginia Republicans have spent six figures on attack ads in 12 key swing districts. And , they’re spewing lies about Democrats’ stance on crime, education, and the economy. We need to set the record straight. We can’t sit idly by while Republicans forge a path to power. **GET TO KNOW VIVEK:** Vivek is an American entrepreneur, political and cultural thought leader, and New York Times bestselling author of **_Woke, Inc.: Inside Corporate America's Social Justice Scam_** , along with his second book, **_Nation of Victims: Identity Politics, the Death of Merit, and the Path Back to Excellence_**.

He has been a prominent and outspoken critic against ESG, discriminatory practices under the guise of critical race theory, and of what he calls "government-tech censorship," where the government colludes with big tech to do through the backdoor what it couldn't do through the front door under the Constitution.

Vivek is a first-generation American. Growing up in the Cincinnati area, he often recounts the sage advice he received from his father that "If you're going to stand out, then you might as well be outstanding." This advice set the course for his life: an accomplished pianist, a nationally ranked tennis player, and the valedictorian of his Jesuit high school. He went on to graduate summa cum laude in Biology from Harvard in 2007 and received his J.D. degree from Yale Law School in 2013, worked at a hedge fund, then started a pharmaceutical company, Roivant Sciences, where his team developed five drugs that went on to become FDA-approved.

In 2022, he founded Strive Asset Management, an Ohio-based firm, that takes direct aim at asset managers like BlackRock, State Street, Vanguard and others for their fiduciary breach of duty to maximize the wealth of their clients for Wokeism. Strive's mission is to restore the voices of everyday citizens in the American economy by leading companies to focus on excellence over politics.

Friend – This Labor Day, all of us here on Team Salinas are reaffirming our commitment to defending organized labor and ensuring American workers are treated with the dignity and respect they deserve.

**Andrea comes from a proud union family.** It was her father’s good-paying union job that secured her family a ticket to the middle class. She understands – _firsthand_ – that unions sit at the backbone of our country and hold the key to the middle class. So you can trust that Andrea will always stand with workers.

But the reality is, with the American unionization rate sitting at a record low, the fight to defend unions and protect this path to the middle class will take all of us. That’s why we have to ask:
**Will you stand with Andrea in the fight to expand workers’ rights and protect labor unions?** _[YES](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/69209534/425406883/-1307986631?refcode=em_eng_2023.09.04_b1_as_all_YES-nd&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MTM4ZGMxMS03YzRiLWVlMTEtYTNmMS0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EZORFg6o1MGN9ECXZcWBL1nCOCDx6J7BTMyDxZisU34=)_

clean_body: Friend – This Labor Day, all of us here on Team Salinas are reaffirming our commitment to defending organized labor and ensuring American workers are treated with the dignity and respect they deserve. = | Happy Labor Day! This Labor Day, we need to know: Will you stand with Andrea in the fight to expand workers’ rights and protect labor unions? | = = All responses will be sent immediately to Salinas HQ. Friend – This Labor Day, all of us here on Team Salinas are reaffirming our commitment to defending organized labor and ensuring American workers are treated with the dignity and respect they deserve. Andrea comes from a proud union family. It was her father’s good-paying union job that secured her family a ticket to the middle class. She understands – firsthand – that unions sit at the backbone of our country and hold the key to the middle class. So you can trust that Andrea will always stand with workers. But the reality is, with the American unionization rate sitting at a record low, the fight to defend unions and protect this path to the middle class will take all of us. That’s why we have to ask: Will you stand with Andrea in the fight to expand workers’ rights and protect labor unions? = = All responses will be sent immediately to Salinas HQ. Thanks for your response, Team Salinas | | = Andrea Salinas for Oregon PO Box 230985 Tigard, OR 97281 Paid for by Andrea Salinas for Oregon Andrea is fighting for Oregon, fighting for affordable quality health care, an economy that works for everyone, and clean air, land, and water. Now, Andrea's ready to show Washington what hard work can accomplish – real, meaningful change for all of us. | =_
